milestones.description: Mrs Justice Thornton dismissed the challenge.

milestones.title: Galliford Try steps aside
milestones.description: National Highways retendered the £100m construction contract after Galliford Try withdrew.

milestones.title: Court of Appeal dismisses appeal
milestones.description: Cumulative carbon assessment ruled 'scientifically pointless' given emissions have no geographical boundary.

milestones.title: Supreme Court refuses permission
milestones.description: Domestic legal avenues exhausted; DCO confirmed.

milestones.title: Ministers reapprove £200m funding
milestones.description: Final funding go-ahead, citing 44,000 homes and 33,000 jobs in Greater Norwich City Deal.

milestones.title: Skanska awarded £153m contract
milestones.description: Enabling and advance works began on site.

milestones.title: Main construction begins
milestones.description: Main construction of the two underpasses scheduled to start in Q3 of FY 2025-26.

milestones.title: Scheme open to traffic (expected)
milestones.description: Programmed to open to traffic in early FY 2028-29.

TR010006.name: M20 Junction 10A
TR010006.summary: New motorway junction east of M20 J10 at Ashford, Kent, providing relief to the A2070 and supporting major south Ashford housing growth. Originally consulted on in 2008 and postponed in 2012, revived under the DCO regime with consent granted December 2017. Construction by Highways England began in 2018 and the junction opened to traffic on 31 October 2019.

milestones.title: Initial public consultation
milestones.description: Highways Agency consulted on a new M20 J10a and link to the A2070.

milestones.title: Government approval in principle
milestones.description: Initial government approval as part of the Highways Agency's programme for Ashford.

milestones.title: Scheme postponed
milestones.description: Postponed amid the post-recession spending review of the strategic road programme.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation under PA2008
milestones.description: Highways England launched statutory consultation as the project entered the NSIP regime.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted and accepted
milestones.description: Submitted to PINS as TR010006 and accepted for examination summer 2016.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: SoS made the M20 J10a DCO 2017 (SI 2017/1202).

milestones.title: Notice to proceed; construction start
milestones.description: Notice to proceed issued; Balfour Beatty appointed as principal contractor.

milestones.title: New junction opened to traffic
milestones.description: £104m junction opened ahead of the original 2020 schedule.

TR010007.name: A160 - A180 Port of Immingham Improvement
TR010007.summary: Highways Agency NSIP to upgrade the A160 single carriageway between the A180 Brocklesby Interchange and the Port of Immingham (the UK's largest port by tonnage) to dual carriageway. Consulted from 2009, granted development consent in February 2015, and opened to traffic in March 2017 at a cost of around £88.4 million.

milestones.title: Public consultation on route options
milestones.description: Highways Agency held public consultation in summer 2009 on options for upgrading the A160 corridor.

milestones.title: Preferred route announced
milestones.description: Highways Agency announced its preferred route for dualling the A160.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Highways Agency submitted the DCO; accepted for examination on 27 January 2014.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: SoS made the A160/A180 (Port of Immingham Improvement) DCO 2015.

milestones.title: Construction starts
milestones.description: Ground-breaking ceremony marked the start of works; ~£88.4m budget.

milestones.title: Scheme opened to traffic
milestones.description: Upgraded dual carriageway between Brocklesby Interchange and the Port of Immingham opened.

TR010011.name: Woodside Link Houghton Regis Bedfordshire
TR010011.summary: A 2.9 km single-carriageway link built by Central Bedfordshire Council connecting the Woodside Industrial Estate in Houghton Regis to junction 11a of the M1. DCO granted on 30 September 2014, construction began in 2015, and the road opened to traffic on 13 April 2017 at a cost of around £40 million.

milestones.title: Preferred corridor consultation
milestones.description: Central Bedfordshire Council consulted on the preferred corridor for the new road, then known as the Woodside Connection.

milestones.title: Scoping opinion and public exhibitions
milestones.description: PINS issued its scoping opinion on the Proposed Woodside Connection.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Central Bedfordshire Council submitted the DCO; accepted on 11 June 2013.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: SoS for Transport granted the Central Bedfordshire Council Woodside Link DCO 2014.

milestones.title: Construction starts
milestones.description: Main works began alongside the parallel A5-M1 Link scheme.

milestones.title: Road opens to traffic
milestones.description: Opened ahead of schedule and on budget at around £40m, with over £30m from SEMLEP and developer contributions.

TR010009.name: M1 Junction 10a Grade Separation - Luton
TR010009.summary: Luton Borough Council DCO to replace the at-grade roundabout at M1 Junction 10a with a grade-separated junction. The DCO was made in October 2013, construction ran from December 2013 to summer 2015, and the £30m scheme was officially opened by the Minister for Transport on 16 July 2015.

milestones.title: Application accepted for examination
milestones.description: Luton Borough Council's application accepted for examination by PINS.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order made
milestones.description: M1 Junction 10a (Grade Separation) Order 2013 (SI 2013/2808) made.

milestones.title: Construction begins
milestones.description: Main construction commenced under VolkerFitzpatrick.

milestones.title: Junction officially opened
milestones.description: £30m scheme officially opened by Transport Minister Andrew Jones MP.

TR010010.name: Morpeth Northern Bypass
TR010010.summary: A 3.8km single-carriageway link from the A1 north of Morpeth to the A197 Whorral Bank roundabout, promoted by Northumberland County Council as the first phase of the South East Northumberland Strategic Link Road. A bypass for Morpeth had been mooted since the 1960s; the modern Northern Bypass scheme secured DfT major-scheme funding in 2011-12, received its DCO in January 2015, and opened to traffic in spring 2017.

milestones.title: DfT major-scheme funding confirmed
milestones.description: DfT contribution of around £21m towards the bypass as part of devolved LA major-schemes programme.

milestones.title: Carillion appointed under ECI contract
milestones.description: Carillion awarded the Early Contractor Involvement contract; AECOM as designer.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted and accepted
milestones.description: NCC submitted the DCO under reference TR010010; accepted for examination 2014.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: SoS made the Morpeth Northern Bypass DCO 2015 on 12 January 2015.

milestones.title: Construction commenced
milestones.description: Main construction began in spring 2015, led by Carillion; 310,000 m3 of earthworks, three new bridges, new grade-separated A1 junction.

milestones.title: Open to traffic
milestones.description: New road opened in spring 2017, providing the first phase of the South East Northumberland Strategic Link Road.

TR010014.name: A30 Temple to Higher Carblake Improvement
TR010014.summary: Dualling of the final 4.5 km single-carriageway section of the A30 across Bodmin Moor between Temple and Higher Carblake, completing continuous dual carriageway from the M5 at Exeter to Carland Cross. Promoted by Cornwall Council with Highways England, consented in February 2015 and opened to traffic in July 2017.

milestones.title: Long-standing dualling commitment confirmed
milestones.description: Improving the Temple to Higher Carblake bottleneck had been a Cornwall Council aspiration for around 25 years; in December 2012 ministers announced upgrade with Cornwall Council taking the lead as applicant.

milestones.title: Pre-application consultation
milestones.description: Cornwall Council and Highways Agency held statutory pre-application consultation across Bodmin Moor.

milestones.title: DCO application accepted
milestones.description: Cornwall Council submitted the DCO for the 5.15 km highway improvement; accepted for examination.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: SoS made the Cornwall Council A30 Temple to Higher Carblake DCO 2015 (SI 2015/147).

milestones.title: Construction starts (Kier)
milestones.description: Kier Infrastructure began main works under a c.£40m contract within the £60m total scheme cost.

milestones.title: Dual carriageway opened to traffic
milestones.description: All four lanes opened, completing continuous A30 dual carriageway between the M5 at Exeter and Carland Cross.

TR010017.name: A19/A1058 Coast Road Junction Improvement
TR010017.summary: A Highways England scheme to upgrade the Silverlink junction in North Tyneside, where the A19 meets the A1058 Coast Road, by lowering the A19 into an underpass beneath the existing roundabout to create a free-flow north-south route. The DCO was granted in January 2016, construction by a Sisk/Lagan JV began in June 2016, and the c.£75m three-level interchange opened to traffic in April 2019.

milestones.title: Public consultation
milestones.description: Highways Agency ran statutory consultation on the preferred grade-separated underpass design.

milestones.title: DCO accepted by PINS
milestones.description: PINS accepted the DCO for examination on 3 December 2014.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: SoS made the A19/A1058 Coast Road DCO; came into force 18 February 2016.

milestones.title: Construction starts on site
milestones.description: Sisk/Lagan JV began main works in June 2016 to lower the A19 into an open-cut underpass.

milestones.title: Junction opened to traffic
milestones.description: The North East's first three-level stack interchange opened, serving more than 80,000 vehicles per day.

TR010020.name: A19 / A184 Testos Junction Improvement
TR010020.summary: Highways England scheme to replace the at-grade Testo's roundabout south of the Tyne Tunnel with a grade-separated junction, raising the A19 on a flyover above a widened A184 roundabout. Granted development consent in September 2018, construction began in March 2019 and the new flyover opened to traffic in June 2021.

milestones.title: Statutory pre-application consultation
milestones.description: Highways England ran consultation on the preferred grade-separated option.

milestones.title: DCO accepted for examination
milestones.description: PINS accepted the DCO TR010020 in August 2017.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: SoS granted the DCO authorising the A19 flyover.

milestones.title: Main construction starts
milestones.description: Costain began main works; Cleveland Bridge fabricated the 142m weathering steel flyover.

milestones.title: Flyover opens to traffic
milestones.description: New A19 flyover over Testo's roundabout opened to traffic.

milestones.title: Scheme officially completed
milestones.description: £130m junction upgrade fully complete; ~60% of construction hours delivered by local workforce.

TR010024.name: A19 Downhill Lane Junction Improvement
TR010024.summary: £51.6 million Highways England scheme replacing the A19/A1290 Downhill Lane junction on the Sunderland/South Tyneside border with a full roundabout, new road bridge and non-motorised user bridge to provide capacity for the International Advanced Manufacturing Park (IAMP) and Nissan plant. DCO granted July 2020; opened to traffic 11 March 2022.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation
milestones.description: Highways England held statutory consultation Sep-Oct 2017.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Highways England submitted the DCO; accepted for examination 22 February 2019.

milestones.title: Development consent granted
milestones.description: SoS for Transport granted the DCO.

milestones.title: Construction starts
milestones.description: Main works began six months ahead of schedule; Costain as principal contractor.

milestones.title: Junction opens to traffic
milestones.description: New A19/A1290 Downhill Lane junction opened to support IAMP and Nissan plant access.

TR010035.name: A585 Windy Harbour to Skippool Improvement Scheme
TR010035.summary: A 3-mile dual carriageway bypass on the A585 between the M55 and Fleetwood, delivered by Highways England/National Highways. Development consent granted on 9 April 2020 after a 2018-2019 DCO examination; Kier Highways was contracted to build it for around £150m, and the bypass opened to traffic in December 2023 with official completion in August 2024.

milestones.title: Preferred route announced
milestones.description: Highways England selected the preferred route on 24 October 2017.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted and accepted
milestones.description: Highways England submitted the DCO on 29 October 2018; PINS accepted on 26 November 2018.

milestones.title: Public examination
milestones.description: Preliminary meeting and open floor hearing in April 2019, followed by issue-specific hearings.

milestones.title: Development consent granted
milestones.description: Grant Shapps granted the DCO authorising the 4.85 km dual two-lane bypass.

milestones.title: Kier Highways contracted
milestones.description: ~£150m main works contract awarded; site set-up began spring 2020.

milestones.title: Bypass opens to traffic
milestones.description: Phased opening from 18 December 2023; opening had been delayed due to challenging ground conditions.

milestones.title: Project completion
milestones.description: National Highways marked official project completion after final landscaping and cycleway works.

TR010038.name: A47 North Tuddenham to Easton
TR010038.summary: DCO for 9km dualling of the A47 between North Tuddenham and Easton in Norfolk. Consent granted August 2022; survived Boswell carbon-cumulative-impact judicial review at High Court (July 2023) and Court of Appeal (February 2024). Main construction by Galliford Try began October 2024; full scheme spring 2027.

milestones.title: Initial public consultation
milestones.description: Non-statutory consultation on options ran 13 March-21 April 2017; 1,447 responses received.

milestones.title: Preferred route announced (Option 2)
milestones.description: Dualling along the existing A47 alignment with amendments.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation
milestones.description: Statutory pre-application consultation Feb-Apr 2020.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Submitted to PINS; accepted for examination 12 April 2021.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: SoS granted the DCO for the £100-£250m dualling scheme.

milestones.title: High Court dismisses Boswell JR
milestones.description: Mrs Justice Thornton dismissed Boswell's challenge across the three Norfolk A47 DCOs.

milestones.title: Court of Appeal upholds DCO
milestones.description: Appeal dismissed; clears the scheme to proceed.

milestones.title: Main construction begins
milestones.description: Galliford Try began full works on the £250m, 9km dualling scheme.

milestones.title: Traffic switched to new section
milestones.description: National Highways switched A47 traffic onto a newly built section, marking past 50% complete.

milestones.title: Full scheme expected to open
milestones.description: Full dual carriageway scheduled to open spring 2027.

TR010039.name: A47 Wansford to Sutton
TR010039.summary: National Highways scheme to dual approximately 2.5km of the A47 between the A1 at Wansford and the existing dual carriageway at Sutton. Development consent granted on 17 February 2023, but following the 2025 Spending Review the government withdrew funding and the SoS for Transport is now (February 2026) consulting on revoking the DCO.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation
milestones.description: National Highways ran statutory consultation Sep-Nov 2018, receiving over 680 responses.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Submitted to PINS; accepted on 2 August 2021.

milestones.title: Examination opened
milestones.description: Six-month examination began under the Examining Authority.

milestones.title: Development consent granted
milestones.description: SoS for Transport granted the A47 Wansford to Sutton DCO 2023.

milestones.title: Funding withdrawn at Spending Review
milestones.description: Following the 2025 Spending Review, government decided not to continue funding, effectively cancelling construction.

milestones.title: Proposed revocation of DCO
milestones.description: SoS published notice of intention to revoke the 2023 DCO; representations open until 25 March 2026.

milestones.title: Revocation order expected
milestones.description: Following close of consultation 25 March 2026, SoS expected to make a formal revocation order.

TR010040.name: A47 Blofield to North Burlingham
TR010040.summary: National Highways scheme to dual a 2.6km gap on the A47 east of Norwich between Blofield and North Burlingham. The DCO was granted in June 2022 and survived a Boswell JR (dismissed by HC 2023, CoA 2024, SC refused permission). Main construction began 2024-25; traffic switched to new dual carriageway spring 2026; full completion expected by summer 2026.

milestones.title: Initial public consultation
milestones.description: Highways England ran consultation 13 March-21 April 2017; 441 responses, ~63% supporting need for improvement.

milestones.title: Preferred route announced
milestones.description: New 2.4-2.6km two-lane dual carriageway approximately 70m south of the existing A47.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation
milestones.description: Statutory consultation Sep-Oct 2018; 767 responses.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: National Highways submitted the DCO; accepted for examination 27 January 2021.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: SoS for Transport granted DCO; SI 2022/738 made the same day.

milestones.title: Boswell JR launched
milestones.description: Andrew Boswell filed JR against three A47 DCOs arguing combined climate impact not assessed.

milestones.title: High Court dismisses challenge
milestones.description: Mrs Justice Thornton dismissed Boswell's claim in July 2023.

milestones.title: Court of Appeal dismisses appeal
milestones.description: Court ruled the DCOs had been appropriately assessed for carbon emissions; SC refused permission.

milestones.title: Main construction underway
milestones.description: Galliford Try progressed main construction including bridge works at the B1140.

milestones.title: Traffic switched to new dual carriageway
milestones.description: Spring 2026 traffic switch onto the new 2.6km dual carriageway; full scheme completion expected by summer 2026.

TR010066.name: A46 Coventry Junctions (Walsgrave)
TR010066.summary: National Highways scheme to replace the existing A46 Walsgrave roundabout (last at-grade junction on the A46 east of Coventry between the M6 and M40) with a new grade-separated dumbbell junction approximately 800m north of the existing roundabout. Estimated cost ~£112m. DCO granted on 4 February 2026; construction expected to start in autumn 2026, opening 2028.

milestones.title: Non-statutory consultation on Option 11
milestones.description: First public consultation Jan-Feb 2022 on a fully grade-separated dumbbell junction; supported as the preferred design.

milestones.title: Statutory (Section 42/47) consultation
milestones.description: Statutory consultation 25 October-6 December 2023; 66% agreed need, 51% agreed with proposed route.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: National Highways submitted the DCO on 14 November 2024.

milestones.title: Application accepted for examination
milestones.description: PINS accepted the application for examination on 12 December 2024.

milestones.title: Examination closes
milestones.description: Six-month examination phase concluded.

milestones.title: Development consent granted
milestones.description: Lord Hendy on behalf of the SoS for Transport granted the DCO.

milestones.title: Construction expected to start
milestones.description: Main construction works on the new grade-separated Walsgrave junction expected to begin autumn 2026.

milestones.title: Scheme expected to open
milestones.description: £112m upgraded junction expected to open in 2028, removing the last at-grade roundabout on the A46 east of Coventry.

TR020001.name: London Luton Airport Expansion
TR020001.summary: Luton Rising's (LLAL, owned by Luton Borough Council) DCO to raise capacity from 18 to 32 mppa was granted by the Transport Secretary on 3 April 2025, overruling the Examining Authority's May 2024 recommendation to refuse on environmental grounds. LADACAN's judicial review was dismissed by the High Court in December 2025; a Court of Appeal rolled-up hearing is listed for May 2026.

milestones.title: First statutory pre-application consultation
milestones.description: LLAL ran its first statutory DCO consultation in December 2019 on raising the cap from 18 to 32 mppa.

milestones.title: Second statutory consultation
milestones.description: Second statutory consultation in late 2022 on making best use of the existing runway.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Luton Rising submitted the DCO; accepted for examination 27 March 2023.

milestones.title: Examination opens
milestones.description: Examination of the application began 10 August 2023.

milestones.title: Examining Authority recommends refusal
milestones.description: ExA report cited noise, traffic, Chilterns National Landscape, GHG, landscape and heritage harms outweighing economic benefits.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: Heidi Alexander granted the DCO, raising the permitted cap from 18 to 32 mppa with a second terminal (estimated cost over £2.5bn 2023 prices), overriding the ExA's recommendation.

milestones.title: LADACAN files judicial review
milestones.description: LADACAN, represented by Leigh Day, lodged a crowdfunded statutory challenge.

milestones.title: High Court dismisses challenge
milestones.description: High Court rejected all five grounds of LADACAN's claim, upholding the SoS's decision.

milestones.title: Court of Appeal rolled-up hearing
milestones.description: Two-day rolled-up hearing scheduled at the Court of Appeal to consider permission to appeal and, if granted, the substantive appeal.

TR020002.name: Manston Airport
TR020002.summary: Manston Airport closed in May 2014 after Ann Gloag's £1 takeover, then became one of the most contentious NSIPs in UK planning history. RiverOak Strategic Partners' DCO to reopen the site as a cargo airport was granted in 2020, quashed by judicial review in 2021, granted again in 2022, survived a second JR through 2024, and is now in pre-construction with airspace consultation in 2026 and operations targeted for 2028-29.

milestones.title: Manston Airport closes
milestones.description: Ann Gloag, who bought the airport for £1 in November 2013, closed Manston with the loss of 144 direct jobs. KLM withdrew its twice-daily Schiphol service.

milestones.title: RiverOak submits DCO application
milestones.description: RiverOak Strategic Partners submitted a DCO to reopen Manston as a dedicated air cargo hub handling at least 10,000 freight movements per year.

milestones.title: Examination opens
milestones.description: PINS examination ran January-July 2019; eight issue-specific hearings, two compulsory acquisition hearings and four open floor hearings in Margate and Sandwich.

milestones.title: ExA recommends refusal
milestones.description: Examining Authority recommended refusing consent, finding no need case for reopening Manston as a cargo airport.

milestones.title: First DCO granted
milestones.description: Grant Shapps granted development consent against the ExA's recommendation, accepting RiverOak's case.

milestones.title: First DCO quashed by High Court
milestones.description: Jenny Dawes (resident, crowdfunded) won; SoS conceded he had failed to give adequate reasons for departing from the ExA's findings on need.

milestones.title: Redetermination begins
milestones.description: SoS issued a Statement of Matters; Ove Arup commissioned as independent aviation assessor.

milestones.title: Second DCO granted on redetermination
milestones.description: DfT granted DCO for a second time. Independent Assessor concluded no quantitative need case but SoS again sided with RiverOak.

milestones.title: Second judicial review filed
milestones.description: Jenny Dawes lodged a fresh JR; permission partially granted by Mrs Justice Lieven in March 2023.

milestones.title: High Court dismisses second JR
milestones.description: After hearing in July 2023, HC rejected the bid to quash the second DCO.

milestones.title: Court of Appeal dismisses challenge
milestones.description: Court of Appeal dismissed Dawes' substantive appeal, exhausting domestic legal challenges.

milestones.title: Amendment Order extends deadlines
milestones.description: S.I. 2025/597 extended statutory time limits for compulsory acquisition and start of construction, reflecting litigation delays.

milestones.title: Airspace change consultation opens
milestones.description: RiverOak's CAP1616 airspace design public consultation runs March-June 2026.

milestones.title: Construction expected to start
milestones.description: Main construction — runway resurfacing, cargo terminal, control tower, hangars — planned for early 2027 subject to airspace approval.

milestones.title: First cargo flights targeted
milestones.description: Initial freight operations targeted late 2028; full capacity (~10-12k cargo movements/year) by 2029.

TR020005.name: Gatwick Airport Northern Runway
TR020005.summary: A £2.2bn DCO project to bring Gatwick's existing standby runway into routine dual operation with the main runway, expanding capacity to 80 mppa. Consent granted on 21 September 2025 after a six-month examination and a 'minded to approve' phase, currently subject to a CAGNE judicial review heard at the High Court in January 2026; operational date targeted 2030.

milestones.title: 1979 agreement with West Sussex County Council
milestones.description: BAA entered a legally binding agreement not to seek planning permission for a second runway at Gatwick for 40 years, until 1 January 2019.

milestones.title: Davies Commission shortlists Gatwick
milestones.description: Airports Commission interim report shortlisted three options: a new runway at Gatwick alongside two Heathrow options.

milestones.title: Davies Commission selects Heathrow
milestones.description: Final report concluded Heathrow's NW Runway was strongest, leaving Gatwick without government backing for full runway expansion.

milestones.title: 1979 agreement expires
milestones.description: 40-year legal commitment preventing Gatwick from pursuing a second runway expired.

milestones.title: First statutory consultation
milestones.description: GAL ran consultation Sep-Dec 2021 on bringing the existing standby runway into routine use.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: GAL submitted the DCO; accepted for examination August 2023.

milestones.title: Examination opens
milestones.description: Public examination by the Examining Authority began; ran statutory six months.

milestones.title: Secretary of State 'minded to approve'
milestones.description: Heidi Alexander issued a Written Ministerial Statement indicating she was minded to approve subject to strengthened conditions on noise and public transport.

milestones.title: Gatwick responds to minded-to-approve letter
milestones.description: London Gatwick formally responded accepting further conditions on noise mitigation and public transport mode share.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: SoS for Transport granted the DCO with modifications, authorising routine dual-runway operations and capacity to 80 mppa.

milestones.title: High Court judicial review hearing
milestones.description: CAGNE-led JR challenging the consent on climate-impact and Jet Zero Strategy grounds heard 20-23 January 2026.

milestones.title: Northern runway targeted operational
milestones.description: Subject to JR outcome, GAL anticipates routine dual operation by 2030, with associated terminal/taxiway works continuing until 2038.

TR030001.name: Able Marine Energy Park
TR030001.summary: A proposed 360-hectare offshore-wind manufacturing port on the south bank of the Humber at North Killingholme, granted one of the earliest DCOs in 2013. More than a decade after consent, the quay remains unbuilt: anchor tenants DONG (2016) and SeAH (2022) walked away, and the Secretary of State has now extended the completion period to October 2032. A Material Change 3 application is anticipated.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted to IPC
milestones.description: Able Humber Ports submitted its Planning Act 2008 application proposing a solid quay at Killingholme with compensatory habitat at Cherry Cobb Sands.

milestones.title: ExA report delivered
milestones.description: Examining Authority report and recommendation submitted to SoS for Transport.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: SoS for Transport granted development consent; the DCO subsequently laid before Parliament 10 February 2014.

milestones.title: ABP judicial review withdrawn
milestones.description: Associated British Ports pursued a JR of the DCO and compulsory acquisition powers but withdrew its challenge in 2014.

milestones.title: DONG Energy abandons AMEP base
milestones.description: After a 2015 MOU, DONG Energy withdrew from the site in 2016, leaving the consented quay without an anchor tenant.

milestones.title: Material Change 1 / Amendment Order 2021
milestones.description: Amendment Order made, granting changes from a 2018 non-material change application.

milestones.title: Material Change 2 authorised
milestones.description: SoS authorised MC2 (TR030006) revising the quay layout.

milestones.title: SeAH Wind selects Teesside over AMEP
milestones.description: South Korean manufacturer SeAH Wind, originally signed for a 750-job monopile factory at AMEP, relocated to Teesworks — removing the project's prospective anchor tenant.

milestones.title: Time extension application submitted
milestones.description: Able UK applied for an extension to the DCO completion period.

milestones.title: DCO completion period extended to 2032
milestones.description: SoS extended the completion period by seven years to 27 October 2032, providing a further construction window.

TR0310001.name: Able Marine Energy Park Material Change 3
TR0310001.summary: Pre-application proposal by Able Humber Ports to amend the 2014 Able Marine Energy Park DCO, altering quay layout and construction method and permitting certain incoming cargo. PINS adopted a Scoping Opinion on 26 February 2025; formal application submission was anticipated in Q4 2025.

milestones.title: Material Change 3 registered at pre-application
milestones.description: Able Humber Ports registered the third proposed material change with PINS as TR0310001.

milestones.title: Scoping Opinion adopted
milestones.description: PINS adopted a Scoping Opinion for the MC3 environmental impact assessment.

milestones.title: Statutory pre-application consultation
milestones.description: Applicant undertaking statutory consultation ahead of submission.

milestones.title: Application submission anticipated
milestones.description: Formal application anticipated Q4 2025.

TR030002.name: York Potash Harbour Facilities Order
TR030002.summary: DCO consented July 2016 for a Teesside harbour at Bran Sands designed to export polyhalite from Sirius Minerals' Woodsmith Mine in the North York Moors. Sirius's bond-financing failure in 2019 triggered a £405m rescue takeover by Anglo American in March 2020; the order was re-phased in 2022, development was scaled back in 2024-25 after BHP's takeover approach, and Mitsubishi signed an investment agreement in February 2026 with first production now targeted for 2032.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Sirius Minerals subsidiary York Potash Ltd submitted the DCO for Bran Sands harbour.

milestones.title: Examination opens
milestones.description: PINS accepted the application; preliminary hearing 21 July 2015.

milestones.title: Secretary of State grants DCO
milestones.description: SoS made the York Potash Harbour Facilities Order 2016 authorising a 28m × 280m quay at Bran Sands and a conveyor link to Wilton International.

milestones.title: Woodsmith Mine construction begins
milestones.description: Sirius began construction of the linked Woodsmith Mine shaft and 37km mineral transport tunnel on which the harbour depends.

milestones.title: Sirius $500m bond cancelled
milestones.description: Sirius pulled a $500m senior bond after UK government declined to guarantee $1bn of debt, triggering an existential financing crisis.

milestones.title: Anglo American agrees £405m takeover
milestones.description: Boards of Sirius and Anglo American announced a recommended cash acquisition at 5.5p per share, valuing Sirius at ~£405m.

milestones.title: Anglo American completes acquisition
milestones.description: Scheme of arrangement effective; Anglo American Projects UK Ltd took ownership of Sirius and the Woodsmith / York Potash assets.

milestones.title: Harbour Facilities (Amendment) Order 2022
milestones.description: Non-material change consented re-phasing the harbour into Phase 1a/1b for incremental build to 6.5 Mt/yr throughput.

milestones.title: Anglo slows Woodsmith after BHP bid
milestones.description: Following rejection of BHP's $49bn takeover approach, Anglo slashed Woodsmith capex, slowing tunnelling and halting production shaft sinking; ~800 jobs lost.

milestones.title: Mitsubishi investment agreement signed
milestones.description: Mitsubishi took an initial equity stake with option for up to 25% at future FID; first production now targeted 2032.

milestones.title: Targeted first production
milestones.description: First polyhalite production through Woodsmith and the Teesside harbour now expected 2032, contingent on a future Anglo FID.

TR030003.name: Tilbury2
TR030003.summary: New 152-acre port terminal developed by Port of Tilbury London (Forth Ports) on the former Tilbury Power Station site, comprising a roll-on/roll-off ferry terminal and a construction materials aggregates terminal. DCO granted February 2019, ferry terminal opened May 2020, with full construction complete November 2020 and official opening by the Prime Minister in January 2021.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Port of Tilbury London submitted its DCO under section 37 of the Planning Act 2008.

milestones.title: Accepted for examination
milestones.description: PINS accepted the application on behalf of the Secretary of State.

milestones.title: Six-month public examination
milestones.description: Panel of three considered ecology, flood risk, heritage, noise and air quality issues.

milestones.title: Development consent granted
milestones.description: SoS for Transport granted the DCO authorising construction of the new port terminal.

milestones.title: Construction begins
milestones.description: GRAHAM began main works on both terrestrial and marine packages mid-March 2019.

milestones.title: Ferry terminal opens
milestones.description: £250m Tilbury2 ferry freight terminal opened, handling containers and trailers with P&O Ferries during the COVID-19 pandemic.

milestones.title: Construction completed
milestones.description: Delivered the UK's largest unaccompanied freight ferry port plus a 2km conveyor and 775m rail head.

milestones.title: Official opening by Prime Minister
milestones.description: PM Boris Johnson officially opened Tilbury2, marking the UK's newest port becoming fully operational.

TR030007.name: Immingham Eastern Ro-Ro Terminal
TR030007.summary: A £200m+ joint ABP / Stena Line roll-on/roll-off freight terminal at the Port of Immingham, comprising a three-berth jetty and terminal buildings. DCO granted October 2024; a High Court JR by adjacent oil terminal operators was dismissed July 2025 (Saini J, [2025] EWHC 1992 (Admin)); construction began November 2025 with completion targeted 2026.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation; scheme reduced
milestones.description: ABP held statutory consultation; scheme reduced from four berths to three following feedback.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: ABP submitted the DCO after withdrawing an earlier January 2023 submission.

milestones.title: Accepted for examination
milestones.description: PINS accepted the application; interested-party registration opened 9 March 2023.

milestones.title: Examination commenced
milestones.description: Preliminary meeting and first issue-specific hearing opened the six-month examination.

milestones.title: Recommendation to Secretary of State
milestones.description: Examining Authority issued its Recommendation Report.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: SoS Louise Haigh granted DCO (SI 2024/1014).

milestones.title: Correction Order issued
milestones.description: SoS issued a Correction Order (SI 2025/143) to amend drafting errors.

milestones.title: High Court dismisses JR
milestones.description: Saini J dismissed the JR by the adjacent Immingham Oil Terminal operators on both EIA-adequacy and apparent-bias grounds.

milestones.title: Construction begins
milestones.description: ABP and Stena Line started work on the £200m+ terminal; ~700 construction jobs, 200 permanent operational roles, completion targeted 2026.

TR030008.name: Immingham Green Energy Terminal
TR030008.summary: DCO consent granted February 2025 for ABP to build a liquid bulk import terminal at Immingham designed to receive green ammonia and feed an adjacent Air Products 300MW hydrogen cracking facility supplied from NEOM. In June 2025 Air Products announced it was pausing the hydrogen production element, citing UK policy that excludes imported renewable ammonia from hydrogen support schemes, leaving the consented terminal without a confirmed anchor customer.

milestones.title: First statutory consultation
milestones.description: ABP ran its first statutory consultation 9 January - 20 February 2023.

milestones.title: Second statutory consultation
milestones.description: Second round 24 May - 20 July 2023 on refinements to the scheme design.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: ABP submitted the DCO to PINS.

milestones.title: Accepted for examination
milestones.description: PINS accepted the application; Examining Authority appointed 31 October.

milestones.title: Preliminary meeting; examination opens
milestones.description: Six-month statutory examination begins.

milestones.title: Recommendation issued
milestones.description: Examining Authority submitted its recommendation report to SoS for Transport.

milestones.title: Development consent granted
milestones.description: Heidi Alexander granted DCO authorising the multi-user liquid bulk import terminal.

milestones.title: Air Products pauses hydrogen anchor project
milestones.description: Air Products paused its £2bn ammonia-cracking facility citing UK policy excluding imported renewable ammonia from hydrogen support schemes. DCO consent remains in place but no anchor customer.

milestones.title: FID outstanding
milestones.description: No FID or construction start announced; project depends on UK government extending hydrogen support to imported renewable ammonia.

TR040011.name: Portishead Branch Line - MetroWest Phase 1
TR040011.summary: Reopening of the 5.5km Portishead branch line (closed to passengers in 1964) with two new stations at Portishead and Pill, delivering an hourly direct service to Bristol Temple Meads. Promoted by North Somerset Council, the DCO was granted in November 2022; main construction is scheduled to start in April 2026 with services expected in early 2028 — 64 years after closure.

milestones.title: Passenger services withdrawn
milestones.description: Portishead branch closes to passengers under the Beeching cuts.

milestones.title: Revival campaign launched
milestones.description: Portishead Railway Group begins sustained effort to reopen the line, citing population growth and A369 congestion.

milestones.title: Initial business case and City Deal funding
milestones.description: Costs estimated at ~£58m, funded via West of England City Deal.

milestones.title: Scheme rescoped after cost increase
milestones.description: Costs rise to £145-175m; staged approach reduces initial offer to one train per hour.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: North Somerset Council submitted the DCO to PINS.

milestones.title: Examination begins (COVID-delayed)
milestones.description: Six-month examination opened after pandemic delay, ran to April 2021.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: SoS for Transport granted DCO, authorising reopening of 5.5km of line and new stations at Portishead and Pill.

milestones.title: Final funding package confirmed
milestones.description: Total scheme cost ~£152m (£80m main 1b construction).

milestones.title: Enabling works and contractor appointment
milestones.description: Vegetation clearance and utility diversions; Morgan Sindall confirmed as principal contractor after original 2023 award to VolkerFitzpatrick.

milestones.title: Main construction begins
milestones.description: Main works commence with compounds at Harbour Road (Portishead), Monmouth Road (Pill) and off the A369.

milestones.title: Passenger services begin
milestones.description: Hourly direct services from Portishead to Bristol Temple Meads expected early 2028, ending a 64-year passenger service gap.

TR040012.name: East West Rail - Bedford to Cambridge and Western improvements
TR040012.summary: Major revival of the Oxford-Cambridge Varsity Line closed in 1967-68, with the Bedford-Cambridge section requiring a DCO for new-build railway via Tempsford and Cambourne approaching Cambridge from the south. Project is championed by the NIC as central to the Oxford-Cambridge growth corridor; in its final pre-application statutory consultation (April-June 2026) ahead of a planned DCO submission in January 2027.

milestones.title: Oxford-Cambridge Varsity Line closed
milestones.description: LNWR's 1862 Oxford-Cambridge line closes to passengers on grounds of being uneconomic, despite not being listed in the 1963 Beeching Report. The modern East West Rail project is the revival of this closed route.

milestones.title: East West Rail Consortium formed
milestones.description: Local authorities led by Ipswich BC form the EWR Consortium to promote and secure the strategic railway.

milestones.title: NIC Partnering for Prosperity report
milestones.description: National Infrastructure Commission identifies completion of East West Rail by 2030 as central to the Cambridge-MK-Oxford growth arc.

milestones.title: East West Railway Company established
milestones.description: DfT creates EWR Co Ltd as a non-departmental public arms-length body.

milestones.title: First Bedford-Cambridge route consultation
milestones.description: EWR Co consulted on five corridor options A-E.

milestones.title: Route E selected
milestones.description: Grant Shapps announced Route E (Bedford-Tempsford-Cambourne-Cambridge) as preferred corridor.

milestones.title: Nine alignment options consultation
milestones.description: EWR Co consulted on nine alignments within Route E; ~10,000 responses raised over 160,000 matters.

milestones.title: Southern approach to Cambridge confirmed
milestones.description: New stations at Tempsford and Cambourne; southern approach into Cambridge connecting with Cambridge South.

milestones.title: Non-statutory consultation on emerging designs
milestones.description: Ten-week consultation Nov 2024 - Jan 2025; formal council responses from Bedford, Cambridge and other affected authorities.

milestones.title: Bicester-Bletchley western section handed over
milestones.description: EWR Alliance completed western section on time and on budget; freight services from June 2025; passenger services delayed by driver-only operation dispute.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: Eight-week statutory consultation 14 April - 9 June 2026, the final formal engagement before DCO submission. Now includes provision for a Stewartby station to serve the proposed Universal Resort theme park.

milestones.title: DCO submission to PINS
milestones.description: EWR Co anticipates submitting the DCO in January 2027.

milestones.title: Targeted full Oxford-Cambridge service
milestones.description: Indicative target for full Oxford-Cambridge passenger services, per NIC 2017 recommendation.

TR050001.name: Daventry International Rail Freight Terminal
TR050001.summary: DIRFT III was the first Strategic Rail Freight Interchange consented under the Planning Act 2008 regime, with a DCO granted on 3 July 2014 authorising approximately 8 million sq ft of rail-connected warehousing on the former Rugby Radio Station site in Northamptonshire. Prologis has built out the park in phases, with a new intermodal rail terminal completed in 2021 and remaining plots being developed on a build-to-suit basis through 2025-2026.

milestones.title: DIRFT Phase I opens
milestones.description: Original Daventry International Rail Freight Terminal opened 27 May 1997 with a 475,000 sq ft Eddie Stobart warehouse and rail link to WCML.

milestones.title: DIRFT Phase II planning permission
milestones.description: Local permission granted for a 130-acre westward expansion adding 1.9m sq ft of rail-served warehousing; Tesco 840,000 sq ft DC completed Sep 2011.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Rugby Radio Station Ltd Partnership and Prologis UK submitted the DCO for DIRFT III on the former Rugby Radio Station site.

milestones.title: DCO granted (first SRFI under PA2008)
milestones.description: DIRFT III became the first SRFI in the UK to secure a DCO under the Planning Act 2008 regime.

milestones.title: First DIRFT III warehouses delivered
milestones.description: Initial speculative and build-to-suit warehouses came forward; adjacent Houlton residential development saw first occupations.

milestones.title: Intermodal terminal construction begins
milestones.description: Prologis appointed Winvic under a £29m contract; 79,000 sq m of concrete slab, 9 km of new track, three bridges.

milestones.title: New intermodal rail terminal completed
milestones.description: Capable of handling up to 24 trains/day at 775m length; combined with existing railport lifts capacity to ~32 trains/day.

milestones.title: Major occupiers take DIRFT III space
milestones.description: M&S (1.3m sq ft), GXO, XPO, Dunelm — establishing DIRFT as the UK's largest inland intermodal hub.

milestones.title: Remaining plots under build-to-suit
milestones.description: ~1.8m sq ft remains; new speculative units (e.g. DC107) marketed for Q3 2026 delivery.

TR050005.name: West Midlands Interchange
TR050005.summary: Strategic Rail Freight Interchange at Four Ashes near M6 J12 in South Staffordshire, promoted by Four Ashes Ltd (Oxford Properties / Logistics Capital Partners). DCO granted on 4 May 2020 after a 2018-2019 examination; no judicial review was filed. Enabling works started in 2023 and main construction in October 2024, with Carlsberg Britvic confirmed in July 2025 as the first occupier of the £2bn logistics hub.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Four Ashes Ltd submitted the DCO to PINS.

milestones.title: Accepted for examination
milestones.description: PINS accepted the application.

milestones.title: Examination held
milestones.description: Six-month examination with preliminary meeting, written reps and issue-specific hearings.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: SoS for Transport approved the West Midlands Rail Freight Interchange Order 2020.

milestones.title: JR window closes without challenge
milestones.description: South Staffs Council resolved not to challenge; Stop the WMI campaign could not fund a claim independently.

milestones.title: Phase 1 enabling works begin
milestones.description: Phase 1 main works started: new road infrastructure, public rights of way, energy undergrounding, earthworks.

milestones.title: Main construction on site
milestones.description: Main construction commenced on the wider logistics hub platform.

milestones.title: Carlsberg Britvic confirmed as first occupier
milestones.description: First occupier announced — 222,000 sq ft depot with c.£4m fit-out; BREEAM Excellent / Net Zero Carbon Aligned.

milestones.title: Groundbreaking on first unit
milestones.description: First spades in the ground for the Carlsberg Britvic depot.

milestones.title: First occupier facility opens (expected)
milestones.description: Carlsberg Britvic's 222,000 sq ft depot expected to open Q4 2026.

TR030006.name: Able Marine Energy Park Material Change 2
TR030006.summary: First-ever material amendment to a Development Consent Order under the Planning Act 2008, made to the 2014 Able Marine Energy Park DCO. Submitted by Able Humber Ports in June 2021; SoS authorised the changes on 18 July 2022, modifying quay layout (two changes) and a footpath diversion.

milestones.title: Material Change 2 application submitted
milestones.description: Able Humber Ports submitted the material amendment seeking changes to quay layout and footpath diversion.

milestones.title: Examination commenced
milestones.description: PINS began examining the first material change application under the Planning Act 2008.

milestones.title: Recommendation submitted
milestones.description: Examining Authority concluded examination and submitted its recommendation to SoS for Transport.

milestones.title: Amendment Order made
milestones.description: SoS authorised the proposed changes — the first material amendment to a DCO granted under the Planning Act 2008 regime.

TR0310002.name: Solent Gateway 2
TR0310002.summary: ABP-led proposal to expand the Solent Gateway port at Marchwood with a new two-berth Ro-Ro jetty, automotive terminal with multi-deck vehicle storage, and a direct A326 access road avoiding Marchwood village. Currently in pre-application; non-statutory consultation closed October 2025, Scoping Opinion issued December 2025; statutory consultation 2026 and DCO submission June 2027 expected; ~£500m construction contract.

milestones.title: ABP signals support for Marchwood expansion
milestones.description: ABP publicly committed to helping Marchwood Port achieve its full potential, foreshadowing NSIP-scale proposals.

milestones.title: Non-statutory consultation launched
milestones.description: ABP opened consultation 16 Sep - 13 Oct 2025; over 1,200 attended events and 300+ submitted responses.

milestones.title: PINS Scoping Opinion published
milestones.description: Scoping Opinion set out the scope of EIA required for the DCO.

milestones.title: Procurement: £500m ECI/works tender
milestones.description: ABP scheduled indicative tender publication for an ECI and Works contractor package ~£500m, provisional dates Oct 2026 - Aug 2031.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation planned
milestones.description: ABP has committed to formal Section 42/47 consultation during 2026 ahead of DCO submission.

milestones.title: DCO application submission expected
milestones.description: DCO application expected June 2027.

TR040001.name: North Doncaster Rail Chord (near Shaftholme)
TR040001.summary: One of the first Development Consent Orders granted under the Planning Act 2008, the North Doncaster Rail Chord (Shaftholme Flyover) is a 3.2 km double-track freight link including a 246 m six-span viaduct over the East Coast Main Line. Consent granted October 2012; line opened to freight traffic on 1 June 2014.

milestones.title: Concept identified in Route Utilisation Strategy
milestones.description: Grade-separated chord near Shaftholme identified as a capacity enhancement in Network Rail's Yorkshire & Humber RUS.

milestones.title: Pre-application consultation
milestones.description: Network Rail formally announced the £45m scheme in autumn 2011.

milestones.title: DCO submitted and accepted
milestones.description: One of the earliest rail DCOs to be examined under the Planning Act 2008 regime.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: SoS made the Network Rail (North Doncaster Chord) Order 2012.

milestones.title: Construction commenced
milestones.description: Site work began; early progress disrupted by the Hatfield Colliery landslip.

milestones.title: Opened to regular freight traffic
milestones.description: The chord and Shaftholme Viaduct opened to regular freight services, removing freight conflicts from the ECML at Doncaster.

milestones.title: Official opening ceremony
milestones.description: Patrick McLoughlin formally opened the £45m chord.

TR040002.name: Ipswich Rail Chord
TR040002.summary: A ~1.2km freight chord (the 'Bacon Factory Curve') north of Ipswich Goods Yard connecting the East Suffolk Line to the Great Eastern Main Line, allowing Felixstowe-Nuneaton freight to avoid reversing through Ipswich and cutting roughly an hour from journey times. Consented under the Planning Act 2008 in September 2012; opened to commercial traffic March 2014 at ~£59m (part EU TEN-T funded).

milestones.title: DCO application accepted by IPC
milestones.description: Network Rail's application accepted; registration of interested parties opened.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: SoS granted consent; Ipswich Chord Order 2012 came into force 26 September.

milestones.title: Main construction begins
milestones.description: Four new bridges, two embankments, a 413m retaining wall and 1.2km of new track and signalling.

milestones.title: First revenue freight train
milestones.description: GBRf 66733 hauled the first revenue-earning freight train along the newly commissioned chord.

milestones.title: Chord opened to commercial traffic
milestones.description: £59m Ipswich Chord opened on time and on budget; cuts ~1 hour off Felixstowe-Midlands freight journey times.

TR040004.name: Stafford Area Improvements - Norton Bridge Railway
TR040004.summary: Network Rail DCO authorising a grade-separated flyover at Norton Bridge Junction plus six miles of new 100 mph railway, removing a major at-grade bottleneck on the West Coast Main Line. Consent granted March 2014; flyover commissioned for revenue services on 29 March 2016 as part of the wider £250m Stafford Area Improvement Programme.

milestones.title: Pre-application consultation begins
milestones.description: Network Rail engaged Norton Bridge residents and stakeholders on options to grade-separate the junction.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Network Rail submitted the DCO for the Norton Bridge Area Improvements.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: Network Rail (Norton Bridge Area Improvements) Order 2014 came into force 21 April 2014.

milestones.title: Main construction begins
milestones.description: Staffordshire Alliance (Atkins, Laing O'Rourke, Network Rail, VolkerRail) began earthworks on the six-mile alignment.

milestones.title: Flyover opens to revenue services
milestones.description: First passenger trains used the new grade-separated flyover, enabling additional WCML, cross-country and freight paths.

milestones.title: Programme completion
milestones.description: £250m Stafford Area Improvement Programme complete, delivering up to two extra trains per hour London-North West and one extra freight path per hour.

TR040005.name: Redditch Branch Enhancement Scheme
TR040005.summary: Network Rail's DCO to construct an ~3 km dynamic passing loop on the single-track Cross-City Line branch between Barnt Green and Redditch, including a new platform and footbridge at Alvechurch. Order made 31 October 2013; line reopened after an eight-week blockade on 1 September 2014; three-trains-per-hour timetable from December 2014.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Network Rail submitted the DCO to PINS.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: Network Rail (Redditch Branch Enhancement) Order 2013 came into force 21 November 2013.

milestones.title: Eight-week blockade begins
milestones.description: Branch closed to passengers for construction of the new passing loop and Alvechurch platform.

milestones.title: Line reopens to passengers
milestones.description: Reopened following completion of the dynamic loop, new northbound Alvechurch platform, footbridge and signalling works.

milestones.title: Three-trains-per-hour timetable
milestones.description: Cross-City Line service to Redditch increased from two to three trains per hour.

TR050002.name: East Midlands Gateway Rail Freight Interchange
TR050002.summary: Strategic Rail Freight Interchange north of East Midlands Airport near Castle Donington, consented by DCO in January 2016. Developed as SEGRO Logistics Park East Midlands Gateway (~700 acres, ~4m sq ft of warehousing); the rail terminal is operated by Maritime Transport under a 25-year lease and has been fully operational since the first commercial freight train arrived on 7 January 2020.

milestones.title: Statutory pre-application consultation
milestones.description: Roxhill ran statutory community consultation under a Statement of Community Consultation.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Roxhill (Kegworth) submitted the DCO; accepted 19 September 2014.

milestones.title: Examination opens
milestones.description: Examination by an appointed Panel began.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: SoS for Transport authorised the SRFI: up to 547,414 sqm of warehousing, intermodal terminal up to 16 trains/day at 775m length, and new rail link to the Castle Donington branch.

milestones.title: Enabling infrastructure works begin
milestones.description: Construction commenced on the c.£100m enabling package, including A453 dualling and A50 junction upgrades.

milestones.title: Rail connection commissioned
milestones.description: New rail connection from the Castle Donington line commissioned; warehouse plots progressively let to Amazon, DHL, XPO, Games Workshop.

milestones.title: First commercial freight train
milestones.description: DB Cargo UK operated the first commercial rail freight service into the new East Midlands Gateway Terminal.

milestones.title: Maritime terminal officially opened
milestones.description: Rail Minister formally opened Maritime Transport's intermodal terminal at EMG, operated under a 25-year lease from SEGRO.

TR050006.name: Northampton Gateway Rail Freight Interchange
TR050006.summary: A Strategic Rail Freight Interchange of up to 5-6 million sq ft beside M1 Junction 15, granted development consent in October 2019 under an application by Roxhill (subsequently acquired by SEGRO). Main construction began in 2021, a 2023 amendment order permitted occupation of warehousing in advance of the rail connection, the terminal was tied into the West Coast Main Line in 2025, and the SRFI officially opened in 2026.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Roxhill Developments submitted the DCO; accepted for examination on 15 June 2018.

milestones.title: Examination opens
milestones.description: Examining Authority began examination with autumn deadlines and an accompanied site inspection 18 December.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: Northampton Gateway RFI Order 2019 came into force 30 October 2019, authorising the SRFI, M1 J15 improvements, and a Roade bypass.

milestones.title: Main infrastructure works begin
milestones.description: Winvic began £107.5m infrastructure works, including earthworks, J15 improvements and plot platforms.

milestones.title: Rail freight connection construction begins
milestones.description: Construction of strategic rail freight connection commenced including tunnel works and terminal sidings.

milestones.title: Non-material amendment order made
milestones.description: Amendment Order 2023 permitted occupation of warehouse floorspace ahead of the rail connection being delivered.

milestones.title: Rail terminal tied into WCML
milestones.description: Network Rail completed the connection of the 35-acre rail freight terminal to the West Coast Main Line.

milestones.title: SRFI officially opens
milestones.description: Strategic Rail Freight Interchange opened, operating a five-day-a-week intermodal service to DP World Southampton.

TR050007.name: Hinckley National Rail Freight Interchange
TR050007.summary: Tritax Symmetry's proposed c.£750m Strategic Rail Freight Interchange north-east of Hinckley, Leicestershire, was refused development consent by the Secretary of State for Transport on 10 March 2025, primarily on highway safety grounds following the Planning Inspectorate's examination. Tritax stated it would seek legal advice on next steps; no JR has been confirmed in public records.

milestones.title: Project announced
milestones.description: Tritax Symmetry brought forward proposals; campaign opposition led by local MP and community groups.

milestones.title: Statutory pre-application consultation
milestones.description: Public consultation events; maximum building height later reduced from 33m to 28m.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Tritax Symmetry (Hinckley) Ltd submitted the DCO.

milestones.title: Examination opens
milestones.description: Preliminary meeting held; six-month examination begins.

milestones.title: Examination closes
milestones.description: Final deadline for written submissions and evidence.

milestones.title: ExA report submitted to SoS
milestones.description: Examining Authority's recommendation submitted to SoS for Transport.

milestones.title: 'Minded to refuse' letter; decision extended
milestones.description: SoS indicated minded to refuse on highway safety grounds; decision deadline extended to 10 March 2025.

milestones.title: Development consent REFUSED
milestones.description: SoS for Transport refused the DCO, concluding that adverse highway safety impacts outweighed the scheme's benefits and need case.

TR050008.name: Oxfordshire Strategic Rail Freight Interchange
TR050008.summary: Proposed Strategic Rail Freight Interchange next to the Chiltern Main Line south-west of M40 Junction 10 near Ardley, Oxfordshire, promoted by Oxfordshire Railfreight Limited (a Mountpark Logistics SPV). Comprises an intermodal rail terminal and up to ~603,850 sqm of warehousing. DCO submitted 31 March 2026, accepted for examination 28 April 2026; currently pre-examination.

milestones.title: Stage 1 non-statutory consultation
milestones.description: First round of public consultation 9 May - 4 July 2022 presenting an early scheme outline.

milestones.title: Stage 2 statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: Six-week statutory consultation under section 47 of the Planning Act 2008.

milestones.title: Stage 2 closes
milestones.description: Statutory consultation closed; responses reviewed ahead of finalising the DCO.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted to PINS
milestones.description: OxRL submitted the DCO covering an intermodal rail terminal and up to ~603,850 sqm of warehousing.

milestones.title: Application accepted for examination
milestones.description: PINS accepted the application after its 28-day acceptance review.

milestones.title: Examination expected to open
milestones.description: Six-month statutory examination expected in H2 2026.

milestones.title: Secretary of State decision expected
milestones.description: Following ExA recommendation, SoS decision expected in 2027.

TR0510001.name: Intermodal Logistics Park North
TR0510001.summary: Strategic Rail Freight Interchange proposed by Tritax Big Box on land east of Newton-le-Willows between the M6 and Chat Moss line. At full build-out the terminal is designed for up to 16 trains/day with ~767,000 m² of warehousing, projected ~6,000 jobs. Pre-application stage; DCO submission expected November 2026.

milestones.title: Informal non-statutory consultation launched
milestones.description: Tritax Big Box launched consultation with feedback accepted until 21 March 2025.

milestones.title: EIA Scoping Report submitted
milestones.description: Scoping Report published on the PINS portal.

milestones.title: Section 46 notification published
milestones.description: Applicant's Section 46 notification to PINS alongside Design Code.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation phase
milestones.description: Public events in Winwick, Culcheth and surrounding communities.

milestones.title: DCO application expected
milestones.description: DCO submission expected November 2026 following final consultation.

milestones.title: Examination expected
milestones.description: Examination anticipated after acceptance; SoS decision targeted 2027-28.

TR0510002.name: East Midlands Gateway Rail Freight Interchange Material Change
TR0510002.summary: SEGRO's Material Change Order application to amend the 2016 East Midlands Gateway DCO, seeking consent for an additional warehouse on Plot 16 (EMG1), increased rail-freight crane heights, and expanded bus terminal and site management facilities. Currently in Examination, jointly with the related EMG2 DCO (BC0410001); SoS decision expected ~March 2027.

milestones.title: Application received by PINS
milestones.description: SEGRO submitted the Material Change Order application to amend the EMG RFI and Highway Order 2016.

milestones.title: Application accepted and published
milestones.description: PINS opened the 28-day relevant representation registration window; examined jointly with EMG2 DCO (BC0410001).

milestones.title: Preliminary Meeting; examination opens
milestones.description: Examining Authority held the Preliminary Meeting at 10am followed by Compulsory Acquisition Hearing 1; Issue Specific Hearings 1 and 2 on 11-12 March.

milestones.title: Open Floor Hearing 1 and CAH2
milestones.description: Open Floor Hearing 1 and Compulsory Acquisition Hearing 2 held following Deadline 3 submissions.

milestones.title: Examination expected to close
milestones.description: Statutory examination due to conclude around September 2026.

milestones.title: Secretary of State decision expected
milestones.description: Recommendation due within 3 months of exam close, SoS decision a further 3 months later.

WS010001.name: East Northants Resource Management Facility
WS010001.summary: Hazardous and low-level radioactive waste landfill at King's Cliffe, Northamptonshire, operated by Augean South Ltd. One of the earliest waste NSIPs (DCO 2013, SI 2013/1752); a Western Extension DCO followed in January 2023, allowing operations until 2046. Long history of organised local opposition, particularly over the disposal of low-level radioactive waste.

milestones.title: Kings Cliffe landfill begins hazardous waste operations
milestones.description: Site opens as a hazardous waste landfill at Stamford Road, King's Cliffe.

milestones.title: Augean applies to dispose of low-level radioactive waste
milestones.description: Submits proposals to dispose of LLW and high-volume VLLW in phases 4B, 5A and 5B; triggers Environment Agency permit consultation.

milestones.title: 'Waste Watchers' (WANT) opposition mobilises
milestones.description: Residents organise through WANT (Waste Watchers Against Nuclear Technology); Northamptonshire CC rejects the LLW proposal after strong local protests.

milestones.title: SoS approves LLW disposal on appeal
milestones.description: After recovered appeal and Public Inquiry, SoS grants permission to deposit low-level radioactive waste; EA permit decision 25 May 2011. Hundreds of protestors march in King's Cliffe.

milestones.title: Original DCO granted under PA2008
milestones.description: East Northamptonshire Resource Management Facility Order 2013 (SI 2013/1752) — one of the earliest waste NSIPs consented under Planning Act 2008.

milestones.title: Groundwater contamination charity payment
milestones.description: Augean pays charity in lieu of prosecution after EA finds groundwater contamination linked to site operations.

milestones.title: Augean PLC acquired by Ancala/Fiera (£341m)
milestones.description: Ancala Partners / Fiera Infrastructure consortium beats Morgan Stanley Infrastructure's £314.9m bid with a 325p/share offer (~£341m), taking Augean private.

milestones.title: Western Extension DCO submitted (WS010005)
milestones.description: Augean South submits the Western Extension DCO; PINS accepts 24 September 2021.

milestones.title: Western Extension DCO granted
milestones.description: SoS for Levelling Up granted the DCO (SI 2023/110): expansion from 200,000 to 250,000 tpa and operations extended until 2046.

milestones.title: Operational ceiling under current consent
milestones.description: Landfilling and soil treatment must cease and the site be restored by the 2046 consent end-date.

WS010003.name: Whitemoss Landfill Western Extension
WS010003.summary: Hazardous waste landfill extension at Skelmersdale, Lancashire — the only hazardous waste landfill serving the North West. Consented as an NSIP in May 2015 after a contentious examination drawing over 3,000 representations (second-highest of any NSIP at the time). Survived multiple JRs from local residents and ARROW; site now operated by Veolia following 2019 acquisition.

milestones.title: Western Extension proposed
milestones.description: Whitemoss Landfill Ltd developed proposals for a new landfill void to the west of the existing site, classified as an NSIP under PA2008.

milestones.title: Examination opens
milestones.description: Six-month examination ran 21 May - 21 November 2014; 3,297 relevant representations, the second-highest of any NSIP at the time.

milestones.title: ExA report submitted
milestones.description: Examining Authority submitted its recommendation to the Secretary of State.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: The White Moss Landfill Order 2015 (SI 2015/1317) granted consent for the Western Extension.

milestones.title: Reaffirmed by Greg Clark
milestones.description: After an initial legal challenge, Communities Secretary Greg Clark reapproved the scheme citing strategic need for hazardous waste self-sufficiency.

milestones.title: Judicial review permitted
milestones.description: A Skelmersdale resident won permission for a full JR; ARROW (Action to Reduce and Recycle Our Waste) and landowner Arthur Scarisbrick led the challenge.

milestones.title: Court of Appeal rejects challenge
milestones.description: Lindblom and Irwin LJJ dismissed the appeal, finding no legal flaw in the SoS's decision.

milestones.title: Supreme Court refuses permission to appeal
milestones.description: ARROW's application for permission to appeal refused, exhausting JR route.

milestones.title: Veolia acquires Whitemoss Landfill
milestones.description: Veolia plc acquired Whitemoss Landfill Ltd, integrating the site into its UK hazardous waste landfill estate.

milestones.title: Site operating under Veolia
milestones.description: Continues operating as a hazardous waste landfill; the only operational hazardous waste landfill serving the North West.

WS010005.name: East Northants Resource Management Facility Western Extension
WS010005.summary: Augean South's westward extension of the King's Cliffe hazardous and low-level radioactive waste landfill, adding 2.5 million m³ of void space across 26.8 hectares and extending site operations until 2046. DCO granted by the SoS in January 2023; local opposition focused on wildlife connectivity between Fineshade Wood and Collyweston Great Wood. Construction commenced in 2023.

milestones.title: Land option secured for extension
milestones.description: Augean took an option on approximately 90 acres of land adjacent to the existing ENRMF site to enable a westward extension.

milestones.title: Statutory pre-application consultation begins
milestones.description: Augean launched statutory consultation running through to February 2021; significant concerns raised about wildlife connectivity.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Augean South submitted the DCO; PINS accepted 24 September 2021.

milestones.title: Examination held with hearings
milestones.description: Examining Authority held the six-month examination including ISH3 in June 2022; evidence from Anglian Water, Friends of Fineshade and local residents.

milestones.title: ExA recommendation issued
milestones.description: Examining Authority submitted its recommendation to the Secretary of State.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: Lee Rowley, on behalf of SoS for Levelling Up, granted the DCO authorising a 2.5 million m³ westward landfill extension over 26.8 hectares, extending operations to 2046. SI 2023/110.

milestones.title: Construction commenced
milestones.description: Augean began enabling works in spring 2023, with protective fencing erected at woodland edges and clay engineering for the containment cells through summer.

milestones.title: Authorised end of site operations
milestones.description: DCO permits operations until 2046, after which restoration and ecological enhancement of the link between Fineshade Wood and Collyweston Great Wood is required.

EN010001.name: Hinkley Point C New Nuclear Power Station
EN010001.summary: Hinkley Point C was first proposed by the CEGB in 1982 as part of a UK PWR programme; it secured planning consent in 1990 after a 14-month public inquiry but was abandoned in 1995 when the privatised electricity market would not finance it. Revived by the 2008 nuclear White Paper, EDF took the project through a 2013 Development Consent Order and 2016 Final Investment Decision; construction is now under way with first power targeted for 2030 and total cost projected at around £46bn (2024 prices).

milestones.title: CEGB announces Hinkley Point C
milestones.description: Central Electricity Generating Board announces plans for a third station at Hinkley Point as part of a UK PWR programme alongside Sizewell B.

milestones.title: CEGB submits planning application
milestones.description: CEGB lodges a formal planning application for a 1,200 MW PWR station with West Somerset District Council.

milestones.title: Barnes Inquiry opens
milestones.description: 14-month public inquiry opens at Cannington, hearing around 600 witnesses and considering some 22,000 objections.

milestones.title: Inquiry recommends approval; consent granted
milestones.description: Michael Barnes QC's 3,300-page report recommends consent for the station; planning permission subsequently granted.

milestones.title: Nuclear stripped from privatisation
milestones.description: Electricity privatisation proceeds without nuclear assets; new PWR build is left without low-cost government finance.

milestones.title: Hinkley Point C abandoned
milestones.description: Following the 1994-95 government nuclear review, ministers conclude the private sector will not finance new nuclear and the project is shelved.

milestones.title: Nuclear White Paper
milestones.description: Brown government publishes 'Meeting the Energy Challenge', concluding nuclear should play a role and inviting private investment.

milestones.title: EDF acquires British Energy
milestones.description: EDF completes its £12.5bn takeover of British Energy, gaining the Hinkley Point site.

milestones.title: Hinkley Point nominated
milestones.description: Government lists Hinkley Point among eight sites considered suitable for new nuclear under the National Policy Statement process.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: NNB Generation Company (EDF subsidiary) submits its DCO application to the Infrastructure Planning Commission.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: Energy Secretary Ed Davey grants the DCO authorising EDF to build and operate Hinkley Point C.

milestones.title: Strike price agreed
milestones.description: Government and EDF agree a Contract for Difference at £92.50/MWh (2012 prices), index-linked, for 35 years from commissioning.

milestones.title: EDF Final Investment Decision
milestones.description: EDF's board approves FID with CGN as 33.5% partner; UK government approval follows in September.

milestones.title: First nuclear concrete poured
milestones.description: EDF pours the first nuclear-grade concrete for the Unit 1 common raft, formally starting nuclear island construction.

milestones.title: Cost rises to £22-23bn
milestones.description: EDF reports the project budget has risen to £21.5-22.5bn (2015 prices) with a 15-month delay risk.

milestones.title: Cost up to £46bn, start delayed to 2029-31
milestones.description: EDF announces the project will cost up to £46bn in 2024 prices, with Unit 1 start-up now expected between 2029 and 2031.

milestones.title: First power slips to 2030
milestones.description: EDF confirms electromechanical work has pushed Unit 1 first power back to 2030; outturn cost rising to about £35bn (2015 prices).

milestones.title: Unit 1 first power (target)
milestones.description: EDF's current schedule targets first electricity from Unit 1 in 2030.

milestones.title: Unit 2 first power (target)
milestones.description: Unit 2 scheduled to come online approximately a year after Unit 1, completing the 3.2 GW twin-EPR station.

WW010001.name: Thames Tideway Tunnel
WW010001.summary: London's 25km, 7.2m-diameter super-sewer intercepting 34 of the most polluting combined sewer overflows on Bazalgette's Victorian system. Consented under the NSIP regime in September 2014, financed via a separate Ofwat-regulated infrastructure provider (Bazalgette Tunnel Limited), built 2016-2024 at a final cost of around £5bn (up from the £3.5bn estimate at DCO), fully commissioned February 2025.

milestones.title: EU Urban Waste Water Treatment Directive non-compliance identified
milestones.description: UK identified as non-compliant with the EU Urban Waste Water Treatment Directive due to repeated sewage overflows from the Victorian-era Bazalgette interceptor system into the tidal Thames. Thames Tideway Strategic Study commissioned to assess options.

milestones.title: Thames Tideway Strategic Study reports
