milestones.description: The Strategic Study (initiated 2001) concluded that screening, storage or treatment at the discharge point was required, recommending a deep storage and transfer tunnel along the river as the preferred solution.

milestones.title: Thames Tunnel proposal adopted; route options shortlisted
milestones.description: Three main alignments were evaluated: the full River Thames Route, the Rotherhithe Route, and the Abbey Mills Route. The Abbey Mills option (connecting at Abbey Mills Pumping Station via the Lee Tunnel) was selected as it saved around 9km and £900m versus the full river route.

milestones.title: Phase 1 public consultation opens
milestones.description: Thames Water launched its first phase of consultation (September 2010 - January 2011) on the preferred Abbey Mills route and a shortlist drawn from 373 candidate worksites, receiving 2,389 feedback forms and 480 letters. Strong objection at the originally preferred King Edward Memorial Park (Shadwell) and Chambers Wharf (Bermondsey) sites led to later route adjustments.

milestones.title: Phase 2 consultation including Pimlico alternatives
milestones.description: A second consultation (November 2011 - February 2012) presented revised worksites, including alternatives around Pimlico/Chelsea Embankment and downstream of Abbey Mills. Cost estimate rose to £3.6bn (Thames Tunnel) plus around £1bn for the Lee Tunnel.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted to PINS
milestones.description: Thames Water submitted its Development Consent Order application under the Planning Act 2008 to the Planning Inspectorate (ref WW010001); deemed valid on 27 March 2013.

milestones.title: Examination begins
milestones.description: A five-member Examining Authority was appointed (3 June 2013) and the preliminary meeting held on 12 September 2013. Public hearings ran November 2013 to February 2014; examination closed 12 March 2014.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: The Secretaries of State for Communities and Local Government and for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs jointly granted the DCO, overriding the Examining Authority's reservations on certain sites (notably King Edward Memorial Park and Chambers Wharf). Estimated capital cost at consent was around £3.5bn (2014 prices).

milestones.title: Judicial reviews fail
milestones.description: At least three judicial reviews challenging the DCO and site selection were brought by Blue Green UK, Southwark Council and others; all were dismissed, clearing the way to financial close.

milestones.title: Bazalgette Tunnel Limited licensed; commercial close
milestones.description: Ofwat designated Bazalgette Tunnel Limited (trading as Tideway), backed by Allianz, Amber Infrastructure, Dalmore Capital and DIF, as a separate regulated infrastructure provider under a bespoke project licence. The Regulated Asset Base model with government contingent support enabled a low cost of capital of around 2.5%.

milestones.title: Main construction begins
milestones.description: Site enabling works started across 24 main worksites along the Thames; main tunnelling contracts had been awarded in February 2015 to three joint ventures covering the west, central and east drives.

milestones.title: TBM tunnelling commences
milestones.description: Six tunnel boring machines began driving the 25km main tunnel at depths of 30-70m beneath the river. Secondary lining works began in 2019.

milestones.title: Main tunnelling completed
milestones.description: All TBM drives completed, connecting the western section at Acton Storm Tanks to Abbey Mills Pumping Station in the east. COVID-19 added an estimated £233m and around a year of delay to the programme.

milestones.title: First flows; system commissioning begins
milestones.description: The concrete plug separating the Tideway Tunnel from the Lee Tunnel was removed in May 2024; first sewage flowed into the system in September 2024 and valves at four of 21 CSO connection sites were brought into operation in October. Cost estimate had risen to around £4.5bn (2024 outturn).

milestones.title: Full commissioning
milestones.description: All 21 CSO connection sites brought online; the tunnel became fully operational, expected to cut CSO discharge events from around 60 a year to fewer than five and capture approximately 95% of spill volume.

milestones.title: Official opening by King Charles III
milestones.description: The completed Thames Tideway Tunnel was formally opened, marking the most significant upgrade to London's sewerage system since Bazalgette. Final outturn cost reported at around £5bn including financing.

WW010003.name: Cambridge Waste Water Treatment Plant Relocation
WW010003.summary: Anglian Water's proposal to relocate Cambridge's existing Cowley Road sewage works to a Green Belt site at Honey Hill (Horningsea/Fen Ditton/Stow-cum-Quy), to free the existing brownfield site for the 'North East Cambridge' (Hartree) housing district of c. 5,600-8,500 homes. Funded by a 2019 Housing Infrastructure Fund award, the project was opposed by Save Honey Hill and Friends of the Cam; the Examining Authority recommended refusal but the Secretary of State granted the DCO on 8 April 2025. A judicial review was threatened in April 2025 but dropped in May 2025, and in August 2025 MHCLG withdrew the HIF funding, leaving delivery uncertain.

milestones.title: Housing Infrastructure Fund allocation
milestones.description: Homes England allocates £227m HIF funding to Anglian Water and Cambridge City Council to relocate the existing Milton/Cowley Road waste water treatment plant, unlocking the North East Cambridge brownfield site for housing.

milestones.title: Phase One non-statutory consultation on site options
milestones.description: Anglian Water consults on a shortlist of three candidate relocation sites north of the A14: Honey Hill (between Fen Ditton, Horningsea and Stow-cum-Quy), land north-west of Milton, and land south-east of Impington. Around 5,000 responses received.

milestones.title: Honey Hill selected as preferred site
milestones.description: On 28 January 2021 Anglian Water announces Honey Hill (north of the A14 between Fen Ditton, Horningsea and Stow-cum-Quy) as its preferred relocation site. Save Honey Hill campaign group forms in opposition.

milestones.title: Phase Two statutory consultation on plant design
milestones.description: Statutory consultation on the design, layout and access proposals for the new treatment plant on the Honey Hill site, including masterplan, landscape and odour-management options.

milestones.title: Phase Three statutory consultation
milestones.description: Final round of statutory consultation on updated proposals ahead of DCO submission; opposition from Save Honey Hill, Friends of the Cam, parish councils and the local Green Party.

milestones.title: Initial DCO application submitted then withdrawn
milestones.description: Anglian Water submits its Development Consent Order application to the Planning Inspectorate on 31 January 2023, but withdraws it on 22 February 2023 after PINS advised that more work was needed on alternatives including a 'do-nothing' option and upgrading the existing site.

milestones.title: DCO application resubmitted
milestones.description: Anglian Water Services resubmits the DCO application to the Planning Inspectorate on 28 April 2023, including additional alternatives assessment.

milestones.title: Application accepted for examination
milestones.description: Planning Inspectorate accepts the resubmitted DCO application for examination on 24 May 2023.

milestones.title: Examination opens
milestones.description: Examining Authority opens its six-month examination, with three rounds of hearings including a Compulsory Acquisition Hearing on 9 January 2024. Issues scrutinised include Green Belt impact, alternatives, River Cam water quality and decommissioning.

milestones.title: Examination closes
milestones.description: Examination formally closes on 17 April 2024; the Examining Authority begins drafting its recommendation report to the Secretary of State.

milestones.title: Examining Authority recommends refusal
milestones.description: On 12 July 2024 the Examining Authority submits its report to the Secretary of State, concluding there is not a convincing case for the DCO and recommending it should not be granted.

milestones.title: Decision deadline extended
milestones.description: Defra Secretary of State extends the statutory decision deadline (originally October 2024) to allow further consideration of the recommendation against grant.

milestones.title: Secretary of State grants DCO
milestones.description: On 8 April 2025 Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs Steve Reed disagrees with the Examining Authority and grants development consent, citing the need to unlock North East Cambridge for thousands of new homes.

milestones.title: Save Honey Hill issues pre-action JR letter
milestones.description: Campaign group Save Honey Hill issues a pre-action protocol letter on 24 April 2025 setting out grounds for a proposed judicial review of the Secretary of State's decision.

milestones.title: Judicial review challenge dropped
milestones.description: Save Honey Hill abandons its judicial review plans after the government's response to the pre-action letter indicated the chance of success was low and the costs of proceeding could not be justified.

milestones.title: Government withdraws HIF funding
milestones.description: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government confirms in August 2025 that Housing Infrastructure Fund money will not be made available to fund the relocation, putting delivery of the consented scheme and the dependent North East Cambridge housing district into doubt.

BC0310001.name: The Trelavour Lithium Project
BC0310001.summary: Cornish Lithium G5 Limited proposes to mine lithium-bearing granite from the former Trelavour Pit china clay workings near St Austell and refine it on site into battery-grade lithium hydroxide monohydrate, targeting around 10,000 tonnes per year. The project grew from a UKRI-funded feasibility study in 2018 into a demonstration plant that produced the first UK-refined lithium hydroxide in October 2025, and was designated a Nationally Significant Infrastructure Project in September 2024, the first mining project to receive that status.

milestones.title: Cornish Lithium founded
milestones.description: Jeremy Wrathall incorporates Cornish Lithium Ltd to investigate lithium extraction from Cornwall's geothermal brines and hard rock geology.

milestones.title: Li4UK Faraday Battery Challenge grant
milestones.description: Cornish Lithium joins a £500,000 Faraday Battery Challenge feasibility consortium with Wardell Armstrong and the Natural History Museum, which subsequently produced lithium carbonate from samples taken at the Trelavour site.

milestones.title: 41-hole drilling programme at Trelavour
milestones.description: A drilling campaign at the Trelavour Downs china clay pit defines an initial JORC-compliant lithium resource and supports a decision to take the project forward as a hard rock development.

milestones.title: £6 million Crowdcube raise
milestones.description: Cornish Lithium raises £6 million from retail investors on Crowdcube, with proceeds supporting work at Trelavour and the United Downs geothermal site.

milestones.title: £18m TechMet investment
milestones.description: TechMet commits up to £18 million through a tranched subscription, providing equity to progress the Trelavour scoping study and brine extraction work.

milestones.title: Trelavour scoping study completed
milestones.description: The scoping study sets out a 20-year, 7,800 tpa lithium hydroxide operation with a post-tax NPV of US$318.6 million and initial capex of US$243.8 million, prompting TechMet to exercise a further £9 million tranche.

milestones.title: £1.8m ATF grant for demonstration plant
milestones.description: Innovate UK awards a £1.8 million Automotive Transformation Fund Scale-up Readiness Validation grant to fund the hydrometallurgical section of a demonstration plant at the TreLith Processing Site.

milestones.title: Tregothnan Estate lease signed
milestones.description: Cornish Lithium signs a lease with the Tregothnan Estate covering the Trelavour Pit and the adjacent TreLith processing site.

milestones.title: US$67m investment led by UK Infrastructure Bank
milestones.description: The UK Infrastructure Bank makes its first direct equity investment (approx. £24m) alongside EMG (approx. £24m) and TechMet (US$7m), as part of a wider funding package of up to US$210m intended to take the project to commercial production.

milestones.title: Section 35 NSIP direction
milestones.description: The Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government issues a Section 35 direction under the Planning Act 2008, designating the Trelavour project as a Nationally Significant Infrastructure Project and requiring consent through a Development Consent Order. It is the first mining project to receive NSIP status.

milestones.title: Demonstration plant inaugurated
milestones.description: The Trelavour demonstration plant, the UK's first lithium hydroxide demonstration facility, is officially opened, using the licensed Lepidico process on granite from the Trelavour Pit.

milestones.title: EIA scoping report submitted to PINS
milestones.description: Cornish Lithium G5 Limited submits the Environmental Impact Assessment scoping report for the DCO application, formally opening the pre-application stage on the PINS register.

milestones.title: Non-statutory community consultation opens
milestones.description: A six-week non-statutory consultation runs from early July to 9 August 2025, presenting the proposed mine, concentrator and hydrometallurgical plant designs to local communities around St Dennis.

milestones.title: 50% resource upgrade announced
milestones.description: An updated JORC-compliant Mineral Resource of 88.5 Mt at 0.21% Li2O (454.5 kt LCE) is announced, a 50% increase on the 2022 figure, feeding into the in-progress feasibility study.

milestones.title: £31m National Wealth Fund commitment
milestones.description: The National Wealth Fund commits up to £31m as part of a £35m equity round, with TechMet adding £4m, to fund completion of the Trelavour feasibility study and continued demonstration plant testing, subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals.

milestones.title: First UK-refined lithium hydroxide produced
milestones.description: The Trelavour demonstration plant produces the first lithium hydroxide monohydrate to be both mined and refined in the UK, following Cornish Lithium's earlier acquisition of the Lepidico process IP.

milestones.title: Targeted start of commercial production
milestones.description: Cornish Lithium targets first commercial production of battery-grade lithium hydroxide at a rate of 10,000 tonnes per year, following an expected two-year construction period after a DCO is granted.

BC0410001.name: East Midlands Gateway Phase 2
BC0410001.summary: Phase 2 is a proposed expansion of the existing SEGRO Logistics Park East Midlands Gateway, a strategic rail freight interchange near East Midlands Airport in Leicestershire. The original Phase 1 received development consent in January 2016 (Roxhill, later acquired by Segro in 2018) and is now operational. Phase 2 received a Section 35 direction in February 2024, a DCO and Material Change Order application were submitted in October 2025, and the joint examination opened on 10 March 2026.

milestones.title: Phase 1 first public consultation by Roxhill
milestones.description: Roxhill Developments held initial public exhibitions in Lockington, Castle Donington and Kegworth on the proposed strategic rail freight interchange. The advertising was later judged inadequate and exhibitions were repeated.

milestones.title: Phase 1 DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Roxhill (Kegworth) Ltd submitted its development consent order application for the East Midlands Gateway Rail Freight Interchange to the Planning Inspectorate.

milestones.title: Phase 1 examination opened
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate's examination of the Phase 1 DCO application began, with hearings running through to July 2015. Local groups including the J24 Action Group raised concerns about landscape impact on Hemington, Lockington and Castle Donington.

milestones.title: Phase 1 DCO granted
milestones.description: The Secretary of State for Transport granted development consent for the East Midlands Gateway Rail Freight Interchange and Highway Order 2016, authorising up to around 6 million sq ft of logistics floorspace and a 50 acre rail terminal.

milestones.title: Segro acquires Roxhill management platform
milestones.description: Segro completed the acquisition of the Roxhill management platform, taking on the East Midlands Gateway site as part of the deal. The original 2016 partnership had given Segro phased option rights to Roxhill's big-box pipeline.

milestones.title: Rail freight terminal opens for service
milestones.description: The first freight train was handled at the on-site Strategic Rail Freight Interchange, operated under a 25-year lease by Maritime Transport. The Rail Minister formally opened the terminal in March 2020.

milestones.title: East Midlands Freeport tax sites go live
milestones.description: Tax sites within the East Midlands Freeport, including land adjacent to the existing SEGRO Logistics Park, became active. The Phase 2 site forms part of the East Midlands Airport and Gateway Industrial Cluster freeport zone.

milestones.title: Section 35 direction for Phase 2
milestones.description: The Secretary of State directed under Section 35 of the Planning Act 2008 that Phase 2 should be treated as a nationally significant infrastructure project, routing the scheme through the DCO regime rather than local planning.

milestones.title: Phase 2 statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: Segro opened a statutory consultation on the Phase 2 proposals, covering around 260 acres of warehousing south of East Midlands Airport with associated highway works and a community park. The consultation ran to 17 March 2025.

milestones.title: Phase 2 additional non-statutory consultation
milestones.description: Segro held a further round of non-statutory consultation on amendments to the emerging proposals before submission. The application package was prepared as a co-joined DCO and Material Change Order to the existing Phase 1 consent.

milestones.title: Phase 2 DCO and MCO applications submitted
milestones.description: Segro submitted the Phase 2 DCO application (BC0410001) and a paired Material Change Order application (TR0510002) amending the Phase 1 consent. The proposals total up to 300,000 sq m of floorspace plus 200,000 sq m of mezzanines.

milestones.title: Examination opens
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate opened the joint examination of the DCO and MCO applications with a preliminary meeting at Hilton East Midlands Airport, Castle Donington, followed by initial compulsory acquisition and issue specific hearings.

milestones.title: Examining Authority's first written questions issued
milestones.description: The Examining Authority issued its first round of written questions (ExQ1) to interested parties, covering compulsory acquisition, transport, environment and the freeport need-case.

milestones.title: Examination scheduled to close
milestones.description: The six-month examination period is scheduled to close, after which the Examining Authority has three months to submit a recommendation to the Secretaries of State for Transport and for Housing, Communities and Local Government.

milestones.title: Expected Secretary of State decision
milestones.description: Based on the statutory timetable, a decision on the DCO and MCO is expected approximately six months after examination closes, allowing three months for the Examining Authority's report and three months for ministerial decision.

WA010002.name: Hampshire Water Transfer and Water Recycling Project
WA010002.summary: Southern Water's proposed Strategic Resource Option for Hampshire, comprising a water recycling plant at Harts Farm Way, Havant, taking treated effluent from Budds Farm Wastewater Treatment Works for advanced purification, transfer into Havant Thicket Reservoir, and a 40km pipeline to Otterbourne Water Supply Works. The scheme is intended to provide up to 90 million litres per day to offset reduced abstraction from the Rivers Test and Itchen under the 2018 Section 20 agreement, and has progressed through Ofwat's RAPID gated funding process toward a Development Consent Order application.

milestones.title: Section 20 agreement on Test and Itchen abstraction
milestones.description: Southern Water and the Environment Agency signed a Water Resources Act 1991 Section 20 operating agreement following a public inquiry, amending abstraction licences on the Rivers Test, Itchen and Candover Brook and setting a 2027 deadline for alternative supply to reduce reliance on drought permits.

milestones.title: RAPID gated process established
milestones.description: Ofwat launched the Regulators' Alliance for Progressing Infrastructure Development to oversee funding for strategic regional water resource solutions. Southern Water entered the Hampshire water recycling option, alongside desalination and a raw water transfer from the planned Havant Thicket Reservoir, into the accelerated gate process.

milestones.title: RAPID Gate One final decision
milestones.description: Ofwat published final accelerated Gate One decisions, allowing the Hampshire water recycling option and the related Havant Thicket raw water transfer to proceed with further development funding alongside other Hampshire-serving Strategic Resource Options.

milestones.title: RAPID Gate Two final decision
milestones.description: Ofwat confirmed the Hampshire Water Transfer and Water Recycling option as the preferred solution to progress to accelerated Gate Three, with continued development funding allowed. Recycling was selected over the earlier desalination alternative.

milestones.title: Section 35 direction designates NSIP
milestones.description: The Secretary of State issued a direction under Section 35 of the Planning Act 2008 confirming the Hampshire Water Transfer and Water Recycling Project as a Nationally Significant Infrastructure Project, bringing it within the Development Consent Order regime.

milestones.title: Inclusion in Southern Water draft WRMP24 and WRSE regional plan
milestones.description: The scheme was included as a core supply-side option in Southern Water's draft Water Resources Management Plan 2024 and the Water Resources South East regional plan, identified as providing approximately half of the projected 192 million litres per day Hampshire drought shortfall by 2050.

milestones.title: Non-statutory public consultation opens
milestones.description: Southern Water opened an eight-week non-statutory consultation on the project design, site at Harts Farm Way, pipeline route to Otterbourne and long sea outfall, running until 23 July 2024. Havant Borough Council, Hampshire and Isle of Wight Wildlife Trust and local groups raised concerns over site impacts, habitats and discharge to the Solent.

milestones.title: EIA Scoping Report submitted
milestones.description: Southern Water submitted its Environmental Impact Assessment Scoping Report to the Planning Inspectorate covering the water recycling plant, transfer pipeline, Havant Thicket interface and long sea outfall via Eastney Pumping Station.

milestones.title: Market engagement for Direct Procurement for Customers
milestones.description: Southern Water issued market engagement notices for the estimated £1.3bn delivery contract under Ofwat's Direct Procurement for Customers financing model, mandated at Price Review 2024.

milestones.title: RAPID Gate Three final decision
milestones.description: Ofwat published the Gate Three final decision, the first scheme in the RAPID programme to reach this stage. Up to £25.25m was allowed for progressing the solution to Gate Four, with further allowances for the period to March 2030 determined through PR24.

milestones.title: Section 46 notification of proposed application
milestones.description: Southern Water served the Planning Inspectorate with notification under Section 46 of the Planning Act 2008, alongside Section 42 consultee letters and a Section 48 notice, formalising the start of statutory pre-application consultation.

milestones.title: Supplementary statutory consultation
milestones.description: Southern Water held a four-week supplementary consultation between 5 March and 4 April 2025 on design refinements, pipeline routing and environmental mitigation following feedback from the 2024 round.

milestones.title: Expected DCO application submission
milestones.description: Southern Water indicates submission of the Development Consent Order application to the Planning Inspectorate in May 2026, with acceptance, examination and Secretary of State decision to follow under the Planning Act 2008 timetable.

milestones.title: Indicative target operational date
milestones.description: Southern Water's current programme anticipates construction starting in 2028 or 2029, with the scheme entering operation in the early 2030s, indicatively around 2034. Originally targeted for 2029 under the Section 20 timetable, the date has been revised as the project moved through the gated process.

WA010003.name: Lincolnshire Reservoir
WA010003.summary: Anglian Water's proposed 55 million cubic metre raw water storage reservoir located approximately 7km southwest of Sleaford between Swaton, Scredington and Helpringham, developed as a Strategic Resource Option through Ofwat's RAPID gated process. The scheme would supply around 166 million litres per day to roughly 500,000 homes, with a Development Consent Order application targeted for late 2028 and operational date around 2040.

milestones.title: Need-case identified in Water Resources East regional plan
milestones.description: Anglian Water's long-term supply-demand forecasts identified a deficit in eastern England, with a new Lincolnshire reservoir included as a Strategic Resource Option (SRO) under Ofwat's Regulators' Alliance for Progressing Infrastructure Development (RAPID) gated funding process.

milestones.title: RAPID Gate 1 submission
milestones.description: Anglian Water, then in partnership with Affinity Water through the South Lincolnshire Water Partnership, submitted the Gate 1 preliminary feasibility assessment to Ofwat. A site had not yet been selected at this stage.

milestones.title: Project announced as preferred strategic resource option
milestones.description: Anglian Water confirmed plans to take forward a new reservoir in south Lincolnshire alongside a second proposed reservoir in the Fens, as part of its draft Water Resources Management Plan 2024.

milestones.title: Preferred site published and Phase 1 non-statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: Anglian Water announced the preferred site located 7km southwest of Sleaford between Swaton, Scredington and Helpringham, following a four-stage site selection process. The first non-statutory consultation launched on 12 October 2022.

milestones.title: RAPID Gate 2 submission
milestones.description: Anglian Water submitted the Gate 2 report setting out site identification, early concept design and a planned 37km treated water transfer pipeline. The submission noted target water-into-supply between 2039 and 2041, subject to PR24 funding decisions.

milestones.title: Ofwat publishes Lincolnshire Reservoir case study
milestones.description: Ofwat issued a case study confirming RAPID's continuing support for the scheme as a strategic resource option, with reservoir capacity given as 55 million cubic metres and projected daily output of up to 166 million litres.

milestones.title: Phase 2 non-statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: Second phase of non-statutory consultation ran from 30 May to 9 August 2024, with in-person events across the region. Proposals indicated displacement of around 50 homes and businesses, drawing concern from parish councils in Swaton, Scredington, Helpringham, Burton and Spanby.

milestones.title: Market engagement notice issued for combined reservoir works
milestones.description: Anglian Water issued a Periodic Indicative Notice on the OJEU/UK Find a Tender service for an estimated combined value of around 5 billion pounds covering main works on the Lincolnshire and Fens reservoirs.

milestones.title: Gate 3 submission postponed
milestones.description: Anglian Water requested to postpone the Gate 3 submission scheduled for late 2024. Ofwat's RAPID issued a response letter confirming receipt of the amendment request and an extended submission timetable.

milestones.title: Project formally designated as a Nationally Significant Infrastructure Project
milestones.description: Environment Secretary Steve Reed designated the Lincolnshire Reservoir and Fens Reservoir as NSIPs, transferring decision-making to the Secretary of State under the Planning Act 2008 regime. The project received reference WA010003.

milestones.title: Phase 3 consultation activity
milestones.description: Further consultation engagement undertaken with affected communities and stakeholders, including a National Farmers' Union member meeting on 25 November 2025 covering Round 3 proposals.

milestones.title: Statutory pre-application consultation expected
milestones.description: Statutory consultation under section 42 and section 47 of the Planning Act 2008 is anticipated between 2026 and 2027 ahead of the Development Consent Order application.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order application expected
milestones.description: Anglian Water expects to submit its DCO application to the Planning Inspectorate in November 2028, with examination, recommendation and Secretary of State decision to follow over approximately 15 months thereafter.

milestones.title: Target operational date
milestones.description: Construction estimated to begin between 2029 and 2031 if consented, with a multi-year filling period. Anglian Water targets water into supply around 2040.

WA010004.name: Fens Reservoir
WA010004.summary: A proposed Strategic Resource Option jointly promoted by Anglian Water and Cambridge Water for a 55 million cubic metre reservoir near Chatteris in the Cambridgeshire Fens, designed to address forecast supply deficits in the East of England and support growth in Greater Cambridge. The scheme is progressing through Ofwat's RAPID gated process with a DCO application targeted for early 2027 and water supply from around 2036.

milestones.title: Gate 1 submission and need confirmed
milestones.description: Anglian Water and Cambridge Water submitted the Gate 1 preliminary feasibility report to Ofwat's RAPID process, establishing the Fens Reservoir as a strategic regional option to address forecast supply deficits in the East of England.

milestones.title: Preferred site near Chatteris announced
milestones.description: The joint applicants confirmed the preferred location for the reservoir within Fenland district, between Chatteris and March, near Doddington, Wimblington and Manea.

milestones.title: Phase one non-statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: First phase of non-statutory community consultation ran from 12 October to 21 December 2022, seeking views on the preferred site and concept features, with events held around Chatteris, Doddington and Wimblington.

milestones.title: Gate 2 submission to RAPID
milestones.description: Anglian Water and Cambridge Water submitted the Gate 2 report to Ofwat's RAPID process, setting out a preferred 55 million cubic metre reservoir with 50 million cubic metres of usable storage.

milestones.title: Ofwat publishes Gate 2 final decision
milestones.description: Ofwat issued the standard Gate 2 final decision allowing the scheme to progress to the next stage of the RAPID gated process, with a conditional review point scheduled for January 2024.

milestones.title: Included in Water Resources East regional plan
milestones.description: Water Resources East published its first 50-year Regional Water Resources Plan for Eastern England in December 2023, identifying the Fens Reservoir as a pivotal low-regret strategic option alongside the South Lincolnshire Reservoir.

milestones.title: Indicative DCO and operational timeline published
milestones.description: Project disclosures set out an estimated £2.2 billion capital cost, a reservoir supplying around 87 to 100 million litres per day, and a target to submit the DCO application in 2025, begin construction in 2029 and bring water into supply between 2035 and 2037.

milestones.title: Phase two non-statutory consultation
milestones.description: Phase two non-statutory consultation ran from 30 May to 9 August 2024, covering the main reservoir site, water sources and supply infrastructure, construction and transport options, environmental approach and renewable energy options.

milestones.title: Government water investment package cites scheme
milestones.description: Defra announced a £7.9 billion water investment package for the East of England identifying the Fens Reservoir as a key project to support sustainable growth in Greater Cambridge and address a forecast regional supply deficit of around 600 million litres per day by 2050.

milestones.title: Phase three consultation opens with revised design
milestones.description: Third phase of non-statutory consultation ran from 15 October to 10 December 2025, presenting updated design proposals for the main reservoir, water source options and associated pipelines.

milestones.title: Gate 3 submission delayed by RAPID
milestones.description: Ofwat's RAPID team wrote to the applicants delaying the scheduled 30 April 2026 Gate 3 submission, citing the need for further work on solution design, water source options and environmental protection. RAPID set a revised deadline rather than granting the longer extension requested by the companies.

milestones.title: DCO application target deadline
milestones.description: Regulators set a deadline of no later than March 2027 for submission of the Development Consent Order application to the Planning Inspectorate, following Gate 3 sign-off and statutory consultation.

milestones.title: Target construction start
milestones.description: Subject to DCO consent, the joint applicants are targeting the start of main construction works at the Chatteris site.

milestones.title: Target water-in-supply date
milestones.description: The reservoir is planned to enter supply around 2036, providing approximately 87 to 100 million litres per day to the Anglian Water and Cambridge Water regions and reducing reliance on chalk stream abstractions.

WA010005.name: White Horse Reservoir (previously known as South East Strategic Reservoir Option)
WA010005.summary: Thames Water has proposed a major reservoir south-west of Abingdon since the 1990s. A 150 million cubic metre Upper Thames Reservoir was rejected after the 2010 public inquiry led by Inspector Wendy Burden, who recommended the scheme be scrapped on grounds of unproven need and inadequate assessment of alternatives. The proposal was revived through the Ofwat RAPID strategic resource option process from 2020, accelerated after the 2022 drought, selected in WRMP24, and renamed White Horse Reservoir for the 2025-26 statutory consultation ahead of a planned DCO application in autumn 2026.

milestones.title: Reservoir concept first proposed
milestones.description: Thames Water first identifies a strategic reservoir south-west of Abingdon as a long-term supply option in early water resource planning work.

milestones.title: Reservoir included in draft WRMP and public consultation
milestones.description: Thames Water sets out a proposal for a 150 million cubic metre Upper Thames Reservoir near Abingdon in its draft Water Resources Management Plan and consults the public.

milestones.title: Environment Agency questions need case
milestones.description: The Environment Agency advises Defra that Thames Water's case for the reservoir is not proven and that demand forecasts and alternatives require further work.

milestones.title: Revised draft WRMP submitted with reservoir as preferred option
milestones.description: Thames Water submits its revised draft Water Resources Management Plan to Defra retaining the Upper Thames Reservoir as the preferred large strategic option, triggering a Defra-ordered public inquiry.

milestones.title: Public inquiry held into Upper Thames Reservoir
milestones.description: A public inquiry led by senior planning inspector Wendy Burden hears evidence from Thames Water, the Environment Agency, CPRE Oxfordshire and the Group Against Reservoir Development (GARD) on the need case and alternatives.

milestones.title: Inspector Burden recommends scheme be scrapped
milestones.description: Inspector Wendy Burden reports to ministers that Thames Water has not justified the 150 million cubic metre reservoir, citing overestimated demand and inadequate appraisal of alternatives. She recommends the proposal be dropped from the WRMP and that smaller options up to 50 million cubic metres be appraised instead. Defra accepts the recommendation.

milestones.title: WRMP14 does not include large Abingdon reservoir
milestones.description: Thames Water's 2014 Water Resources Management Plan, covering 2015 to 2040, relies on demand management, leakage reduction and transfers, and does not select the Abingdon reservoir as a preferred resource option.

milestones.title: Reservoir returns in draft WRMP19
milestones.description: Thames Water reintroduces the Abingdon reservoir, sized at up to 150 million cubic metres, as a strategic option in its draft Water Resources Management Plan 2019, attracting renewed opposition from GARD, CPRE Oxfordshire and Vale of White Horse District Council.

milestones.title: Designated a Strategic Resource Option under Ofwat RAPID
milestones.description: Ofwat's Regulators' Alliance for Progressing Infrastructure Development (RAPID) designates the South East Strategic Reservoir Option (SESRO) as one of a small number of strategic regional water resource solutions to be developed through a gated funding process jointly by Thames Water, Affinity Water and Southern Water.

milestones.title: RAPID Gate 1 decision: SESRO progresses to Gate 2
milestones.description: Following submission of Gate 1 reports in July 2021, RAPID publishes its Gate 1 final decision in January 2022 confirming SESRO should continue receiving development funding and progress to Gate 2.

milestones.title: 2022 drought reinforces strategic supply case
milestones.description: Hot, dry conditions across summer 2022 and into 2023 lead to hosepipe bans across much of southern England and prompt the Environment Agency and Defra to emphasise the need for new strategic supply infrastructure including reservoirs.

milestones.title: RAPID Gate 2 decision: SESRO progresses to Gate 3
milestones.description: RAPID publishes its Gate 2 final decision on 28 June 2023, approving continued development of the now 150 million cubic metre SESRO scheme towards a Development Consent Order application.

milestones.title: Government confirms SESRO will be taken forward as an NSIP
milestones.description: Defra's strategic policy statement and the Secretary of State confirm that SESRO will proceed as a Nationally Significant Infrastructure Project under the Planning Act 2008, to be determined by Development Consent Order rather than through a further public inquiry. Vale of White Horse District Council, Oxfordshire County Council, GARD and CPRE Oxfordshire continue to oppose the scheme and call for a public inquiry.

milestones.title: Non-statutory pre-application consultation
milestones.description: Thames Water runs a non-statutory consultation on the SESRO proposals, gathering local feedback ahead of formal pre-application consultation under the Planning Act 2008.

milestones.title: EIA scoping opinion issued by Planning Inspectorate
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate issues its Environmental Impact Assessment scoping opinion on the SESRO proposals, setting out the issues the eventual DCO Environmental Statement must address.

milestones.title: Judicial review brought by SAFERWaters and CPRE dismissed
milestones.description: The High Court hears a judicial review claim brought by SAFERWaters and CPRE challenging the inclusion of SESRO in the regional plan without a separate public inquiry. The claim is dismissed on 25 July 2025.

milestones.title: Statutory pre-application consultation opens; project renamed White Horse Reservoir
milestones.description: Thames Water launches statutory pre-application consultation under section 47 of the Planning Act 2008, running from 28 October 2025 to 13 January 2026. The project is rebranded from SESRO to White Horse Reservoir. Estimated capital cost is disclosed at between £5.5 billion and £7.5 billion, up from earlier figures around £2.2 billion.

milestones.title: RAPID Gate 3 draft decision: SESRO recommended to pass
milestones.description: RAPID publishes its Gate 3 draft decision on 3 December 2025 proposing that SESRO should pass Gate 3, unlocking around £100 million in further development funding while flagging that the submission did not fully demonstrate best value given the increased cost estimate.

milestones.title: DCO application targeted
milestones.description: Thames Water plans to submit its Development Consent Order application for the White Horse Reservoir to the Planning Inspectorate in November 2026.

milestones.title: Construction targeted to begin
milestones.description: Subject to development consent, Thames Water indicates construction would begin in 2029.

milestones.title: Reservoir target operational date
milestones.description: Thames Water plans for the reservoir to begin operating in 2040, supplying water to customers of Thames Water, Affinity Water and Southern Water.

WA010006.name: Teddington Direct River Abstraction (TDRA)
WA010006.summary: Thames Water Utilities Limited's proposed drought-resilience scheme to abstract up to 75 megalitres per day from the River Thames upstream of Teddington Weir, replaced by recycled effluent from Mogden Sewage Treatment Works. Identified as a Strategic Resource Option through the Ofwat RAPID gated process and Thames Water's WRMP24, the project was directed as an NSIP in December 2023 and is at pre-application stage, with a DCO submission expected in 2026 and a target operational date of 2033.

milestones.title: RAPID Gate 1 submission
milestones.description: Thames Water submits Gate 1 reports for the London Water Recycling solutions, including the Teddington Direct River Abstraction concept, under the Ofwat RAPID gated process for Strategic Resource Options.

milestones.title: RAPID Gate 2 submission
milestones.description: Thames Water submits Gate 2 reports to RAPID, with TDRA progressing as a sub-option within the London Water Recycling scheme.

milestones.title: RAPID Gate 2 final decision
milestones.description: RAPID publishes its final Gate 2 decision allowing the London Water Recycling scheme, with TDRA as the preferred sub-option, to progress to further development.

milestones.title: Included in WRSE revised draft regional plan
milestones.description: Water Resources South East (WRSE) identifies the Teddington Direct River Abstraction as a best-value option in its revised draft regional plan.

milestones.title: Non-statutory public consultation opens
milestones.description: Thames Water opens its first non-statutory public consultation on the TDRA proposals, running until 11 December 2023. Around 2,312 responses are received.

milestones.title: Richmond Council public meeting and opposition
milestones.description: Richmond upon Thames Council hosts a public meeting on the scheme and confirms its opposition, citing water quality, public perception, and impacts on open space at Ham Lands.

milestones.title: Section 35 direction issued
milestones.description: The Secretary of State directs under section 35 of the Planning Act 2008 that the TDRA is to be treated as a Nationally Significant Infrastructure Project requiring a Development Consent Order.

milestones.title: Parliamentary debate on the project
milestones.description: Westminster Hall debate on the Teddington Direct River Abstraction project, with the local MP and others raising concerns over environmental and public health impacts.

milestones.title: Revised design published after consultation feedback
milestones.description: Thames Water publishes a revised concept design, removing the continuous discharge of recycled water upstream of Teddington Weir and routing maintenance flows to an outfall at Isleworth using existing infrastructure.

milestones.title: WRMP24 published with TDRA included
milestones.description: Defra approves Thames Water's Water Resources Management Plan 2024, which includes the Teddington Direct River Abstraction and the South East Strategic Reservoir (later renamed White Horse Reservoir) as core supply-side options.

milestones.title: RAPID Gate 3 submission
milestones.description: Thames Water submits its Gate 3 report for the London Water Recycling scheme with TDRA as the single preferred sub-option, alongside detailed conceptual design drawings.

milestones.title: Statutory pre-application consultation opens
milestones.description: Thames Water serves its section 46 notification on the Planning Inspectorate and opens the statutory pre-application consultation under the Planning Act 2008, running until 26 August 2025.

milestones.title: RAPID Gate 3 approval
milestones.description: RAPID approves the Gate 3 report, with Ofwat allowing £22.6m of further development expenditure for TDRA to progress to Gate 4 as the single preferred solution.

milestones.title: Greater London Authority formal response
milestones.description: The GLA's Development Management team submits a formal response to the statutory consultation, asking the applicant to fully assess environmental impacts and alignment with the London Plan and NPPF.

milestones.title: DCO application submission
milestones.description: Thames Water expects to submit its Development Consent Order application to the Planning Inspectorate, accompanied by the Consultation Report and supporting environmental documentation.

milestones.title: Target operational date
milestones.description: Per WRMP24, the scheme is targeted to be available from 2033 to provide up to 75 megalitres per day of drought-resilience supply for London.

WA0210001.name: The Grand Union Canal Transfer
WA0210001.summary: A joint Strategic Resource Option from Affinity Water, Severn Trent Water and the Canal & River Trust to transfer up to 115 million litres of recycled water per day from Minworth in Birmingham along the Coventry, Oxford and Grand Union canals to a service reservoir near Luton. The scheme has progressed through Ofwat's RAPID gated process since 2020 and is in NSIP pre-application stage, with a DCO submission targeted for June 2027 and operation by 2033.

milestones.title: Identified in PR19 final determination
milestones.description: Ofwat included the Grand Union Canal Transfer among the strategic regional water resource solutions for development through the RAPID gated process, intended to address forecast supply deficits in Affinity Water's south-east area.

milestones.title: RAPID Gate 1 submission
milestones.description: Affinity Water, Severn Trent Water and the Canal & River Trust submitted their Gate 1 reports to Ofwat's RAPID setting out the concept design and outline costs for the transfer.

milestones.title: RAPID Gate 1 final decision
milestones.description: Ofwat issued the Gate 1 final decision allowing the Grand Union Canal Transfer to continue development under the RAPID gated process. Up to 100 Ml/d capacity was endorsed at concept stage.

milestones.title: RAPID Gate 2 submission
milestones.description: Solution owners submitted their standard Gate 2 reports to Ofwat for assessment, covering further concept design, environmental work and procurement strategy.

milestones.title: RAPID Gate 2 final decision
milestones.description: Ofwat published the Gate 2 final decision permitting continued funding to progress design and pre-application work for the transfer and the linked Minworth water recycling SRO.

milestones.title: Phase One non-statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: First non-statutory public consultation ran from 11 September to 25 October 2024 across local authorities along the route including North Warwickshire, Rugby, Daventry, Aylesbury Vale, Milton Keynes, Central Bedfordshire and Luton, seeking views on the broad route corridor and scheme principles.

milestones.title: Section 35 direction confirmed
milestones.description: The Secretary of State indicated approval for treating the Grand Union Canal Transfer as a Nationally Significant Infrastructure Project, bringing it within the DCO regime.

milestones.title: RAPID Gate 3 submission
milestones.description: Affinity Water, Severn Trent and Canal & River Trust submitted their Gate 3 reports to Ofwat covering detailed design, environmental assessment and procurement strategy via the Direct Procurement for Customers route.

milestones.title: EIA Scoping Opinion received
milestones.description: The EIA Scoping Report was submitted to the Planning Inspectorate and a Scoping Opinion was issued, defining the scope and methodology for the Environmental Statement to accompany the DCO application.

milestones.title: RAPID Gate 3 final decision
milestones.description: Ofwat confirmed continued funding for the £510m Grand Union Canal Transfer and £200m Minworth SRO to progress to Gate 4, while imposing Conditional Review Points covering solution maturity, delivery programme gaps and cost uncertainty.

milestones.title: Phase Two statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: Statutory pre-application consultation under section 47 of the Planning Act 2008 opened for seven weeks alongside publication of the Preliminary Environmental Information Report. A section 46 notification to the Planning Inspectorate was issued the same day.

milestones.title: Phase Two consultation closes
milestones.description: The statutory pre-application consultation on the route corridor, abstraction points and treatment site closed for responses. Issues raised in earlier engagement included canal ecology, invasive species transfer risks and abstraction near Leighton Buzzard.

milestones.title: DCO application targeted
milestones.description: Submission of the Development Consent Order application to the Planning Inspectorate is expected in June 2027.

milestones.title: RAPID Gate 4 decision expected
milestones.description: Ofwat's Gate 4 decision point for the GUC and Minworth SROs is scheduled for August 2027, aligning with the PR24 price review period.

milestones.title: Construction start targeted
milestones.description: Subject to development consent and Gate 4 funding, construction of the Minworth treatment upgrades, transfer pipelines and canal interventions is expected to begin in 2030.

milestones.title: Target operational date
milestones.description: The scheme is targeted to be commissioned and delivering up to 115 Ml/d to Affinity Water's Chaul End service reservoir near Luton by 2033.

WA0210003.name: Suffolk Water Recycling Transfer and Storage
WA0210003.summary: Essex and Suffolk Water (Northumbrian Water) proposal for an Advanced Water Recycling Plant near Lowestoft producing up to 11 million litres per day, two service reservoirs near Sibton and Eye, and around 80km of new pipelines. The scheme forms part of Water Resources East's regional plan and Essex and Suffolk Water's WRMP24, and was directed into the NSIP regime by the Secretary of State in September 2025. Phase one non-statutory consultation closed in December 2025, with a DCO application targeted for 2028.

milestones.title: Need case set out in draft WRMP24 Statement of Response
milestones.description: Essex and Suffolk Water published its Statement of Response confirming continued detailed design of the Lowestoft Water Reuse option alongside a North Suffolk Reservoir option as supply-side schemes to address the Hartismere zone deficit, with an adaptive pathway between the two.

milestones.title: Included in Water Resources East regional plan
milestones.description: The Water Resources East regional plan for Eastern England, published in December 2023, identified water recycling and transfer infrastructure in Suffolk among the supply-side options needed to close a projected 800 million litres per day regional shortfall by 2050.

milestones.title: Scheme confirmed in final WRMP24
milestones.description: Essex and Suffolk Water's final Water Resources Management Plan 2024 was published, with the Lowestoft Water Reuse scheme, strategic transfers and new service reservoirs forming the preferred set of supply-side investments for the Essex and Suffolk supply area.

milestones.title: Section 35 direction issued
milestones.description: The Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs issued a Section 35 direction under the Planning Act 2008, treating the Suffolk Water Recycling, Transfer and Storage project as a Nationally Significant Infrastructure Project requiring a Development Consent Order.

milestones.title: Phase one non-statutory consultation launched
milestones.description: Essex and Suffolk Water opened its first public consultation, presenting early-stage proposals for the Advanced Water Recycling Plant near Lowestoft, two service reservoirs at Lodgewood and Eye, and pipeline corridors, with eight drop-in events across north Suffolk.

milestones.title: Phase one consultation closed
milestones.description: The phase one consultation closed, with feedback to inform site selection, route options and environmental assessment. Suffolk County Council and Suffolk Wildlife Trust were among consultees raising concerns about pipeline alignment and habitats designations in north Suffolk.

milestones.title: Preferred route and site announcements
milestones.description: Essex and Suffolk Water plans to publish a preferred pipeline route, preferred Advanced Water Recycling Plant site and preferred service reservoir locations by the end of 2026, ahead of a statutory phase two consultation in 2027.

milestones.title: Statutory pre-application consultation
milestones.description: A further statutory consultation on the refined scheme design, route and environmental assessment is planned for 2027 to meet Planning Act 2008 pre-application requirements.

milestones.title: Target DCO submission
milestones.description: Essex and Suffolk Water aims to submit a Development Consent Order application to the Planning Inspectorate in 2028.

milestones.title: Target completion and Hartismere moratorium lift
milestones.description: The scheme is planned to be operational in 2032 to 2033, at which point the temporary moratorium on new non-domestic connections in the Hartismere Water Resource Zone is anticipated to be lifted.

EN010007.name: Wylfa Newydd Nuclear Power Station
EN010007.summary: Wylfa Newydd was a proposed twin Advanced Boiling Water Reactor station on Anglesey, developed by Horizon Nuclear Power under Hitachi ownership from 2012. Hitachi suspended the project in January 2019 citing funding difficulties, and Horizon withdrew the Development Consent Order application in April 2021. The site has since been acquired by Great British Nuclear in 2024 and is being assessed for a new nuclear project.

milestones.title: Government commits to new nuclear in 2008 White Paper
milestones.description: The UK Government published its White Paper on Nuclear Power, confirming policy support for new nuclear stations and setting the framework for private-sector development at designated sites including Wylfa.

milestones.title: Horizon Nuclear Power formed by RWE and E.ON
milestones.description: German utilities RWE npower and E.ON UK established Horizon Nuclear Power as a joint venture to develop new nuclear capacity in the UK, with Wylfa on Anglesey identified as a primary site.

milestones.title: EN-6 Nuclear National Policy Statement designates Wylfa
milestones.description: Parliament approved the National Policy Statement for Nuclear Power Generation (EN-6), formally listing Wylfa as one of eight sites considered potentially suitable for new nuclear deployment before the end of 2025.

milestones.title: Hitachi acquires Horizon Nuclear Power
milestones.description: Hitachi Ltd completed the purchase of Horizon Nuclear Power from RWE and E.ON for around 696 million pounds, committing to deploy its UK Advanced Boiling Water Reactor (ABWR) technology at Wylfa and Oldbury.

milestones.title: Generic Design Assessment of UK ABWR begins
milestones.description: The Office for Nuclear Regulation and the Environment Agency commenced the Generic Design Assessment of Hitachi-GE's UK ABWR reactor, the technology proposed for Wylfa Newydd.

milestones.title: Stage 1 pre-application consultation
milestones.description: Horizon launched the first stage of public consultation on its initial proposals for Wylfa Newydd, covering site selection, plant design, and broad environmental considerations on Anglesey.

milestones.title: Stage 2 pre-application consultation
milestones.description: Horizon held its second statutory consultation, presenting a more developed design including reactor layout, cooling water infrastructure, and worker accommodation proposals at Land off A5025.

milestones.title: Stage 3 pre-application consultation
milestones.description: The third consultation round refined transport, accommodation, and environmental mitigation proposals, including options for a temporary workers' campsite and park-and-ride facilities.

milestones.title: UK ABWR Generic Design Assessment completed
milestones.description: The Office for Nuclear Regulation and Natural Resources Wales issued the Design Acceptance Confirmation and Statement of Design Acceptability for the UK ABWR, confirming the reactor design was suitable for deployment in Great Britain.

milestones.title: Stage 4 targeted consultation
milestones.description: Horizon held a targeted consultation on specific design changes ahead of submission, focusing on revisions to the power station, off-site infrastructure, and associated developments.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted to Planning Inspectorate
milestones.description: Horizon Nuclear Power submitted its Development Consent Order application for the 2,700 MW twin-reactor Wylfa Newydd station to the Planning Inspectorate under the Planning Act 2008.

milestones.title: DCO application accepted for examination
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate accepted Horizon's application for examination, with relevant representations invited and an Examining Authority appointed.

milestones.title: Hitachi suspends the Wylfa Newydd project
milestones.description: Hitachi announced it was suspending work on Wylfa Newydd after failing to reach a funding agreement with the UK Government, taking a write-down of around 300 billion yen. The examination of the DCO was subsequently paused.

milestones.title: Hitachi formally withdraws from the project
milestones.description: Hitachi confirmed it would not resume the Wylfa Newydd project, citing the deteriorating investment environment and the impact of COVID-19, and began winding down Horizon Nuclear Power.

milestones.title: Examining Authority recommendation issued
milestones.description: The Examining Authority submitted its recommendation report to the Secretary of State for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy following the suspended examination.

milestones.title: DCO application formally withdrawn
milestones.description: Horizon Nuclear Power formally withdrew the Development Consent Order application before the Secretary of State issued a decision, ending the Hitachi-era project.

milestones.title: Great British Nuclear acquires the Wylfa site
milestones.description: The UK Government, through Great British Nuclear, completed the purchase of the Wylfa and Oldbury sites from Hitachi for 160 million pounds, identifying Wylfa as a leading candidate for future gigawatt-scale nuclear deployment.

milestones.title: Bechtel and GBN site assessment work continues
milestones.description: Great British Nuclear progressed site characterisation and engineering studies at Wylfa with delivery partners including Bechtel, ahead of decisions on reactor technology and developer selection.

EN010119.name: North Falls Offshore Wind Farm
EN010119.summary: North Falls is a proposed offshore wind farm of up to 1.2 GW located approximately 24.5 km off the Suffolk coast, developed as a joint venture between SSE Renewables and RWE. The project is an extension of the existing Greater Gabbard zone, secured through a Crown Estate Extension Project Agreement. A Development Consent Order application was accepted by the Planning Inspectorate in 2024 and the project is currently awaiting a decision by the Secretary of State.

milestones.title: Crown Estate Extension Project agreement
milestones.description: The Crown Estate granted SSE and RWE (then innogy) rights to develop extensions to existing offshore wind sites, including Greater Gabbard, under its Extension Project programme.

milestones.title: Project announced as joint venture
milestones.description: SSE Renewables and RWE Renewables confirmed the North Falls extension project as a 50/50 joint venture to develop the Greater Gabbard extension area.

milestones.title: EIA Scoping Report submitted
milestones.description: Developer submitted Environmental Impact Assessment scoping report to the Planning Inspectorate to define the scope of environmental studies.

milestones.title: PINS Scoping Opinion issued
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate issued its Scoping Opinion to the applicant under the EIA Regulations.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation (stage 1) opens
milestones.description: First stage of statutory pre-application consultation with local communities in Suffolk and Essex, including landfall and onshore cable route options.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation (stage 2) opens
milestones.description: Second stage of statutory consultation on refined project design, including substation location near Tendring, Essex.

milestones.title: Targeted consultation
milestones.description: Targeted consultation on further design refinements ahead of DCO submission.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Development Consent Order application submitted to the Planning Inspectorate.

milestones.title: DCO application accepted for examination
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate accepted the application as meeting the standards required for examination.

milestones.title: Examination opens
milestones.description: Preliminary meeting held and six-month examination period commenced, led by the appointed Examining Authority.

milestones.title: Examination closes
milestones.description: Examination concluded; Examining Authority began preparing its recommendation report to the Secretary of State.

milestones.title: ExA recommendation submitted to Secretary of State
milestones.description: Examining Authority submitted its recommendation report to the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero.

milestones.title: Secretary of State decision expected
milestones.description: Statutory deadline for the Secretary of State to issue a decision on the Development Consent Order.

EN010125.name: Dogger Bank South Offshore Wind Farms
EN010125.summary: Two adjacent 1.5 GW offshore wind farms (DBS East and DBS West), totalling 3 GW, located on the southern Dogger Bank approximately 100 km off the Yorkshire coast. Developed by RWE (51%) and Masdar (49%) following award of seabed rights in The Crown Estate's Round 4 leasing process. The DCO application was submitted in June 2024, examined through 2025, and is awaiting a Secretary of State decision rescheduled to 14 May 2026.

milestones.title: Crown Estate launches Round 4 leasing
milestones.description: The Crown Estate opens its fourth offshore wind leasing round, the first new English and Welsh seabed leasing competition in a decade, targeting around 7 GW of new capacity.

milestones.title: RWE named preferred bidder for Round 4 Dogger Bank South sites
milestones.description: The Crown Estate announces RWE as preferred bidder for two adjacent sites on the southern Dogger Bank with combined potential capacity of 3 GW, at a lease option fee of around £82,552 per MW per year.

milestones.title: Habitats Regulations Assessment cleared, lease agreements approved
milestones.description: Following the conclusion of the Habitats Regulations Assessment, The Crown Estate confirms intention to enter Agreements for Lease with RWE for the two Dogger Bank South sites.

milestones.title: EIA Scoping Report submitted to PINS
milestones.description: RWE submits the Environmental Impact Assessment Scoping Report for the Dogger Bank South projects to the Planning Inspectorate.

milestones.title: PINS issues Scoping Opinion
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate issues its Scoping Opinion, setting out the matters to be addressed in the Environmental Statement.

milestones.title: Round 4 lease agreements signed
milestones.description: RWE signs Agreements for Lease with The Crown Estate for the DBS East and DBS West sites, with option fee payments beginning.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: Statutory consultation under Section 42 of the Planning Act 2008 runs from 6 June to 17 July 2023, based on the Preliminary Environmental Information Report. A supplementary statutory consultation follows between 4 August and 15 September 2023.

milestones.title: RWE and Masdar form joint venture
milestones.description: RWE and Masdar announce a partnership in which Masdar will acquire a 49% stake in the projects, with RWE retaining 51% and lead responsibility for development, construction and operation.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: RWE Renewables UK Dogger Bank South (West) Limited and RWE Renewables UK Dogger Bank South (East) Limited submit the Development Consent Order application to the Planning Inspectorate.

milestones.title: Application accepted for examination
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate accepts the DCO application for examination following the 28-day acceptance review.

milestones.title: Examination opens
milestones.description: The Examining Authority opens its examination of the application with the preliminary meeting, beginning the statutory six-month examination period.

milestones.title: Examination closes
milestones.description: The Examining Authority closes the examination, starting the three-month period for its recommendation report to the Secretary of State.

milestones.title: ExA recommendation submitted to Secretary of State
milestones.description: The Examining Authority submits its recommendation report to the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, starting the statutory three-month decision period with an original deadline of 10 January 2026.

milestones.title: Decision deadline extended
milestones.description: The Secretary of State postpones the decision deadline from 10 January 2026 to 30 April 2026, citing the need for further information; the final completion date is later set to 14 May 2026.

milestones.title: Secretary of State decision expected
milestones.description: Revised deadline for the Secretary of State's determination on the Development Consent Order application.

EN010164.name: Xlinks Morocco-UK Power Project
EN010164.summary: Xlinks 1 Limited proposed a 3,800km HVDC subsea interconnector linking a solar, wind and battery generation complex in Guelmim-Oued Noun, Morocco to a converter station and grid connection at Alverdiscott in Devon, intended to deliver up to 3.6 GW into Great Britain. The project sought a Development Consent Order under the Planning Act 2008 and a bespoke Contract for Difference, but the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero declined to progress a price agreement in 2025 and the developer subsequently withdrew the scheme.

milestones.title: Project publicly announced
milestones.description: Xlinks, chaired by former Tesco chief executive Sir Dave Lewis, unveiled plans for a 10.5 GW solar and wind generation site in Morocco connected to Great Britain via subsea HVDC cables to supply around 8 percent of UK electricity demand.

milestones.title: Alverdiscott landfall and converter site identified
milestones.description: Xlinks confirmed Alverdiscott in North Devon as the proposed UK landing point and converter station location, citing proximity to the National Grid 400 kV substation, and began early engagement with Devon County Council and Torridge District Council.

milestones.title: TotalEnergies takes equity stake
milestones.description: TotalEnergies became a strategic investor in Xlinks, joining the project alongside founding shareholders to support development capital for the subsea interconnector and Moroccan generation complex.

milestones.title: Octopus Energy and GE Vernova invest
milestones.description: Octopus Energy committed around 5 million pounds and GE Vernova joined as a strategic investor and prospective HVDC technology supplier, taking total development funding raised by Xlinks past 100 million pounds.

milestones.title: Section 35 direction requested
milestones.description: Xlinks 1 Limited requested a direction from the Secretary of State under section 35 of the Planning Act 2008 to treat the UK elements of the interconnector as a Nationally Significant Infrastructure Project, enabling consent through a Development Consent Order.

milestones.title: Section 35 direction granted
milestones.description: The Department for Energy Security and Net Zero issued a section 35 direction confirming the UK onshore and offshore elements would be examined as an NSIP, with the Planning Inspectorate assigning reference EN010164 and the South West region designation.

milestones.title: EIA scoping report submitted
milestones.description: Xlinks submitted an Environmental Impact Assessment scoping report to the Planning Inspectorate covering the converter station, onshore cable corridor through Devon and the UK territorial waters section of the subsea route.

milestones.title: Statutory pre-application consultation opens
milestones.description: The first round of statutory consultation under sections 42, 47 and 48 of the Planning Act 2008 began, with public events in North Devon covering the proposed converter station at Alverdiscott and onshore cable route options.

milestones.title: House of Lords evidence on price support
milestones.description: Sir Dave Lewis gave evidence to a House of Lords committee setting out the case for a bespoke Contract for Difference with the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero, arguing standard Allocation Round 6 terms were not suited to a cross-border interconnector with embedded generation.

milestones.title: Second statutory consultation
milestones.description: Xlinks held a further round of statutory consultation on refined cable corridor and converter station designs ahead of an intended DCO application submission to the Planning Inspectorate.

milestones.title: Energy Secretary declines national importance designation
milestones.description: Energy Secretary Ed Miliband informed Xlinks that the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero would not designate the scheme a project of national importance or progress a bespoke Contract for Difference, citing value for money and deliverability considerations.

milestones.title: Project withdrawn
milestones.description: Xlinks 1 Limited confirmed it would not proceed with the Development Consent Order application following the government's decision, and the Planning Inspectorate updated the EN010164 project status to Withdrawn.

EN020010.name: Mid Wales Electricity Connection (N Grid)
EN020010.summary: National Grid's proposal for a 400 kV connection from a new substation at Cefn Coch in Powys to Lower Frankton in Shropshire, designed to evacuate generation from Welsh Government TAN 8 wind farm Strategic Search Areas. The scheme attracted sustained opposition from 2011 onwards and was suspended in 2015 after the Department of Energy and Climate Change refused consent for most of the wind farms it was intended to serve. The project never reached formal Development Consent Order application and was eventually withdrawn from the planning system.

milestones.title: Need case established by TAN 8 wind farms
milestones.description: Welsh Government Technical Advice Note 8 Strategic Search Areas in Mid Wales drive a cluster of proposed onshore wind farms, requiring new transmission capacity to connect to the wider network.

milestones.title: Montgomeryshire Against Pylons formed
milestones.description: Around 500 residents attend a protest meeting at Meifod rugby club; campaign group MAP is established to oppose the proposed connection and associated wind farms.

milestones.title: Mass rally at the Senedd
milestones.description: Around 2,000 protesters gather in Cardiff in what organisers describe as the largest demonstration in the National Assembly's history at that point, against the TAN 8 wind farms and pylon connection.

milestones.title: First stage public consultation
milestones.description: National Grid opens public consultation on route corridor and substation site options across Powys and Shropshire.

milestones.title: Preferred substation and route corridor announced
milestones.description: National Grid selects Cefn Coch in Powys as preferred substation site and the 'red north' corridor via Llansantffraid to Lower Frankton in Shropshire as the preferred route.

milestones.title: Conjoined wind farm public inquiry opens
milestones.description: A joint public inquiry into five Mid Wales wind farm schemes, whose output the connection would carry, opens in Welshpool. It runs for around a year, becoming the UK's longest onshore wind inquiry.

milestones.title: Draft route and partial undercabling announced
milestones.description: National Grid publishes a draft 33-mile route and commits to placing around 8 miles underground in the Meifod Valley following consultation feedback.

milestones.title: Conjoined wind farm inquiry closes
milestones.description: The inquiry inspector closes hearings; the report is submitted to the Secretary of State in December 2014.

milestones.title: Application timetable slipped to 2016
milestones.description: National Grid confirms the Development Consent Order application will not be submitted until mid-2016, citing the need to review consultation feedback and await wind farm decisions.

milestones.title: DECC refuses most associated wind farms
milestones.description: The Department of Energy and Climate Change refuses consent for four of the five wind farms considered at the conjoined inquiry, removing the bulk of the generation the connection was designed to serve.

milestones.title: Project suspended
milestones.description: Following the wind farm refusals, National Grid suspends work on the Mid Wales Connection and pauses preparation of a DCO application.

milestones.title: Project withdrawn from planning pipeline
milestones.description: With the need case removed, National Grid does not progress a DCO application; the EN020010 case is later treated as withdrawn by the Planning Inspectorate and removed from active project lists.

EN020022.name: AQUIND Interconnector
EN020022.summary: AQUIND is a proposed 2,000 MW HVDC interconnector between Normandy and Portsmouth, routed onshore to the Lovedean substation. The Secretary of State for BEIS refused development consent in January 2022 against the Examining Authority's recommendation to grant; AQUIND won a judicial review in January 2023 which quashed that refusal, and the application has since been under redetermination, with Ministry of Defence representations and further disclosure procedures continuing into 2025.

milestones.title: Project announced
milestones.description: AQUIND Limited publicly announces a proposed 2,000 MW HVDC link between Normandy and Portsmouth, with an initial target completion of 2021.

milestones.title: Proposals published
milestones.description: Proposed development announced ahead of statutory engagement; non-technical summary released for consultation.

milestones.title: EIA scoping report submitted
milestones.description: EIA scoping report submitted to the Planning Inspectorate; Scoping Opinion issued under the EIA Regulations 2017 in December 2018.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation
milestones.description: Statutory consultation conducted February to April 2019. Portsmouth City Council formally objected in July 2019.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: AQUIND Limited submits its Development Consent Order application to the Planning Inspectorate (PINS reference EN020022).

milestones.title: Examination opens
milestones.description: Examination begins following the Preliminary Meeting on 18 August 2020, with issue-specific, open floor and compulsory acquisition hearings held over the following six months.

milestones.title: Examination closes
milestones.description: Six-month examination concludes. The Examining Authority then prepares its report and recommendation to the Secretary of State.

milestones.title: ExA recommends grant
milestones.description: The Examining Authority submits its report to the Secretary of State for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy, recommending that development consent be granted.

milestones.title: Secretary of State refuses DCO
milestones.description: Business Secretary Kwasi Kwarteng refuses development consent, citing insufficient consideration of alternative onshore cable routes (including an alternative near Mannington, Dorset), against the ExA's recommendation to grant. AQUIND's owners Viktor Fedotov and Alexander Temerko had previously been reported as donors to the Conservative Party; three ministers had earlier recused themselves from the decision.

milestones.title: Permission for judicial review granted
milestones.description: The High Court grants AQUIND Limited permission to bring a judicial review of the Secretary of State's refusal decision.

milestones.title: Judicial review heard
milestones.description: Substantive judicial review hearing takes place before Mrs Justice Lieven in the High Court; judgment reserved.

milestones.title: High Court quashes refusal
milestones.description: In AQUIND Ltd v Secretary of State for BEIS [2023] EWHC 98 (Admin), Mrs Justice Lieven quashes the January 2022 refusal on four grounds, including failure to take account of relevant evidence on alternatives and breach of the Tameside duty. The application is remitted to the Secretary of State for redetermination.

milestones.title: Redetermination begins
milestones.description: The Secretary of State writes to the applicant requesting further information to inform redetermination of the application.

milestones.title: Statement of Matters published
