milestones.description: Department publishes a Statement of Matters for the redetermination and invites further representations from interested parties.

milestones.title: MoD open representations submitted
milestones.description: Ministry of Defence submits open representations on national security considerations, following a process letter issued on 12 July 2024 setting out how MoD evidence would be handled.

milestones.title: Ofgem rejects cap and floor bid
milestones.description: Ofgem declines to award AQUIND a cap and floor regulatory regime in its third application window, concluding the project did not demonstrate sufficient consumer benefit.

milestones.title: Applicant's open representations published
milestones.description: AQUIND's open representations on the MoD evidence are published; the Attorney General had been appointed to make closed submissions on behalf of interested parties in December 2024.

milestones.title: Further disclosure letter
milestones.description: The Department issues a further disclosure letter, with redetermination continuing under the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero. No redetermination decision has been issued as of the research date.

EN070009.name: H2Teesside
EN070009.summary: H2Teesside was a proposed 1.2 GW thermal blue hydrogen production facility at the Wilton International site on Teesside, led by bp and intended to feed the East Coast Cluster's carbon storage network. The DCO application was submitted in March 2024 and examined through to February 2025, but bp withdrew the application on 1 December 2025, citing competing development of the site for a data centre and weaker than expected industrial hydrogen demand in Teesside.

milestones.title: Project announced by bp
milestones.description: bp announced plans for H2Teesside, an initial 1 GW blue hydrogen production facility at Teesside, presented as the UK's largest hydrogen project and a contribution to the government's 5 GW 2030 hydrogen production ambition.

milestones.title: East Coast Cluster selected as Track-1 CCUS cluster
milestones.description: The East Coast Cluster, which would transport and store CO2 from H2Teesside, was selected by BEIS as one of two Track-1 clusters in Phase 1 of the cluster sequencing process, alongside HyNet North West.

milestones.title: Shortlisted in Phase 2 cluster sequencing
milestones.description: H2Teesside was included on the shortlist of power CCUS, industrial carbon capture and CCUS-enabled hydrogen projects announced by BEIS for negotiation under Phase 2 of the Track-1 cluster sequencing process.

milestones.title: Section 35 direction issued
milestones.description: The Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero directed under sections 35(1) and 35ZA of the Planning Act 2008 that H2Teesside be treated as a Nationally Significant Infrastructure Project requiring development consent.

milestones.title: Confirmed on Track-1 project negotiation list
milestones.description: DESNZ confirmed H2Teesside as one of three East Coast Cluster projects, alongside Net Zero Teesside Power and Teesside Hydrogen CO2 Capture, taken forward into Track-1 negotiations for first connection to the cluster from 2027.

milestones.title: Statutory pre-application consultation opens
milestones.description: bp launched the statutory pre-application consultation on the H2Teesside proposals under section 42 of the Planning Act 2008, publishing a Preliminary Environmental Information Report. The consultation closed on 26 October 2023.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: H2 Teesside Limited submitted its application for a Development Consent Order to the Planning Inspectorate. The proposal covered a CCS-enabled hydrogen production facility of up to 1.2 GWth with associated pipelines and connections.

milestones.title: Application accepted for examination
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate accepted the H2Teesside DCO application for examination under reference EN070009.

milestones.title: Examination opens
milestones.description: A panel of planning inspectors, sitting as the Examining Authority, opened the six-month examination of the H2Teesside DCO application.

milestones.title: Non-statutory consultation on application changes
milestones.description: bp opened a non-statutory consultation on proposed changes to the application reflecting further engineering and design development and a reduction in the Order Limits from around 507 to 407 hectares. The consultation closed on 7 October 2024.

milestones.title: Examination closes
milestones.description: The Examining Authority concluded its examination of the application and began preparing its recommendation to the Secretary of State.

milestones.title: Secretary of State decision deferred
milestones.description: The Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero extended the statutory deadline for the decision on the H2Teesside DCO, deferring the decision into December 2025.

milestones.title: Application withdrawn
milestones.description: H2 Teesside Limited wrote to the Secretary of State formally withdrawing the DCO application. bp cited material and significant changes in the development of the wider Teesworks site, including plans for a large data centre on overlapping land, together with weaker industrial hydrogen demand in Teesside as some prospective offtakers scaled back or delayed decarbonisation plans. bp said it would continue with Net Zero Teesside Power and the Northern Endurance Partnership.

EN020026.name: Sea Link
EN020026.summary: Sea Link is a proposed 2 GW, around 138 km HVDC subsea reinforcement between Suffolk (Friston) and Kent (Pegwell Bay) being developed by National Grid Electricity Transmission as part of the Great Grid Upgrade. The Development Consent Order application was accepted by the Planning Inspectorate in April 2025 and the project is at the examination stage, with strong local opposition in Suffolk and Kent on landscape, ecological and need-case grounds.

milestones.title: Need identified in Network Options Assessment 2020/21
milestones.description: National Grid ESO's Network Options Assessment 2020/21 identified a reinforcement option between Suffolk and Kent, providing the regulatory needs case for what later became Sea Link.

milestones.title: Non-statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: National Grid launched its first non-statutory consultation on proposals for a new HVDC link between Suffolk and Kent, running until 18 December 2022.

milestones.title: Non-statutory consultation closes
milestones.description: First round of public feedback closed. Significant opposition was raised in Friston, Saxmundham, Sandwich and at RSPB Pegwell Bay regarding converter station siting and the cable landfall.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: National Grid opened formal statutory consultation under section 42 of the Planning Act 2008, presenting refined onshore and offshore proposals including a converter station near Saxmundham and a Kent landfall at Pegwell Bay.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation closes
milestones.description: Statutory consultation closed at 23:59 on 18 December 2023. Responses included formal objections from Suffolk Wildlife Trust, Kent Wildlife Trust and RSPB on impacts to Minster Marshes and Pegwell Bay nature reserve.

milestones.title: Further targeted consultation opens
milestones.description: National Grid launched a targeted further consultation on design changes made in response to statutory consultation feedback, running until 11 August 2024.

milestones.title: Further targeted consultation closes
milestones.description: Further consultation period ended. Local groups including Suffolk Energy Action Solutions argued the changes did not materially address concerns over the converter station or Pegwell Bay cable corridor.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted to PINS
milestones.description: National Grid submitted its Development Consent Order application for Sea Link to the Planning Inspectorate, reference EN020026.

milestones.title: Application accepted for examination
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate accepted the Sea Link DCO application for examination, confirming it met the standards required under section 55 of the Planning Act 2008.

milestones.title: Preliminary meeting and examination opens
milestones.description: The Examining Authority held the preliminary meeting and formally opened the six-month examination. Open Floor Hearings ran on 5-6 and 11-12 November 2025, with Suffolk County Council lodging a formal objection.

milestones.title: Applicant submits Change Request
milestones.description: National Grid submitted a Change Request to incorporate five design changes into the proposed development following targeted consultation between 7 October and 7 November 2025.

milestones.title: Deadline 7 submissions
milestones.description: Final deadline for written representations on the Examining Authority's schedule of changes to the draft DCO.

milestones.title: Examination closes
milestones.description: The six-month examination period concluded. The Examining Authority began drafting its recommendation report to the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero.

milestones.title: Recommendation report due to Secretary of State
milestones.description: Statutory deadline for the Examining Authority to submit its recommendation to the Secretary of State, who then has three months to issue a decision on consent.

EN020027.name: Norwich to Tilbury
EN020027.summary: National Grid Electricity Transmission's proposed 184 km, 400 kV overhead line and pylon route from Norwich Main substation via Bramford to a new substation near Tilbury, with associated substations and limited undergrounding. The Development Consent Order application was submitted in August 2025 and accepted for examination in September 2025; examination opened in February 2026. The route has drawn sustained cross-party opposition from East Anglian MPs and local authorities advocating for an offshore or undersea alternative.

milestones.title: Project identified in 2021 Network Options Assessment
milestones.description: National Grid Electricity System Operator's 2021 Network Options Assessment identified the need for a major 400 kV reinforcement between East Anglia and the Thames Estuary, forming the basis for what would become the East Anglia GREEN project.

milestones.title: First non-statutory consultation launched as East Anglia GREEN
milestones.description: National Grid opened an eight-week non-statutory consultation on its East Anglia Green Energy Enablement proposals, running from 21 April to 16 June 2022. This was the first public presentation of the approximately 180 km overhead line route between Norwich, Bramford and Tilbury.

milestones.title: Holistic Network Design published
milestones.description: National Grid ESO published its Holistic Network Design under the Offshore Transmission Network Review, confirming the Norwich to Tilbury reinforcement as a critical onshore route while committing five East Anglia projects to explore voluntary coordination with offshore connections.

milestones.title: Government commissions ESO review of offshore alternatives
milestones.description: Following sustained campaigning by East Anglian MPs and local groups including Pylons East Anglia, the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero asked National Grid ESO to review offshore coordination options for East Anglia, including alternatives to the proposed onshore route.

milestones.title: Second non-statutory consultation on preferred draft alignment
milestones.description: National Grid ran a second non-statutory consultation from 27 June to 21 August 2023, presenting a preferred draft alignment for the route through Norfolk, Suffolk and Essex and seeking feedback on detailed design.

milestones.title: Project renamed Norwich to Tilbury
milestones.description: National Grid rebranded the East Anglia GREEN project as Norwich to Tilbury in summer 2023, aligning the project name with the route endpoints ahead of statutory consultation.

milestones.title: DESNZ confirms outcome of offshore coordination review
milestones.description: The Department for Energy Security and Net Zero confirmed the outcome of the first exploratory stage of offshore coordination, proposing to connect North Falls and Five Estuaries offshore wind farms into the Sea Link HVDC project rather than expanding offshore alternatives to the Norwich to Tilbury onshore route.

milestones.title: ESO East Anglia Network Study published
milestones.description: National Grid ESO published its East Anglia Network Study, assessing onshore and offshore options for the region. The study supported the onshore Norwich to Tilbury route as the preferred reinforcement, with ESO stating offshore alternatives would be several times more expensive.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: National Grid launched the project's first statutory consultation under section 47 of the Planning Act 2008, initially scheduled to run from 10 April to 18 June 2024. The deadline was later extended to 26 July 2024 following the announcement of the 4 July general election.

milestones.title: Cross-party MP opposition and council objections
milestones.description: Suffolk County Council formally objected to the proposals and called for a pause. East Anglian MPs including James Cartlidge, Bernard Jenkin, Therese Coffey, Sandy Martin, Liz Truss and others campaigned for an offshore or undersea alternative, supported by Pylons East Anglia, Save Our Suffolk Skyline and CPRE Norfolk.

milestones.title: Supplementary targeted consultation
milestones.description: National Grid held a supplementary targeted consultation between January and April 2025 on localised design refinements ahead of submitting its Development Consent Order application.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order application submitted
milestones.description: National Grid Electricity Transmission submitted its Development Consent Order application to the Planning Inspectorate, comprising 362 documents and over 60,000 pages covering the 184 km route across Norfolk, Suffolk and Essex.

milestones.title: Application accepted for examination
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate accepted the Norwich to Tilbury application for examination under section 55 of the Planning Act 2008, opening the pre-examination registration period for interested parties.

milestones.title: Examination opens with preliminary meeting in Ipswich
milestones.description: The Examining Authority held the preliminary meeting in Ipswich and online to set the procedure and timetable for the six-month examination, which is scheduled to close on 10 August 2026. Issue-specific and compulsory acquisition hearings began the following week.

milestones.title: Examination scheduled to close
milestones.description: The six-month examination is scheduled to close on 10 August 2026, after which the Examining Authority has three months to submit its recommendation to the Secretary of State.

milestones.title: Secretary of State decision due
milestones.description: Under the Planning Act 2008 statutory timetable, the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero is expected to issue a decision on the Development Consent Order within three months of receiving the Examining Authority's recommendation.

EN020032.name: Morgan and Morecambe Offshore Wind Farms Transmission Assets
EN020032.summary: Joint transmission infrastructure DCO for the Morgan (bp/EnBW) and Morecambe (Cobra/Flotation Energy) offshore wind farms in the Irish Sea, comprising offshore export cables, a Fylde coast landfall, onshore cable corridor, and a new onshore substation connecting to the National Grid Penwortham substation in Lancashire. The application was accepted in November 2024, examined through 2025, and is currently at the Secretary of State decision stage with a statutory deadline of 14 May 2026.

milestones.title: Section 35 direction issued
milestones.description: Secretary of State for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy issued a direction under section 35 of the Planning Act 2008 confirming the transmission assets project should be treated as a Nationally Significant Infrastructure Project requiring development consent.

milestones.title: Scoping opinion issued
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate, on behalf of the Secretary of State, issued its scoping opinion setting out the matters to be addressed in the Environmental Statement accompanying the DCO application.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation announced
milestones.description: Applicants Morgan Offshore Wind Limited and Morecambe Offshore Wind Limited announced plans for statutory consultation on substation siting, offshore and onshore cable routing, and preliminary environmental information.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: Section 42 statutory consultation launched, running until 23 November 2023, covering four indicative onshore substation search areas around Kirkham, Newton-with-Scales, Longton, and Penwortham, together with the Preliminary Environmental Information Report.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate received the development consent order application for the Morgan and Morecambe transmission assets, covering offshore export cables, Fylde coast landfall, onshore cable corridor, and onshore substation works connecting to Penwortham.

milestones.title: Application accepted for examination
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate accepted the application for examination following the 28-day acceptance check.

milestones.title: Registration period opens
milestones.description: Registration opened for interested parties to make relevant representations on the application, closing on 27 January 2025.

milestones.title: Examination opens
milestones.description: Preliminary meeting and first open floor hearing held, formally opening the six-month examination period. Subsequent issue-specific hearings, compulsory acquisition hearings, and accompanied site inspections ran through spring, summer, and autumn 2025.

milestones.title: Examination closes
milestones.description: The Examining Authority closed the examination, with three months allotted to prepare the recommendation report to the Secretary of State.

milestones.title: Recommendation submitted to Secretary of State
milestones.description: The Examining Authority submitted its recommendation report to the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, starting the three-month decision period.

milestones.title: Secretary of State consultation letter
milestones.description: The Secretary of State issued a consultation letter on post-examination submissions received between 29 October 2025 and 29 January 2026, and on 26 March 2026 reset the statutory decision deadline to 14 May 2026.

milestones.title: Secretary of State decision deadline
milestones.description: Statutory deadline for the Secretary of State to issue the decision on the development consent order, following the reset announced in March 2026.

EN0110001.name: The Keadby Next Generation Power Station Project
EN0110001.summary: Keadby Next Generation is a proposed combined cycle gas turbine power station of up to 910 MW at SSE Thermal and Equinor's Keadby site in North Lincolnshire, designed to be capable of running on 100% hydrogen, natural gas, or a blend. Unlike the sister Keadby 3 project at the same site (which received a DCO in 2022 and is the carbon-capture-equipped station tied to the East Coast Cluster), Keadby Next Generation focuses on hydrogen readiness rather than post-combustion CCS, and is currently at the Examination stage following acceptance in September 2025.

milestones.title: East Coast Cluster awarded Track-1 status
milestones.description: BEIS confirmed the East Coast Cluster as one of the UK's two Track-1 CCUS clusters, providing the policy backdrop for SSE Thermal and Equinor's Humber low-carbon power proposals at Keadby, including the later hydrogen-enabled Next Generation project.

milestones.title: Project publicly announced
milestones.description: SSE and Equinor publicly launched the Keadby Next Generation proposal, describing a 900 MW hydrogen-capable CCGT alongside the existing Keadby complex, and signalling that environmental scoping would begin in April 2024.

milestones.title: EIA scoping commenced
milestones.description: The applicant entered the Environmental Impact Assessment scoping phase, submitting a scoping request to the Planning Inspectorate to define the matters to be assessed in the Environmental Statement.

milestones.title: Statement of Community Consultation published
milestones.description: Keadby Next Generation Limited issued its Statement of Community Consultation setting out how the statutory pre-application consultation under Sections 42, 47 and 48 of the Planning Act 2008 would be conducted.

milestones.title: Statutory pre-application consultation opens
milestones.description: A six-week statutory consultation under Sections 42, 47 and 48 ran from 9 January to 20 February 2025, gathering views from local authorities, statutory consultees, landowners and the public on the proposed scheme.

milestones.title: Targeted consultation on red-line boundary refinements
milestones.description: Following the statutory consultation, the applicant ran a focused consultation on refinements to the project's red-line boundary affecting a limited group of consultees.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted to PINS
milestones.description: Keadby Next Generation Limited submitted its application for a Development Consent Order to the Planning Inspectorate for the up to 910 MW hydrogen-capable CCGT, including grid connection, gas and hydrogen pipelines, water supply and ancillary works.

milestones.title: Application accepted for examination
milestones.description: PINS issued its acceptance decision, finding the application of a satisfactory standard under Section 55 of the Planning Act 2008. This is the first hydrogen-capable power station application to be accepted for DCO examination in the UK.

milestones.title: Preliminary Meeting
milestones.description: The Examining Authority held the Preliminary Meeting virtually via Microsoft Teams, setting the examination timetable and procedural rules for the six-month statutory period.

milestones.title: Issue Specific Hearing 1
milestones.description: ISH1 covered environmental matters and the draft Development Consent Order, marking the formal start of the examination's oral hearings.

milestones.title: Compulsory Acquisition Hearing 1
milestones.description: The first Compulsory Acquisition Hearing examined the case for the proposed compulsory acquisition powers sought by the applicant over land and rights.

milestones.title: Close of Examination
milestones.description: The statutory six-month examination period is expected to close around late July 2026, after which the Examining Authority moves into the recommendation phase.

milestones.title: Examining Authority's recommendation to Secretary of State
milestones.description: Within three months of the close of examination, the Examining Authority is expected to submit its recommendation report to the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero.

milestones.title: Secretary of State decision expected
milestones.description: The Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero is expected to issue the Development Consent Order decision within three months of receiving the recommendation, subject to any extension. Operations are targeted from 2030.

EN010166.name: Connah's Quay Low Carbon Power Project
EN010166.summary: Uniper's proposed replacement of the existing 1,380 MW Connah's Quay CCGT in Flintshire with a new low-carbon CCGT of up to 1,380 MW fitted with post-combustion carbon capture, connecting to the HyNet North West CO2 transport and storage cluster. The Development Consent Order application was accepted by the Planning Inspectorate in August 2025 and examination opened in January 2026.

milestones.title: EIA Scoping Opinion requested
milestones.description: Uniper submitted a Scoping Report to the Planning Inspectorate for an EIA Scoping Opinion on the proposed low-carbon CCGT with carbon capture at Connah's Quay.

milestones.title: Initial non-statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: Uniper launched its initial public engagement programme on the CQLCP proposals, running from 26 February to 25 March 2024, with events at Connah's Quay Cricket Club, Flint Town Hall and Coleg Cambria Deeside.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: Uniper began its statutory section 42/47/48 pre-application consultation on the CQLCP proposals, running from 8 October to 19 November 2024.

milestones.title: Technip Energies awarded FEED contract
milestones.description: Uniper selected Technip Energies, supported by Balfour Beatty and GE Vernova, for Front-End Engineering Design of the CCGT with carbon capture, using the Canopy by T.EN solution powered by Shell CANSOLV.

milestones.title: Supplementary consultation on design changes
milestones.description: Uniper ran a targeted non-statutory consultation from 8 May to 6 June 2025 on design revisions including increasing absorber and HRSG stack heights to 150 metres.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted; selected for CCUS Track-1 expansion
milestones.description: Uniper submitted its Development Consent Order application to the Planning Inspectorate, and on the same day UK Government included CQLCP on the Project Negotiation List for the CCUS Cluster Sequencing programme.

milestones.title: Application accepted for examination
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate accepted the CQLCP DCO application for examination following its statutory 28-day acceptance review.

milestones.title: Examination commences
milestones.description: The Examining Authority opened the six-month examination of the DCO application, following the preliminary meeting and Rule 8 timetable issued in pre-examination.

milestones.title: Change application accepted
milestones.description: The Examining Authority accepted a formal change application from Uniper into the examination, requiring procedural amendments to the timetable.

milestones.title: Rule 8(3) procedural decision issued
milestones.description: The Examining Authority issued a Rule 8(3) procedural decision letter modifying the examination timetable; hearings provisionally scheduled for the week commencing 4 May 2026 were cancelled.

milestones.title: Examination closes (statutory deadline)
milestones.description: Statutory six-month limit for the examination of EN010166 following its 13 January 2026 opening.

milestones.title: Examining Authority report due to Secretary of State
milestones.description: Statutory three-month window for the Examining Authority to submit its recommendation report to the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero.

milestones.title: Secretary of State decision due
milestones.description: Statutory three-month window for the Secretary of State to issue a decision on the DCO, after which CQLCP would proceed toward FID under CCUS Track-1, targeting commercial operation of phase one before end-2030.

EN010147.name: Botley West Solar Farm
EN010147.summary: Botley West is a proposed ~840 MW (1.4 GW peak) solar farm across roughly 2,150 hectares on three land parcels in Cherwell, West Oxfordshire and Vale of White Horse, which if consented would be the largest solar site in the UK. Local opposition has been organised through the Stop Botley West campaign, and the then-Conservative MPs covering the affected constituencies, Robert Courts (Witney), Victoria Prentis (Banbury) and David Johnston (Wantage), publicly opposed the scheme during the pre-application phase.

milestones.title: Project announced and first non-statutory consultation
milestones.description: Photovolt Development Partners (PVDP) launched the first phase of public consultation for the proposed 840 MW Botley West Solar Farm, with the initial consultation running until 15 December 2022.

milestones.title: EIA Scoping Report submitted to Planning Inspectorate
milestones.description: PVDP, on behalf of SolarFive Ltd, submitted the Environmental Impact Assessment scoping request to the Planning Inspectorate, formally beginning the NSIP pre-application process.

milestones.title: Statutory pre-application (Section 42) consultation opened
milestones.description: PVDP opened the statutory pre-application consultation under section 42 of the Planning Act 2008, running until 8 February 2024 and covering prescribed bodies, affected landowners and the local community.

milestones.title: Commons adjournment debate on Botley West
milestones.description: Witney MP Robert Courts secured a House of Commons debate setting out local objections to the scale of the proposal and concerns about agricultural land, green belt and amenity impacts.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order application submitted
milestones.description: PVDP submitted the DCO application for Botley West Solar Farm to the Planning Inspectorate, seeking consent for up to 840 MW of solar generation with integrated battery storage across three parcels in Oxfordshire.

milestones.title: Application accepted for examination
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate accepted the application for examination under the Planning Act 2008 following the 28-day acceptance review.

milestones.title: Examining Authority appointed
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate appointed the panel of Examining Authority members to conduct the examination of the application.

milestones.title: Preliminary Meeting held and examination opened
milestones.description: The Preliminary Meeting was held on 13 May 2025, formally opening the six-month examination period and setting the timetable of eight deadlines through to 13 November 2025.

milestones.title: Historic England position published
milestones.description: Historic England confirmed it did not consider the proposal would cause harm to Blenheim Palace World Heritage Site, while ICOMOS submitted a separate technical review advising the proposal would have an adverse impact on the property's Outstanding Universal Value.

milestones.title: Final round of issue-specific and open-floor hearings
milestones.description: The Examining Authority held the final week of hearings, with written summaries of oral submissions due at Deadline 6 on 20 October 2025.

milestones.title: Examination closed
milestones.description: The six-month examination of the application closed, ending the period for written and oral evidence and starting the three-month period for the Examining Authority to prepare its recommendation report.

milestones.title: Recommendation submitted to Secretary of State
milestones.description: The Examining Authority submitted its recommendation report on the application to the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, Ed Miliband, starting the statutory three-month decision period.

milestones.title: Decision deadline reset to 10 September 2026
milestones.description: In a written ministerial statement, the Secretary of State reset the statutory decision deadline from 10 May 2026 to 10 September 2026 to allow DESNZ to seek further information from the applicant and for interested parties to comment on it.

milestones.title: Secretary of State clarification letter issued
milestones.description: DESNZ issued a clarification letter seeking further information from the applicant and interested parties, with a response deadline of 23:59 on 9 June 2026.

milestones.title: Statutory deadline for Secretary of State decision
milestones.description: Revised statutory deadline by which the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero must issue a decision on whether to grant the Development Consent Order.

EN010150.name: Lighthouse Green Fuels Project
EN010150.summary: Lighthouse Green Fuels was a proposed sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) facility at Port Clarence, Stockton-on-Tees, intended to convert municipal solid waste-derived feedstock into around 100 million litres of SAF per year. Developed by LGF Projects Limited (a wholly owned subsidiary of Saudi conglomerate Alfanar) and supported by approximately GBP 13.4 million from the UK Advanced Fuels Fund, the DCO application (EN010150) was formally withdrawn in 2025 after the developer concluded that a larger neighbouring site at Seal Sands and a switch from municipal solid waste to sustainably sourced biomass feedstock required a new application (now progressing as EN0110025).

milestones.title: First Advanced Fuels Fund award
milestones.description: Alfanar's Lighthouse Green Fuels project receives GBP 2,417,500 from the UK Government's Advanced Fuels Fund (Department for Transport) to support development of a waste-to-SAF facility on Teesside.

milestones.title: Section 35 direction request
milestones.description: Lighthouse Green Fuels submits a request to the Secretary of State to direct that the proposed SAF facility be treated as a Nationally Significant Infrastructure Project under section 35 of the Planning Act 2008.

milestones.title: Second Advanced Fuels Fund award
milestones.description: The project receives a further GBP 11,001,000 from the Advanced Fuels Fund, bringing total AFF support to approximately GBP 13.4 million for the waste-to-SAF Fischer-Tropsch facility.

milestones.title: EIA Scoping Report submitted
milestones.description: Lighthouse Green Fuels submits its Environmental Impact Assessment Scoping Report to the Planning Inspectorate for the proposed waste-to-SAF facility at Huntsman Drive, Port Clarence, Stockton-on-Tees.

milestones.title: Scoping Opinion issued
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate issues its Scoping Opinion on the proposed Lighthouse Green Fuels Project, setting the scope of environmental information required for the DCO application.

milestones.title: Section 35 direction varied
milestones.description: DESNZ issues a varied section 35 direction reflecting refinements to the project description and red-line boundary at Port Clarence.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: Lighthouse Green Fuels launches statutory pre-application consultation on its proposed SAF plant at Port Clarence, including publication of the Preliminary Environmental Information Report (PEIR). Consultation runs from May to June 2024.

milestones.title: EN010150 DCO application discontinued
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate records EN010150 as DISCONTINUED. LGF Projects confirms the original Port Clarence application has been formally withdrawn following a decision to relocate the facility approximately 500 metres north-east to the former INEOS Nitriles site at Seal Sands and to switch feedstock from municipal solid waste to sustainably sourced biomass.

milestones.title: New Section 35 request submitted
milestones.description: LGF Projects Limited submits a fresh section 35 request to the Secretary of State for the relocated, larger Seal Sands scheme (approximately 175-180 million litres/year of SAF), which is treated as a new NSIP project under reference EN0110025.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation on successor project
milestones.description: A new six-week statutory consultation on the relocated Seal Sands proposal runs from 2 December 2025 to 13 January 2026, progressed under successor reference EN0110025 rather than the discontinued EN010150.

EN010153.name: Frodsham Solar
EN010153.summary: Frodsham Solar Ltd's proposed solar farm on Frodsham Marsh, approximately 500m north of Frodsham in Cheshire West and Chester. The DCO application was accepted in June 2025, examined between October 2025 and April 2026, and is now at the recommendation stage with the Examining Authority's report due to the Secretary of State by 30 July 2026.

milestones.title: EIA scoping report submitted
milestones.description: Applicant submitted EIA Scoping Report to the Secretary of State, formally initiating the pre-application process.

milestones.title: Scoping opinion issued
milestones.description: Planning Inspectorate issued its Scoping Opinion under the 2017 EIA Regulations, setting the scope of environmental assessment.

milestones.title: Section 46 pre-application notification
milestones.description: Applicant's notification under s46 of the Planning Act 2008 and Regulation 8 of the EIA Regulations was received and acknowledged, signalling imminent statutory consultation completion.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Frodsham Solar Ltd submitted the Development Consent Order application to the Planning Inspectorate.

milestones.title: Application accepted for examination
milestones.description: Planning Inspectorate issued its decision to accept the application for examination; application documents published.

milestones.title: Registration to have your say opens
milestones.description: Pre-examination registration window for interested parties opened; deadline to register was 28 August 2025.

milestones.title: Rule 6 letter and preliminary meeting notified
milestones.description: Examining Authority issued the Rule 6 letter with draft examination timetable and published the Initial Assessment of Principal Issues, ahead of a December start.

milestones.title: Examination opens (Rule 8 letter)
milestones.description: Examination timetable issued via the Rule 8 letter following the preliminary meeting; Examining Authority's first written questions (ExQ1) published, with response deadline 19 January 2026.

milestones.title: Compulsory Acquisition and Issue Specific Hearings
milestones.description: Examining Authority held Compulsory Acquisition Hearing 1 and Issue Specific Hearing 2 between 24 and 26 February 2026.

milestones.title: Examination period closes
milestones.description: Deadline 7 submissions published and the six-month examination period formally closed; project moves to the recommendation stage.

milestones.title: Recommendation due to Secretary of State
milestones.description: Examining Authority's recommendation report due to the Secretary of State by this date, after which the three-month decision period begins.

EN010141.name: East Park Energy
EN010141.summary: East Park Energy is a proposed 400 MW solar photovoltaic generating station with 100 MW battery energy storage on land north-west of St Neots between Hail Weston and Swineshead, spanning Bedfordshire, Cambridgeshire and Huntingdonshire, with grid connection at Eaton Socon substation. The applicant, BSSL Cambsbed 1 Limited (formerly RNA CAMBSBED1 Ltd), submitted its DCO application on 3 October 2025; it was accepted on 30 October 2025 and is now in examination, which opened on 17 March 2026.

milestones.title: EIA Scoping Opinion adopted
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate issued its Scoping Opinion on the proposed East Park Energy DCO, setting environmental assessment requirements.

milestones.title: Non-statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: Applicant ran an informal public consultation from 17 October to 21 November 2023, receiving 372 responses on the initial proposals.

milestones.title: Statutory (Section 42) consultation opens
milestones.description: Statutory consultation under sections 42, 44, 46 and 48 of the Planning Act 2008 ran from 24 September to 29 October 2024 on revised proposals, drawing 916 responses.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: BSSL Cambsbed 1 Limited submitted the Development Consent Order application to the Planning Inspectorate.

milestones.title: Application accepted for examination
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate accepted the DCO application for examination, moving the project into the pre-examination stage.

milestones.title: Examination opens with preliminary meeting
milestones.description: Examination commenced on 17 March 2026 with the preliminary meeting and Open Floor Hearing 1; the Examining Authority issued its Rule 6 letter setting out the draft examination timetable.

milestones.title: First Written Questions issued
milestones.description: The Examining Authority issued its First Written Questions to interested parties, with responses due by 26 May 2026.

milestones.title: Examination scheduled to close
milestones.description: Scheduled end of the six-month statutory examination period.

milestones.title: Examining Authority recommendation due
milestones.description: The Examining Authority has three months from close of examination to submit its recommendation report to the Secretary of State.

milestones.title: Secretary of State decision expected
milestones.description: The Secretary of State has three months after receiving the recommendation report to issue a decision on the DCO.

EN010148.name: Tween Bridge Solar Farm
EN010148.summary: RWE Renewables' Tween Bridge Solar Farm is a proposed ground-mounted solar PV development of up to 600-800 MW with co-located battery energy storage, sited east of Thorne in South Yorkshire adjacent to RWE's existing Tween Bridge Wind Farm. The DCO application was accepted by the Planning Inspectorate in September 2025 and is currently in Examination, with a Secretary of State decision expected in 2027.

milestones.title: Project announced
milestones.description: RWE Renewables announces proposals for Tween Bridge Solar Farm adjacent to its existing wind farm east of Thorne.

milestones.title: First statutory (Section 42) consultation
milestones.description: RWE runs first round of statutory consultation on the scheme between October and December 2023.

milestones.title: Second statutory consultation
milestones.description: Further statutory consultation held March to May 2025 reflecting design changes following first-round feedback.

milestones.title: DCO application received (initial)
milestones.description: Application originally submitted to the Planning Inspectorate; later withdrawn on 15 August 2025.

milestones.title: DCO application resubmitted
milestones.description: RWE resubmits the Development Consent Order application to the Planning Inspectorate following the earlier withdrawal.

milestones.title: Application accepted for examination
milestones.description: Planning Inspectorate confirms the application meets standards and is accepted for examination.

milestones.title: Preliminary Meeting and Open Floor Hearing 1
milestones.description: Examining Authority holds Preliminary Meeting and first Open Floor Hearing, marking formal start of the examination.

milestones.title: Rule 8 letter and ExQ1 issued
milestones.description: Examining Authority publishes the formal examination timetable and first written questions to interested parties.

milestones.title: Examination close (expected)
milestones.description: Six-month statutory examination period expected to conclude around mid-October 2026.

milestones.title: Examining Authority recommendation (expected)
milestones.description: Examining Authority's report and recommendation due to the Secretary of State within three months of examination close.

milestones.title: Secretary of State decision (expected)
milestones.description: Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero expected to issue decision on development consent within three months of receiving the recommendation.

EN010151.name: Beacon Fen Energy Park
EN010151.summary: Beacon Fen Energy Park is a proposed 400MW solar photovoltaic farm with up to 600MVA of Battery Energy Storage System (BESS), located in the Fens north of Heckington, Lincolnshire, connecting to the grid at Bicker Fen. The project is promoted by Beacon Fen Energy Park Limited (Low Carbon). Examination closed on 23 February 2026 and the Examining Authority is due to submit its recommendation to the Secretary of State by 23 May 2026.

milestones.title: Project announced
milestones.description: Low Carbon publicly announced the Beacon Fen Energy Park proposal, a solar and battery storage NSIP in Lincolnshire.

milestones.title: Non-statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: Early (non-statutory) public consultation launched, running until 18 June 2023, with in-person events and webinars across local communities.

milestones.title: PINS Scoping Opinion issued
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate issued its EIA Scoping Opinion for the proposed Beacon Fen Energy Park (case reference EN010151).

milestones.title: Project revised, Beacon Fen South withdrawn
milestones.description: Following early consultation feedback, Low Carbon withdrew the Beacon Fen South parcel and confirmed it would progress with Beacon Fen North only.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: Six-week statutory (Section 42/47) consultation on the revised 400MW scheme opens, with public events and webinars across Lincolnshire.

milestones.title: Targeted Section 46 consultation
milestones.description: Targeted consultation on small design changes opens, closing 19 January 2025 ahead of DCO submission.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Beacon Fen Energy Park Limited submitted its Development Consent Order application to the Planning Inspectorate.

milestones.title: Application accepted for examination
milestones.description: PINS accepted the DCO application for examination, confirming it met the statutory tests under the Planning Act 2008.

milestones.title: Registration to have a say opens
milestones.description: Pre-examination registration window for Interested Parties to submit Relevant Representations opens, closing 2 July 2025.

milestones.title: Examination opens (Preliminary Meeting)
milestones.description: The Examining Authority held the Preliminary Meeting and formally opened the six-month examination. Issue Specific Hearing 1 followed on 24 September 2025; Compulsory Acquisition Hearings ran 11-13 November 2025.

milestones.title: Examination closes
milestones.description: Examination concluded after three rounds of written questions and multiple hearings. Completion notification published by the ExA on 24 February 2026.

milestones.title: ExA recommendation due to Secretary of State
milestones.description: Examining Authority's recommendation report to the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero is due.

milestones.title: Secretary of State decision expected
milestones.description: Statutory three-month period for the Secretary of State to issue a decision on the DCO application following receipt of the ExA recommendation.

EN010154.name: Fosse Green Energy
EN010154.summary: Fosse Green Energy Limited is seeking a Development Consent Order for a solar photovoltaic generating station with anticipated capacity in excess of 50MW, with on-site energy storage and grid connection infrastructure, in Lincolnshire. The application was accepted for examination by the Planning Inspectorate on 15 August 2025, and the examination opened on 6 January 2026 with hearings held in Lincoln. The Examining Authority is scheduled to close the examination on 15 June 2026, after which it has three months to submit its recommendation to the Secretary of State.

milestones.title: EIA Scoping Report submitted
milestones.description: Fosse Green Energy Limited submitted an Environmental Impact Assessment Scoping Report to the Secretary of State, formally signalling the project to the Planning Inspectorate.

milestones.title: Scoping Opinion issued
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate issued its Scoping Opinion on the proposed environmental impact assessment, defining the matters to be addressed in the Environmental Statement.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate received the Development Consent Order application from Fosse Green Energy Limited for a generating station with capacity in excess of 50MW comprising solar photovoltaic panels, on-site energy storage, and grid connection infrastructure.

milestones.title: Application accepted for examination
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate accepted the application for examination, with the Consultation Report and supporting application documents published on the project page.

milestones.title: Examining Authority appointed
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate appointed the Examining Authority and published its procedural decisions following acceptance.

milestones.title: Registration period opened
milestones.description: Members of the public and organisations were invited to register as interested parties and submit relevant representations. The registration window ran to 27 October 2025, with a limited extension to 21 November 2025 for parties not given the required statutory notice.

milestones.title: Rule 6 letter issued
milestones.description: The Examining Authority issued the Rule 6 notification of the preliminary meeting, together with the draft examination timetable and initial assessment of principal issues.

milestones.title: Examination opened with Preliminary Meeting
milestones.description: The examination opened with the Preliminary Meeting and Issue Specific Hearing 1 on environmental matters, held as a blended event at the County Assembly Rooms, Lincoln, and virtually via Microsoft Teams.

milestones.title: Accompanied site inspection and ISH3
milestones.description: The Examining Authority conducted an accompanied site inspection on 10 March 2026 and held Issue Specific Hearing 3 on environmental matters, an Open Floor Hearing, and a second Compulsory Acquisition Hearing in Lincoln during the week commencing 9 March 2026.

milestones.title: Scheduled close of examination
milestones.description: The Examining Authority intends to close the examination on 15 June 2026, after which it has three months to submit its recommendation report to the Secretary of State.

EN010158.name: Rosefield Solar Farm
EN010158.summary: Rosefield Solar Farm is a Nationally Significant Infrastructure Project for a solar generating station with a gross output of over 50MW, proposed by Rosefield Energyfarm Limited across land in Buckinghamshire and Oxfordshire. The application was accepted for examination by the Planning Inspectorate on 23 October 2025 and examination opened on 24 February 2026, with hearings continuing into mid-2026 and the examination scheduled to close on 24 August 2026.

milestones.title: Scoping report submitted
milestones.description: Rosefield Energyfarm Limited submitted its EIA Scoping Report (Volumes 1 and 2) to the Secretary of State.

milestones.title: Scoping Opinion adopted
milestones.description: The Secretary of State adopted the Scoping Opinion under the 2017 EIA Regulations, setting out the environmental matters to be assessed.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate received the Development Consent Order application from Rosefield Energyfarm Limited.

milestones.title: Application accepted for examination
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate accepted the application for examination and the application documents were published.

milestones.title: Registration to have your say opened
milestones.description: Members of the public and organisations were invited to register as interested parties, with a deadline of 12 December 2025.

milestones.title: Examination opened (Preliminary Meeting)
milestones.description: The preliminary meeting was held and the examination formally opened. Open Floor Hearing 1 took place the same day, followed by Compulsory Acquisition Hearing 1 on 25 February 2026.

milestones.title: Rule 8 letter and examination timetable issued
milestones.description: The Examining Authority sent the Rule 8 letter to all parties, setting out the examination timetable and procedural decisions.

milestones.title: Examining Authority's first written questions published
milestones.description: The Examining Authority published its first written questions (ExQ1) to parties, with a response deadline of 22 April 2026 (Deadline 2).

milestones.title: Issue Specific Hearings 1 and 2 and site inspection
milestones.description: Accompanied site inspection on 19 May, Issue Specific Hearing 1 on 20-21 May, and Issue Specific Hearing 2 on 22 May 2026.

milestones.title: Examination scheduled to close
milestones.description: The Examining Authority intends to close the examination on this date, after which it has three months to issue its recommendation report to the Secretary of State.

EN010168.name: Lime Down Solar Project
EN010168.summary: Lime Down Solar Project is a c.500 MW solar and battery storage Nationally Significant Infrastructure Project proposed by Lime Down Solar Park Limited (a vehicle of Island Green Power, owned by Macquarie) on approximately 749 hectares north of the M4 near Malmesbury, Wiltshire. The application was submitted to the Planning Inspectorate on 19 September 2025 and accepted on 17 October 2025; examination opened on 21 April 2026 and is scheduled to close on 21 October 2026. Wiltshire Council, the local MP and a community group (Stop Lime Down) have publicly opposed the scheme, citing impacts on best and most versatile agricultural land and proximity to the Cotswolds National Landscape.

milestones.title: First non-statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: Island Green Power launches initial six-week non-statutory community consultation on early-stage proposals for Lime Down Solar Park, running to 26 April 2024.

milestones.title: EIA Scoping Report submitted to PINS
milestones.description: Applicant submits an Environmental Impact Assessment Scoping Report to the Planning Inspectorate requesting a Scoping Opinion for the proposed Development Consent Order application.

milestones.title: PINS issues EIA Scoping Opinion
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate issues its Scoping Opinion setting out the matters to be addressed in the Environmental Statement that will accompany the DCO application.

milestones.title: Statutory (Section 47) consultation opens
milestones.description: Statutory consultation on refined proposals runs from 29 January to 19 March 2025, accompanied by a Statement of Community Consultation and a programme of public events across the Malmesbury area.

milestones.title: Wiltshire Council Cabinet resolves to oppose scheme
milestones.description: Wiltshire Council's Cabinet agrees to submit a formal response to the statutory consultation strongly opposing the proposals, citing loss of best and most versatile agricultural land and impacts on the adjacent Cotswolds National Landscape.

milestones.title: Targeted consultation on localised changes
milestones.description: A further targeted consultation runs from 3 June to 11 July 2025 covering fifteen specific changes that produced small increases to the development boundary, including additional buffer zones.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted to PINS
milestones.description: Lime Down Solar Park Limited formally submits its Development Consent Order application, including Environmental Statement and Consultation Report, to the Planning Inspectorate.

milestones.title: Application accepted for examination
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate accepts the application for examination under the Planning Act 2008, allowing the project to proceed to the pre-examination stage.

milestones.title: Registration for Interested Parties opens
milestones.description: The window for members of the public and organisations to register as Interested Parties and submit Relevant Representations opens, running to 9 January 2026 (extended by PINS to 12 January 2026 due to website issues).

milestones.title: Rule 6 letter and draft examination timetable published
milestones.description: The Examining Authority issues the Rule 6 letter notifying the preliminary meeting, publishing the draft examination timetable and an initial assessment of principal issues.

milestones.title: Examination opens with Preliminary Meeting and OFH1
milestones.description: Examination formally opens with the Preliminary Meeting and Open Floor Hearing 1 on 21 April 2026, followed by Issue Specific Hearing 1 on 22 April covering scope of development, site selection and generating capacity.

milestones.title: Compulsory Acquisition Hearing and further hearings
milestones.description: A scheduled block of hearings includes the Compulsory Acquisition Hearing and Open Floor Hearing 2 on 30 June 2026, followed by Issue Specific Hearings 2 to 4 on 1 to 3 July 2026.

milestones.title: Close of examination
milestones.description: Scheduled deadline for close of examination. The Examining Authority will then have three months to submit its recommendation report to the Secretary of State.

EN010162.name: Great North Road Solar and Biodiversity Park
EN010162.summary: An 800MW solar photovoltaic array with battery energy storage proposed by Elements Green Trent Limited on land northwest of Newark in Nottinghamshire, west of the A1 and north of the A617. The DCO application was accepted in July 2025 and the six-month examination closed on 28 April 2026; the Examining Authority's recommendation is due to the Secretary of State by 28 July 2026.

milestones.title: EIA scoping request submitted
milestones.description: Elements Green Trent Limited submitted an EIA scoping request to the Planning Inspectorate.

milestones.title: First non-statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: Initial public consultation ran from 16 January to 27 February 2024.

milestones.title: Statement of Community Consultation published
milestones.description: SoCC issued in summer 2024 ahead of statutory consultation.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: Section 42/47 statutory consultation ran from 9 January to 20 February 2025.

milestones.title: Targeted consultation
milestones.description: Targeted consultation on localised design additions ran 8 May to 6 June 2025.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Development Consent Order application submitted to the Planning Inspectorate for the 800MW solar and battery storage scheme.

milestones.title: Application accepted for examination
milestones.description: Planning Inspectorate accepted the DCO application for examination.

milestones.title: Preliminary meeting and examination opens
milestones.description: Preliminary meeting held; examination formally opened at its close. Issue Specific Hearing 1 and Open Floor Hearing 1 held 26-27 November 2025.

milestones.title: Compulsory Acquisition and Issue Specific Hearings
milestones.description: Compulsory Acquisition Hearing 1 and further Issue Specific Hearings held 3-4 February 2026.

milestones.title: Accompanied site inspection
milestones.description: Examining Authority undertook an access-required site inspection on 10-11 March 2026.

milestones.title: Examination closes
milestones.description: Six-month examination of the DCO application concluded.

milestones.title: ExA recommendation due to Secretary of State
milestones.description: Examining Authority's recommendation report to be submitted to the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero within three months of examination close.

milestones.title: Expected Secretary of State decision
milestones.description: Statutory three-month decision window following receipt of the ExA recommendation; decision expected by late October 2026.

EN010163.name: Steeple Renewables Project
EN010163.summary: Steeple Renewables Project is a Nationally Significant Infrastructure Project proposed by Steeple Solar Farm Limited (a subsidiary of RES) for up to 400MW of solar generation and a 200MW battery energy storage system on around 930 hectares of land near the decommissioned West Burton Power Station at Sturton-le-Steeple, Bassetlaw, Nottinghamshire. The Examining Authority closed examination on 10 April 2026 and is preparing its recommendation to the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero.

milestones.title: Early informal consultation launched
milestones.description: RES launched a six-week early informal (non-statutory) consultation on outline proposals for up to 400MW of solar generation and 200MW of battery storage near West Burton, with in-person events at South Leverton Memorial Institute.

milestones.title: Early informal consultation closed
milestones.description: The six-week non-statutory consultation period ended; feedback fed into refinement of the proposals ahead of EIA scoping and statutory consultation.

milestones.title: EIA Scoping Opinion issued
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate issued its EIA Scoping Opinion on the Steeple Renewables Project Scoping Report, setting the scope of the Environmental Statement to accompany the DCO application.

milestones.title: Statutory (s42) consultation opened
milestones.description: RES opened a six-week statutory consultation under section 42 of the Planning Act 2008 on the revised proposals for the solar and storage scheme.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation closed
milestones.description: The statutory consultation closed; feedback informed substantial changes to the proposals including reductions in solar panel area and larger buffers to residential properties.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted to PINS
milestones.description: Steeple Solar Farm Limited submitted its Development Consent Order application for the project to the Planning Inspectorate.

milestones.title: Application accepted for examination
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate accepted the DCO application for examination, beginning the pre-examination stage during which the public could register as interested parties.

milestones.title: Examination opened
milestones.description: The six-month examination of the application by the Examining Authority commenced following the preliminary meeting.

milestones.title: Examination closed
milestones.description: The Examining Authority formally closed examination of the application; the notification of completion of examination was published on 13 April 2026.

milestones.title: ExA recommendation due to Secretary of State
milestones.description: The Examining Authority is required to submit its recommendation report to the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero within three months of the close of examination.

milestones.title: Secretary of State decision expected
milestones.description: The Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero is expected to issue a decision on whether to grant the Development Consent Order within three months of receiving the ExA recommendation.

EN010169.name: Meridian Solar Farm
EN010169.summary: Meridian Solar Farm is a proposed Nationally Significant Infrastructure Project by Meridian Solar Farm Ltd (a Downing Renewable Developments vehicle) for a solar photovoltaic and battery storage facility of up to 750MW on around 1,100 hectares of land near Spalding and Crowland in South Holland, Lincolnshire, with a roughly 12km grid connection to the planned Weston Marsh substation. The Planning Inspectorate accepted the Development Consent Order application on 15 April 2026 and the project is currently at the pre-examination stage.

milestones.title: Project announced
milestones.description: Downing Renewable Developments unveiled early-stage plans for a 750MW solar and battery storage NSIP on land south of Spalding in Lincolnshire.

milestones.title: EIA Scoping Report submitted
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate received the applicant's request for a Scoping Opinion under Regulation 10 of the EIA Regulations 2017.

milestones.title: Stage one non-statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: First round of public consultation ran from 30 May to 11 July 2024, with three in-person events and an online webinar.

milestones.title: Scoping Opinion adopted
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate adopted the Scoping Opinion on behalf of the Secretary of State, setting out the required scope of the Environmental Impact Assessment.

milestones.title: Stage two statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: Second round of consultation ran from 24 April to 8 June 2025, presenting refined proposals covering around 1,100 hectares with public events in Sutton St Edmund, Crowland and Weston.

milestones.title: Targeted consultation on grid connection
milestones.description: Additional targeted consultation held between 8 January and 5 February 2026 covering further land required for the grid connection to Weston Marsh Substation B.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Meridian Solar Farm Ltd submitted its Development Consent Order application to the Planning Inspectorate for review.

milestones.title: Application accepted for examination
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate issued its decision to accept the DCO application, moving the project into the pre-examination stage.

milestones.title: Preliminary meeting and examination start (expected)
milestones.description: Following the standard pre-examination period of around three months from acceptance, registration of Interested Parties and the Preliminary Meeting are expected before examination opens.

milestones.title: Examination close and recommendation (expected)
milestones.description: Six-month examination expected to conclude by early 2027, followed by a three-month period for the Examining Authority to produce its recommendation to the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero.

EN010170.name: Green Hill Solar Farm
EN010170.summary: Green Hill Solar Farm is a proposed up to 500 MW solar generating station with battery energy storage on land south and west of Wellingborough and north of Northampton, Northamptonshire, brought forward by Green Hill Solar Farm Limited (Island Green Power). The DCO application was accepted in June 2025 and examination closed on 10 April 2026; the Examining Authority's recommendation to the Secretary of State is due by 10 July 2026 with a decision expected by October 2026.

milestones.title: Project announced
milestones.description: Island Green Power publicly launched the Green Hill Solar Farm proposal via greenhillsolar.co.uk, identifying land south and west of Wellingborough and north of Northampton.

milestones.title: EIA Scoping Report submitted
milestones.description: The applicant submitted its EIA Scoping Report to the Planning Inspectorate under Regulation 10 of the Infrastructure Planning (EIA) Regulations 2017.

milestones.title: Scoping Opinion issued
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate issued its Scoping Opinion setting out the environmental issues to be assessed in the Environmental Statement.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation opened
milestones.description: Section 42/47/48 statutory consultation on the proposed scheme ran from 7 November to 19 December 2024, including public events and a Preliminary Environmental Information Report.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: Green Hill Solar Farm Limited submitted its Development Consent Order application to the Planning Inspectorate for a solar generating station of up to 500 MW with energy storage.

milestones.title: Application accepted for examination
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate accepted the DCO application for examination under section 55 of the Planning Act 2008.

milestones.title: Relevant representations period opened
milestones.description: Registration period for interested parties to submit relevant representations opened, running until 20 August 2025.

milestones.title: Preliminary Meeting and start of examination
milestones.description: The Examining Authority held the Preliminary Meeting, formally opening the six-month examination of the application.

milestones.title: Hearings held
milestones.description: Open Floor Hearing 1, Issue Specific Hearing 3 and Compulsory Acquisition Hearing 1 were held as part of the examination programme.

milestones.title: Examination closed
milestones.description: The Examining Authority closed the examination of the DCO application at 23:59, with the completion notification published on 13 April 2026.

milestones.title: ExA recommendation to Secretary of State due
milestones.description: The Examining Authority has up to three months from the close of examination to submit its recommendation report to the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero.

milestones.title: Secretary of State decision expected
milestones.description: The Secretary of State has up to three months following receipt of the recommendation to decide whether to grant development consent; a decision is anticipated by October 2026.

EN0110012.name: Light Valley Solar
EN0110012.summary: Light Valley Solar is a proposed 500MW solar farm and battery storage NSIP by Light Valley Solar Limited (part of Island Green Power) comprising seven sites covering approximately 1,022 hectares between York and Selby in North Yorkshire, connecting to National Grid's Monk Fryston substation. The DCO application was submitted in February 2026 and accepted for examination by the Planning Inspectorate on 9 March 2026; the project is currently in the Pre-examination stage.

milestones.title: Phase One non-statutory consultation begins
milestones.description: Island Green Power launched the first phase of non-statutory consultation on early-stage proposals for the Light Valley Solar scheme, running October to December 2024.

milestones.title: EIA Scoping Report consultation
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate consulted North Yorkshire Council and other statutory bodies on the Environmental Impact Assessment Scoping Report submitted by the applicant.

milestones.title: PINS issues Scoping Opinion
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate issued its Scoping Opinion setting out the required extent and content of the Environmental Statement for the proposed development.

milestones.title: Phase Two statutory consultation begins
milestones.description: The statutory (Section 42/47) consultation launched, accompanied by the Preliminary Environmental Information Report (PEIR), and ran for six weeks.

milestones.title: Phase Two statutory consultation closes
milestones.description: The statutory consultation on the updated proposals closed after running from 26 June to 7 August 2025.

milestones.title: Targeted consultation on localised changes
milestones.description: A targeted consultation ran from 16 October to 20 November 2025 on around 40 localised, specific changes to the proposals following statutory consultation feedback.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted to PINS
milestones.description: Light Valley Solar Limited submitted its Development Consent Order application to the Planning Inspectorate in February 2026.

milestones.title: DCO application accepted for examination
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate accepted the DCO application for examination, moving the project into the Pre-examination stage.

milestones.title: Deadline to register as Interested Party
milestones.description: Final date for members of the public and organisations to register with the Planning Inspectorate as Interested Parties in order to comment during examination.

milestones.title: Examination expected to begin
milestones.description: Pre-examination typically lasts around three months from acceptance, after which the six-month Examination stage is expected to commence.

EN0110013.name: The Droves Solar Farm
EN0110013.summary: The Droves Solar Farm is a proposed solar photovoltaic generating station with associated Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) and grid connection infrastructure, including a new National Grid substation, located 800m north of Swaffham and 800m south west of Castle Acre in West Norfolk. The application was submitted by The Droves Solar Farm Limited on 19 November 2025 and accepted for examination on 16 December 2025. Examination opened on 6 May 2026.

milestones.title: Scoping Opinion published
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate published its EIA Scoping Opinion for The Droves Solar Farm under the 2017 EIA Regulations.

milestones.title: Section 46 notification of proposed application
milestones.description: The applicant issued its statutory notification to the Secretary of State under section 46 of the Planning Act 2008, signalling the intention to submit a DCO application.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate received the Development Consent Order application from The Droves Solar Farm Limited.

milestones.title: Application accepted for examination
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate issued its section 55 decision accepting the application for examination.

milestones.title: Relevant representations published
milestones.description: Comments submitted by those who registered as interested parties during the registration period were published on the PINS project page.

milestones.title: Unaccompanied site inspection
milestones.description: The Examining Authority carried out an unaccompanied site inspection on 10 and 11 March 2026; the note of inspection was published on 18 March.

milestones.title: Examination opened: Preliminary Meeting and Open Floor Hearing 1
milestones.description: The examination stage opened with the Preliminary Meeting and Open Floor Hearing 1 held at the venue and livestreamed.

milestones.title: Issue Specific Hearing 1
milestones.description: The Examining Authority held Issue Specific Hearing 1 (ISH1), with online observation available.

milestones.title: Deadline 1
milestones.description: Deadline 1 for submissions including written representations, Local Impact Reports, responses to the Examining Authority's first written questions (ExQ1) and comments on relevant representations.

milestones.title: Anticipated close of examination
milestones.description: The examination stage runs for up to six months from its opening on 6 May 2026, after which the Examining Authority has three months to issue its recommendation to the Secretary of State.

EN0110014.name: East Pye Solar
EN0110014.summary: East Pye Solar is a proposed 500MW solar photovoltaic and battery energy storage NSIP by East Pye Solar Ltd (part of Island Green Power), spanning approximately 1,100 hectares across multiple sites south of Norwich and north of Harleston, concentrated east of Long Stratton in South Norfolk. The Planning Inspectorate accepted the application for examination on 2 April 2026, and the project is now in the pre-examination stage with registration of interested parties open until 10 June 2026.

milestones.title: Project unveiled by Island Green Power
milestones.description: Island Green Power publicly unveiled plans for a utility-scale 500MW solar and battery storage scheme on land near Long Stratton in South Norfolk.

milestones.title: Phase One (non-statutory) consultation opens
milestones.description: First phase of community consultation introducing Island Green Power and initial scheme proposals; ran from 23 October to 6 December 2024.

milestones.title: EIA Scoping Report submitted to PINS
milestones.description: East Pye Solar Ltd submitted its Environmental Impact Assessment Scoping Report to the Planning Inspectorate.

milestones.title: PINS publishes Scoping Opinion
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate issued its Scoping Opinion setting out the environmental matters to be assessed in the Environmental Statement.

milestones.title: Section 42 statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: Phase Two statutory consultation ran for seven weeks from 18 June to 6 August 2025, with six in-person events and one online event, attracting over 550 attendees.

milestones.title: Targeted consultation on design changes
milestones.description: Additional targeted consultation between 22 October and 26 November 2025 focused on specific changes informed by ongoing design and environmental assessment work.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted to PINS
milestones.description: East Pye Solar Ltd submitted its Development Consent Order application to the Planning Inspectorate.

milestones.title: Application accepted for examination
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate accepted the East Pye Solar DCO application for examination, moving the project into the pre-examination stage.

milestones.title: Registration period for Interested Parties opens
milestones.description: Registration to participate as an Interested Party and submit Relevant Representations opened, with a deadline of 23:59 on 10 June 2026.

milestones.title: Deadline to register as Interested Party
milestones.description: Closing date for registering to have a say and submitting Relevant Representations to the Examining Authority.

milestones.title: Examination expected to commence
milestones.description: Following the typical three-month pre-examination stage after acceptance, the six-month examination is expected to begin in autumn 2026.

EN010155.name: Dean Moor Solar Farm
EN010155.summary: Dean Moor Solar Farm is a proposed solar generating station of over 50 MW on a c.276.5 ha site west of the A5086 at Branthwaite Edge, Cumberland (south of the Lake District National Park), connecting to the Electricity North West distribution network. The application by FVS Dean Moor Limited was accepted by the Planning Inspectorate in April 2025, examined between July 2025 and January 2026, and the Examining Authority's recommendation was sent to the Secretary of State on 2 April 2026. The Secretary of State is currently in the decision stage, with a statutory decision date of 2 July 2026.

milestones.title: EIA Scoping Report submitted
milestones.description: FVS Dean Moor Limited submitted its Environmental Impact Assessment Scoping Report to the Secretary of State, formally beginning the pre-application process.

milestones.title: Scoping Opinion issued
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate issued its Scoping Opinion to the applicant, setting out the matters the Environmental Statement should address.

milestones.title: DCO application received
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate received the Development Consent Order application from FVS Dean Moor Limited.

milestones.title: Application accepted for examination
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate accepted the application for examination and published the application documents.

milestones.title: Registration period opened
milestones.description: The applicant publicised the accepted application and opened the period for interested parties to register to have their say, with a deadline of 19 June 2025.

milestones.title: Rule 6 letter and preliminary meeting notification
milestones.description: The Examining Authority issued the Rule 6 letter notifying parties of the preliminary meeting, with the draft examination timetable and initial assessment of principal issues.

milestones.title: Examination opens
milestones.description: The preliminary meeting was held on 22 July 2025, with the six-month examination period commencing at its close.

milestones.title: Hearings held
