milestones.description: Issue Specific Hearing 1 was held on 11 November 2025, followed by a Compulsory Acquisition Hearing and Open Floor Hearing on 13 November 2025, with an accompanied site inspection on 4 December 2025.

milestones.title: Examination closes
milestones.description: The Examining Authority closed the examination at 23:59 on 5 January 2026; the Section 99 Notification of Completion of Examination was published the following day.

milestones.title: ExA recommendation sent to Secretary of State
milestones.description: The Examining Authority's recommendation report was sent to the Secretary of State, starting the three-month decision period.

milestones.title: Secretary of State information request
milestones.description: The Secretary of State issued a letter to the applicant and 12 Property FE Limited requesting further information, with a response deadline of 23:59 on 15 May 2026.

milestones.title: Secretary of State decision deadline
milestones.description: The statutory deadline by which the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero is required to issue a decision on the Development Consent Order application.

EN010157.name: Peartree Hill Solar Farm
EN010157.summary: Peartree Hill Solar Farm is a proposed up-to-320 MW solar and battery storage NSIP in East Riding of Yorkshire, north-west of Leven and east of Beverley, brought forward by RWE Renewables UK Solar and Storage Limited (acquired from JBM Solar in March 2023). The DCO application was accepted in March 2025, examined between July 2025 and January 2026, and the Examining Authority's recommendation was submitted to the Secretary of State in April 2026, with a decision deadline of 2 July 2026.

milestones.title: Non-statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: JBM Solar (RWE Group) launched a non-statutory preliminary public consultation on the proposed 320 MW Peartree Hill Solar Farm, running to 6 November 2023.

milestones.title: Scoping request submitted
milestones.description: Planning Inspectorate received an EIA Scoping request from JBM Solar Ltd under Regulation 10 of the 2017 EIA Regulations.

milestones.title: Scoping Opinion adopted
milestones.description: Planning Inspectorate adopted the Scoping Opinion for the proposed Peartree Hill Solar Farm.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: Six-week statutory (s.42/s.47/s.48) consultation on the proposals opened, running until 26 June 2024.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: RWE Renewables UK Solar and Storage Limited submitted the Development Consent Order application to the Planning Inspectorate.

milestones.title: Application accepted for examination
milestones.description: Planning Inspectorate accepted the DCO application for examination under section 55 of the Planning Act 2008.

milestones.title: Registration period opens
milestones.description: Public registration as Interested Parties and submission of relevant representations opened; closed 30 May 2025.

milestones.title: Examination opens
milestones.description: Preliminary Meeting and first Open Floor Hearing held; examination period formally begins.

milestones.title: Issue Specific and Compulsory Acquisition Hearings
milestones.description: Issue Specific Hearings and Compulsory Acquisition Hearings held 21-23 October 2025.

milestones.title: Examination closes
milestones.description: Six-month examination concluded after eight hearings and nine written submission deadlines, with over 700 documents submitted.

milestones.title: ExA recommendation submitted
milestones.description: Examining Authority submitted its report and recommendation to the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero.

milestones.title: Secretary of State information request
milestones.description: Secretary of State issued a request for further information from the applicant and interested parties, with a response deadline of 14 May 2026.

milestones.title: Secretary of State decision deadline
milestones.description: Statutory deadline for the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero to issue a decision on whether to grant the DCO.

EN010159.name: One Earth Solar Farm
EN010159.summary: A proposed 740 MW solar farm with battery energy storage on around 1,409 hectares surrounding the River Trent in Nottinghamshire and Lincolnshire, brought forward by One Earth Solar Farm Ltd (a joint venture between PS Renewables and Orsted). The application is at the Decision stage, with the Secretary of State decision deadline set for 8 July 2026.

milestones.title: Project announced
milestones.description: Orsted announced its entry into UK solar with the One Earth Solar Farm, co-developed with PS Renewables, targeting commercial operation before 2030.

milestones.title: Non-statutory consultation begins
milestones.description: First, non-statutory round of public consultation on early proposals ran September to November 2023.

milestones.title: Scoping Report submitted
milestones.description: Applicant submitted the EIA Scoping Report to the Planning Inspectorate in November 2023.

milestones.title: Scoping Opinion adopted
milestones.description: Planning Inspectorate, on behalf of the Secretary of State, adopted the Scoping Opinion for EN010159.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation begins
milestones.description: Section 42/47/48 statutory consultation on updated proposals and Preliminary Environmental Information Report ran May to July 2024.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: One Earth Solar Farm Ltd submitted the Development Consent Order application to the Planning Inspectorate.

milestones.title: Application accepted for examination
milestones.description: Planning Inspectorate accepted the application for examination under section 55 of the Planning Act 2008.

milestones.title: Preliminary Meeting and examination opens
milestones.description: Preliminary Meeting held on 8 July 2025, marking the start of the six-month examination by the Examining Authority.

milestones.title: Examination closes
milestones.description: Six-month examination concluded on 8 January 2026.

milestones.title: ExA recommendation submitted to Secretary of State
milestones.description: Examining Authority submitted its recommendation report to the Secretary of State within the statutory three-month window after examination close.

milestones.title: Secretary of State requests further information
milestones.description: Secretary of State wrote to interested parties requesting further information, with a response deadline of 15 May 2026.

milestones.title: Secretary of State decision deadline
milestones.description: Statutory deadline for the Secretary of State to issue the decision on the Development Consent Order.

EN020025.name: Continental Link Multi-Purpose Interconnector
EN020025.summary: Continental Link was a proposed 1.8 GW high voltage direct current multi-purpose interconnector (MPI) by National Grid Ventures, intended to link Great Britain to Norway with a converter station near Creyke Beck, East Yorkshire and a landfall on the Holderness Coast, while also enabling the connection of up to 3.6 GW of North Sea offshore wind. The project secured a Section 35 Direction in August 2021 but Norway's transmission system operator Statnett indicated it was not involved at that time, and the project was subsequently withdrawn from the planning process at pre-application stage.

milestones.title: Section 35 request submitted by National Grid Ventures
milestones.description: NGV wrote to the Secretary of State requesting that Continental Link be directed to be treated as development requiring development consent under the Planning Act 2008.

milestones.title: Section 35 Direction issued
milestones.description: Secretary of State issued a Section 35 Direction designating Continental Link as a Nationally Significant Infrastructure Project under the Planning Act 2008.

milestones.title: Project publicly announced
milestones.description: National Grid Ventures announced plans for a 1.8 GW HVDC multi-purpose interconnector intended to link Great Britain with Norway and connect up to 3.6 GW of North Sea offshore wind. Norwegian counterpart Statnett told Reuters it was not currently involved.

milestones.title: Route concept defined
milestones.description: Indicative route from UK seaward limit in the North Sea to a landfall along the Holderness Coast, with a converter station connecting into the National Transmission System at Creyke Beck substation near Cottingham, East Yorkshire.

milestones.title: Norwegian government cools on UK interconnection
milestones.description: Norwegian government rejected a separate proposed 1.4 GW interconnector to the UK (NorthConnect), reflecting wider Norwegian political reluctance to approve new electricity export links and undermining the prospects for Continental Link's Norwegian partner side.

milestones.title: Project withdrawn from Planning Inspectorate
milestones.description: Continental Link was withdrawn from the Planning Inspectorate's pre-application register without progressing to a published scoping opinion, formal consultation or application. PINS records the project as withdrawn; National Grid Ventures has since focused its MPI activity on other projects including LionLink (Netherlands) and GriffinLink (Germany).

EN0110003.name: White Elm Solar Farm
EN0110003.summary: Proposed 200MW (c.675-750 acre) ground-mounted solar photovoltaic and battery storage scheme on land north of Mendlesham across the parishes of Mendlesham, Wickham Skeith and Thwaite in Mid Suffolk, promoted by ELMYA RPC UK Grange Road Limited (Elmya Energy). The project entered the Planning Inspectorate's pre-application service in 2024 and submitted an EIA Scoping Report, but was paused indefinitely and withdrawn from pre-application in May 2025 ahead of planned non-statutory consultation. Suffolk County Council had signalled it would object on grounds of impact on high-quality agricultural land.

milestones.title: Project publicly announced
milestones.description: Elmya Energy publicly identified as promoter of the c.675-750 acre White Elm Solar Farm across Mendlesham, Wickham Skeith and Thwaite, with an indicative pre-application timetable: community engagement autumn 2024, statutory consultation in 2025 and DCO submission in 2026.

milestones.title: EIA scoping request submitted
milestones.description: ELMYA RPC UK Grange Road Limited submitted an Environmental Impact Assessment Scoping Report to the Planning Inspectorate, triggering scoping consultation with statutory consultees including Suffolk County Council.

milestones.title: Scoping consultation deadline
milestones.description: Regulatory deadline for consultee responses to the Planning Inspectorate on the scoping report, after which a scoping opinion would be issued.

milestones.title: Mid Suffolk and Suffolk County Councils request greater transparency
milestones.description: Mid Suffolk District Council and Suffolk County Council publicly called for more detail on grid connection, infrastructure and cumulative landscape impacts, advocating a 'roof before rural' approach.

milestones.title: Project paused indefinitely and withdrawn from pre-application
milestones.description: Elmya announced an indefinite pause of the scheme, cancelling planned non-statutory public consultation events scheduled for 31 May and 4 June 2025, and withdrew the project from the Planning Inspectorate's pre-application service. Suffolk County Council had previously indicated it would object due to the loss of high-quality farmland from food production; the developer did not publicly state a reason.

milestones.title: Local press coverage of withdrawal
milestones.description: Local and trade press reported the indefinite pause and withdrawal, noting cancelled public events and concerns from the council about similar schemes resurfacing under proposed reforms to the Planning and Infrastructure Bill.

EN0110022.name: Kilnside Energy Park
EN0110022.summary: Kilnside Energy Park was a proposed up-to-400 MW solar photovoltaic and battery energy storage scheme on around 950 hectares of land between Pickworth, Exton, and Great Casterton in Rutland, brought forward by Kilnside Energy Park Limited (Aukera UK). The applicant entered the NSIP pre-application process in mid-2025, secured a Scoping Opinion from the Planning Inspectorate, and held early community engagement before withdrawing from the pre-application service in early 2026, citing the National Grid connection queue and the need to review the project timeline.

milestones.title: Scoping request submitted
milestones.description: Kilnside Energy Park Limited submitted a request to the Planning Inspectorate for a Scoping Opinion under regulation 10 of the Infrastructure Planning (EIA) Regulations 2017.

milestones.title: Proposals unveiled
milestones.description: Aukera publicly unveiled proposals for a 400 MW solar and battery energy park spanning approximately 950 hectares in Rutland, from Pickworth Great Wood northward to north-west of Great Casterton, with parcels near Exton Park.

milestones.title: Scoping Opinion issued
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate issued its Scoping Opinion setting out the expected scope of the Environmental Impact Assessment for the proposed development.

milestones.title: Community design workshop
milestones.description: Aukera held a non-statutory design workshop at Great Casterton Church Hall as part of early community engagement, with invitations hand-delivered to selected households.

milestones.title: Project Newsletter 2 published
milestones.description: The applicant issued its second project newsletter updating residents on design progress and noting that a portion of land originally included in the plan had been withdrawn by a local landowner.

milestones.title: Withdrawal letter sent to PINS
milestones.description: Kilnside Energy Park Limited wrote to the Planning Inspectorate stating it did not wish to proceed at this time and requesting the application be removed from the PINS website, citing the National Grid connection queue and a review of the project timeline following a NESO Gate 1 connection offer.

milestones.title: Project withdrawn from pre-application service
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate's project page confirmed Kilnside Energy Park had been withdrawn from the pre-application service while the applicant reviews the timeline for grid connection.

EN0210006.name: Ossian Transmission Infrastructure
EN0210006.summary: The Ossian Transmission Infrastructure NSIP would have brought up to 3.6 GW of electricity from the Ossian floating offshore wind farm in Scottish waters (SSE Renewables, Marubeni, Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners) onshore via HVDC cables landing on the Lincolnshire coast and connecting to two grid points near Weston Marsh and Alford. After receiving a Section 35 direction in October 2024 and a Scoping Opinion in April 2025, the applicant withdrew the application on 27 March 2026, before submitting a Development Consent Order.

milestones.title: ScotWind seabed lease awarded
milestones.description: The SSE Renewables (40%), Marubeni (30%) and Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners (30%) consortium was notified of award of seabed rights in the E1 zone of the Firth of Forth (approximately 858 km2), with potential floating offshore wind capacity initially up to 2.6 GW.

milestones.title: Project named Ossian
milestones.description: The consortium publicly named the floating offshore wind project Ossian. Capacity was later revised upward to up to 3.6 GW.

milestones.title: Section 35 direction issued
milestones.description: The Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero issued a Section 35 direction under the Planning Act 2008, designating the Ossian onshore transmission infrastructure as a Nationally Significant Infrastructure Project to be consented via the DCO regime in England.

milestones.title: Lincolnshire community consultation events
milestones.description: Ossian held early-stage open days in Lincolnshire (including Moulton Seas End) presenting indicative converter station locations at Weston Marsh near Spalding and Alford near Skegness, with cable landfall on the Lincolnshire coast.

milestones.title: EIA Scoping Report submitted
milestones.description: Ossian Offshore Wind Farm Ltd submitted a five-part Environmental Impact Assessment Scoping Report to the Planning Inspectorate, covering HVDC offshore export cables in English waters, landfall, onshore HVDC cables and onshore converter stations connecting to the grid in Lincolnshire.

milestones.title: Scoping Opinion adopted
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate, on behalf of the Secretary of State, adopted the Scoping Opinion for the Ossian Transmission Infrastructure NSIP under the Infrastructure Planning (EIA) Regulations 2017.

milestones.title: Regulation 32 transboundary screening
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate carried out Regulation 32 transboundary screening; responses were subsequently received from Denmark and the Netherlands (18 August 2025).

milestones.title: Application withdrawn
milestones.description: Ossian Offshore Wind Farm Ltd notified the Planning Inspectorate of the withdrawal of the Ossian Transmission Infrastructure pre-application, accompanied by a covering letter. The withdrawal occurred before any DCO application had been formally submitted; the wider Ossian project continues to progress separate Scottish consents.

EN010102.name: Hinkley Point C New Nuclear Power Station Material Change 1
EN010102.summary: MC1 is a Material Change application to the 2013 Hinkley Point C Development Consent Order, brought forward by NNB Generation Company (HPC) Limited to authorise as-built and in-construction design changes including removal of the acoustic fish deterrent, a shift to a dry interim spent fuel store, meteorological mast redesign, sluice gate storage racks, and retention of a permanent electrical substation. The project remains in pre-application; initial statutory consultation ran January-February 2024, a Secretary of State direction on consultation parties was issued in October 2023, and a second phase of consultation followed in 2025. Submission to the Planning Inspectorate is now expected in January 2027, against a backdrop of repeated cost and schedule revisions to the wider HPC build, most recently to £35bn (2015 prices) and first power in 2030.

milestones.title: Original DCO application submitted (EN010001)
milestones.description: NNB Generation Company submits the original Hinkley Point C Development Consent Order application to the Infrastructure Planning Commission.

milestones.title: Original DCO granted
milestones.description: Secretary of State for Energy and Climate Change grants development consent for the Hinkley Point C nuclear generating station (the order modified by MC1).

milestones.title: EDF Board approves Final Investment Decision
milestones.description: EDF Board of Directors votes 10-7 to approve the final investment decision for Hinkley Point C, authorising contract signature.

milestones.title: UK Government confirms project approval
milestones.description: Following a comprehensive re-examination, the UK Government confirms its decision to proceed with HPC, with new conditions on EDF's ownership stake during construction. Initial cost estimate £18bn, first power targeted for end-2025.

milestones.title: First nuclear safety concrete poured
milestones.description: Following ONR approval, the first nuclear safety concrete is poured for the power station galleries, marking the start of permanent nuclear construction.

milestones.title: First cost overrun announced
milestones.description: EDF announces total cost likely to rise to £19.6-20.3bn (2015 prices), an increase of up to £1.5bn from the FID estimate, with the risk of a 15-month delay to unit 1.

milestones.title: J0 milestone reached on Unit 1
milestones.description: Final concrete pour completes the 9,000m3 'common raft' foundation platform for reactor Unit 1, the largest continuous concrete pour in UK construction.

milestones.title: Schedule slip to June 2026 and £22-23bn estimate
milestones.description: EDF revises the schedule following COVID-19 impacts: unit 1 first power moved to June 2026 (from end-2025) and project cost raised to £22-23bn (2015 prices), with around £500m attributed to the pandemic.

milestones.title: Schedule slip to June 2027 and £25-26bn estimate
milestones.description: EDF announces a further one-year delay to June 2027 and a £3bn cost increase to £25-26bn (2015 prices), citing pandemic effects, UK regulatory design adaptations, and marine works overruns.

milestones.title: MC1 pre-application opens: Secretary of State consultation parties direction
milestones.description: Secretary of State issues a letter confirming consultation parties for the proposed Material Change application, formally opening the MC1 pre-application process.

milestones.title: Unit 1 reactor building dome lifted into place
milestones.description: The 245-tonne, 47m-diameter steel dome is lifted onto the Unit 1 reactor building by the Big Carl crane, marking the end of major civil engineering works on the reactor building.

milestones.title: MC1 Phase 1 statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: NNB Generation Company opens its first phase of statutory consultation on the proposed Material Change, covering removal of the acoustic fish deterrent, a wet-to-dry interim spent fuel store change, meteorological mast redesign, sluice gate storage racks, and permanent retention of the temporary electrical substation.

milestones.title: Schedule slip to 2029-2031 and £31-35bn estimate
milestones.description: EDF announces three scenarios: a base case of unit 1 first power in 2029 at £31-34bn (2015 prices), and a worst case of 2031 at £35bn (2015 prices). Inflation-adjusted cost stated as up to £46bn.

milestones.title: MC1 Phase 1 consultation closes
milestones.description: The first phase of statutory consultation on the Material Change closes after eight weeks; responses received from statutory consultees including the Environment Agency.

milestones.title: MC1 Phase 2 consultation begins
milestones.description: NNB Generation Company opens a second phase of consultation responding to Phase 1 feedback, refining the scope of the Material Change application.

milestones.title: Cost rises to £35bn, first power moved to 2030
milestones.description: In its 2025 annual results, EDF confirms unit 1 first power is now scheduled for 2030 (a further 12-month commissioning delay) and the project cost rises to £35bn (2015 prices), triggering a 2.5bn euro impairment.

milestones.title: MC1 application submission expected
milestones.description: NNB Generation Company expected to submit the Material Change DCO application to the Planning Inspectorate (revised from the original early-2026 timeline).

milestones.title: Unit 1 first power expected
milestones.description: EDF's current schedule targets first electricity generation from HPC Unit 1 in 2030, following a 12-month commissioning extension announced in the 2025 results.

EN010144.name: Dogger Bank D Wind Farm
EN010144.summary: Dogger Bank D is a proposed fourth phase of the Dogger Bank offshore wind farm zone in the North Sea around 210km off the Yorkshire coast, being developed as a 50:50 joint venture between SSE Renewables and Equinor with capacity of up to around 2GW. The project is enabled by The Crown Estate's Capacity Increase Programme, which permits an amendment to the existing Dogger Bank C seabed lease, and remains in the pre-application stage with a Development Consent Order submission expected in 2026 or 2027.

milestones.title: First non-statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: A six-week initial non-statutory public consultation on the Dogger Bank D proposals ran from 26 September to 7 November 2023, gathering early feedback on the project.

milestones.title: Crown Estate Capacity Increase Programme announced
milestones.description: The Crown Estate announced its Capacity Increase Programme, allowing seven existing fixed-bottom offshore wind lease areas, including Dogger Bank C, to be amended to unlock up to 4.7GW of additional capacity. SSE and Equinor confirmed this enabled progression of a potential Dogger Bank D fourth phase.

milestones.title: EIA Scoping Report submitted to Planning Inspectorate
milestones.description: SSE and Equinor submitted an Environmental Impact Assessment Scoping Report to the Planning Inspectorate for the proposed Dogger Bank D wind farm, formally beginning the EIA process under the NSIP regime.

milestones.title: Preliminary seabed lease terms agreed with Crown Estate
milestones.description: SSE and Equinor established preliminary commercial terms with The Crown Estate for an amendment to the existing Dogger Bank C seabed lease to enable Dogger Bank D, subject to the outcome of a plan-level Habitats Regulations Assessment.

milestones.title: Scoping Opinion issued by Planning Inspectorate
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate issued its formal Scoping Opinion for the Dogger Bank D Offshore Wind Farm, setting out the scope of the Environmental Impact Assessment to accompany any subsequent Development Consent Order application.

milestones.title: Second non-statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: A second non-statutory public consultation on refined project proposals ran for six weeks from 10 September to 22 October 2024, with in-person events and online sessions across the affected onshore corridor in East Riding of Yorkshire.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation and PEIR published
milestones.description: Statutory consultation opened on 10 June 2025 and ran until 5 August 2025, with publication of the Preliminary Environmental Information Report (PEIR) and section 48 notice. The Planning Inspectorate received the related s46 notification on the same date.

milestones.title: Seabed lease finalised with Crown Estate
milestones.description: Following conclusion of the plan-level Habitats Regulations Assessment for the Capacity Increase Programme, SSE and Equinor finalised the amended seabed lease with The Crown Estate. The project is described as unlocking up to 1.5GW of additional capacity from the Dogger Bank C lease area.

milestones.title: DCO application expected
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate's project page for EN010144 indicates an expected Development Consent Order application submission date of June 2027, with acceptance to be decided within 28 days of submission.

milestones.title: Earliest start of construction
milestones.description: If consented, the developers indicate Dogger Bank D could enter construction in 2029 at the earliest, with completion no later than 2035 and proposed capacity of up to around 2GW from up to 113 fixed-bottom turbines.

EN020033.name: LionLink Interconnector
EN020033.summary: LionLink (formerly EuroLink) is a proposed 1.8 GW HVDC multi-purpose interconnector between Great Britain and the Netherlands, jointly developed by National Grid Ventures and Dutch TSO TenneT, with a Suffolk landfall at Walberswick connecting via a converter station near Saxmundham to the existing Friston substation. The project remains in pre-application; statutory consultation ran from 13 January to 10 March 2026 ahead of a planned Development Consent Order application. Suffolk County Council and local groups oppose the Walberswick landfall and the lack of coordination with other east coast energy projects.

milestones.title: Section 35 direction issued (as EuroLink)
milestones.description: Secretary of State directs that the EuroLink (later renamed LionLink) multi-purpose interconnector be treated as a Nationally Significant Infrastructure Project under section 35 of the Planning Act 2008.

milestones.title: Initial non-statutory consultation
milestones.description: National Grid Ventures held an initial non-statutory consultation on the EuroLink proposals between October and December 2022, presenting early route and landfall options.

milestones.title: UK and Netherlands announce LionLink
milestones.description: The UK and Dutch governments jointly unveiled LionLink as a first-of-its-kind hybrid interconnector linking offshore wind in the North Sea to both national grids. The project was rebranded from EuroLink to LionLink.

milestones.title: Supplementary non-statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: NGV opens a supplementary non-statutory consultation (8 September to 3 November 2023) introducing an alternative landfall at Walberswick and a cable corridor north of Southwold, in addition to the original Southwold option.

milestones.title: Shortlisted landfall sites announced
milestones.description: National Grid publishes shortlisted landfall sites for LionLink at Southwold and Walberswick, with a converter station near Saxmundham and cable route to Friston substation. NGV confirmed it would not coordinate LionLink with the more advanced Sea Link project.

milestones.title: EIA Scoping Report submitted
milestones.description: NGV submits an Environmental Impact Assessment Scoping Report to the Planning Inspectorate for the LionLink project.

milestones.title: Dutch grid connection via Nederwiek 3 confirmed
milestones.description: TenneT and NGV confirm the Dutch end of LionLink will connect via the planned Nederwiek 3 offshore wind farm zone, integrating cross-border interconnection with offshore wind generation.

milestones.title: Walberswick selected as preferred landfall
milestones.description: NGV announces Walberswick as preferred landfall over Southwold, citing a shorter onshore route (about 19.9km versus 32.8km to Saxmundham), smaller footprint and lower combined environmental impact.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: Eight-week statutory consultation opens on revised LionLink plans, including approximately 20km of underground HVDC cable from Walberswick to a converter station east of Saxmundham (up to 26m high, 8.1ha footprint) and HVAC connection to Kiln Lane substation at Friston.

milestones.title: Suffolk County Council formally objects
milestones.description: Suffolk County Council cabinet approves a 23,000-word objection to the statutory consultation, calling the Walberswick landfall unacceptable and criticising the lack of coordination with Sea Link.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation closes
milestones.description: The eight-week statutory consultation on the revised LionLink proposals closes. NGV will use responses to refine the application before submitting a Development Consent Order to the Planning Inspectorate.

milestones.title: TenneT and National Grid sign Joint Development Agreement
milestones.description: TenneT and National Grid Ventures sign a Joint Development Agreement formalising the partnership and setting out procurement, governance and planning arrangements ahead of a final investment decision targeted for 2026.

milestones.title: DCO application submission expected
milestones.description: NGV expects to submit its Development Consent Order application to the Planning Inspectorate in Q4 2026, with a final investment decision also targeted for 2026.

milestones.title: Enabling works planned
milestones.description: Subject to development consent, NGV anticipates enabling works to begin in 2028, with construction completion targeted for 2032.

EN020034.name: North Humber to High Marnham
EN020034.summary: National Grid Electricity Transmission's proposed 400kV overhead transmission line of approximately 90km between a new Birkhill Wood substation near Creyke Beck (East Riding of Yorkshire) and a new substation at High Marnham (Nottinghamshire). Part of the Great Grid Upgrade, the project's need case derives from the National Energy System Operator's Holistic Network Design and Network Options Assessment, which identified east-coast reinforcement as required by 2030 to carry offshore wind and interconnector flows southward. The project is at pre-application stage, having completed three rounds of consultation; statutory consultation closed in April 2025 and a DCO application is expected in 2026.

milestones.title: Identified in Holistic Network Design and NOA refresh
milestones.description: Reinforcement between North Humber and High Marnham identified as required by 2030 in the National Grid ESO Holistic Network Design and Network Options Assessment refresh published in July 2022, setting the need case for the project.

milestones.title: Project Background Document and corridor identification
milestones.description: National Grid published the Project Background Document and route corridor identification materials, formally launching engagement on the proposed 400kV overhead line between Creyke Beck and High Marnham.

milestones.title: First non-statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: National Grid ran a non-statutory consultation from June to July 2023 seeking views on the proposed route corridor across Yorkshire, North Lincolnshire and Nottinghamshire.

milestones.title: EIA Scoping Report submitted to PINS
milestones.description: National Grid submitted its Environmental Impact Assessment Scoping Report to the Planning Inspectorate to define the scope of environmental assessment for the proposed development.

milestones.title: PINS Scoping Opinion issued
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate issued its Scoping Opinion under the Infrastructure Planning (Environmental Impact Assessment) Regulations 2017.

milestones.title: Localised non-statutory consultation
milestones.description: A localised non-statutory consultation ran from 9 July to 6 August 2024, focusing on an alternative corridor between South Wheatley and High Marnham following feedback to the 2023 consultation.

milestones.title: Section 46 notification served on PINS
milestones.description: National Grid served notification on the Planning Inspectorate under section 46 of the Planning Act 2008 of its intention to submit a DCO application, marking the formal start of statutory pre-application consultation.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation opens with PEIR
milestones.description: Eight-week statutory consultation under sections 42, 47 and 48 of the Planning Act 2008 opened, accompanied by the Preliminary Environmental Information Report and detailed proposed pylon positions along the 90km route.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation closes
milestones.description: Statutory consultation closed at 23:59. Submissions included calls from Bassetlaw District Council, parish councils, farmers and the 'United AGAINST the North Humber to High Marnham Great Grid Upgrade' campaign for undergrounding or offshore alternatives in visually sensitive areas.

milestones.title: Post-consultation refinement and community update
milestones.description: National Grid published a community update setting out how feedback from statutory consultation, alongside ongoing technical and environmental assessments, is being used to refine proposals ahead of DCO submission.

milestones.title: DCO application submission expected
milestones.description: National Grid expects to submit its Development Consent Order application to the Planning Inspectorate, triggering a 28-day acceptance review.

milestones.title: Examination expected
milestones.description: If accepted, a pre-examination period of approximately three months would precede a six-month examination by the Planning Inspectorate, followed by a recommendation and Secretary of State decision.

EN0110006.name: Mersey Tidal Power Project
EN0110006.summary: Liverpool City Region Combined Authority's proposed tidal-range barrage across the Mersey estuary, designated as a Nationally Significant Infrastructure Project under EN0110006. Championed by Metro Mayor Steve Rotheram since his 2017 election, the scheme remains in the pre-application phase, with a Development Consent Order submission now expected in 2028 and a Financial Investment Decision targeted for 2030 following a two-year timeline extension announced in January 2026.

milestones.title: Original Mersey Barrage concept identified
milestones.description: A Department of the Environment and UKAEA report identified a site between New Brighton and Brocklebank Dock as suitable for a tidal barrage across the Mersey estuary, the first formal study of the location.

milestones.title: Earlier Peel Energy scheme abandoned
milestones.description: Peel Energy and the North West Development Agency concluded their tidal barrage study, abandoning the scheme after finding the proposed Dingle to New Ferry barrage was not expected to be profitable in the medium term.

milestones.title: Rotheram elected Metro Mayor on barrage commitment
milestones.description: Steve Rotheram won the inaugural Liverpool City Region Metro Mayor election after pledging to revive plans for a tidal barrage across the Mersey as a flagship campaign commitment.

milestones.title: Combined Authority funds Mersey Tidal Commission
milestones.description: The Liverpool City Region Combined Authority unanimously approved funding from its 2018-19 priorities budget for the newly established Mersey Tidal Commission to deliver the first two stages of feasibility work over a 12-month period.

milestones.title: Outline feasibility study completed
milestones.description: The Mersey Tidal Commission delivered its outline business case following a year-long feasibility study, indicating the project's economics were more favourable than in 2011 but still fell short by around 20 per cent.

milestones.title: £2.5m granted for further development
milestones.description: The Combined Authority allocated £2.5 million to advance the next stage of feasibility and technical development work on the tidal barrage proposal.

milestones.title: Project included in LCR Plan for Prosperity
milestones.description: The Liverpool City Region Combined Authority formally committed to the development of Mersey Tidal Power in its 2022 Plan for Prosperity, which sets the policy framework for the region to 2035.

milestones.title: Memorandum of Agreement with K-water signed
milestones.description: Mayor Rotheram signed a Memorandum of Agreement with Korea Water Resources Corporation (K-water), operator of the Sihwa Lake tidal power station, for reciprocal visits and information sharing under the UK-Republic of Korea Innovation Twins Programme.

milestones.title: Combined Authority approves entry into DCO process
milestones.description: At its 15 March meeting, the Combined Authority agreed to enter the formal Development Consent Order planning process and selected a Wirral-to-Liverpool barrage as the preferred option, authorising preparation of a Scoping Report for the Planning Inspectorate.

milestones.title: Scoping Report submitted to Planning Inspectorate
milestones.description: The applicant submitted an Environmental Impact Assessment Scoping Report to the Planning Inspectorate under NSIP reference EN0110006, requesting advice on the scope of ecology and environmental surveys required.

milestones.title: Phase 1 community consultation launched
milestones.description: A six-week non-statutory public consultation opened, inviting comments on initial proposals for the tidal barrage and associated infrastructure. The consultation ran until 15 November 2024.

milestones.title: Scoping Opinion issued by Planning Inspectorate
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate issued its Scoping Opinion on the EIA Scoping Report, setting out the scope of environmental assessment and survey work required ahead of a DCO application.

milestones.title: Project timeline extended by two years
milestones.description: The Combined Authority announced a two-year delay, pushing the target DCO submission from 2026 to 2028 and the Financial Investment Decision to 2030, citing the need to secure development funding from government departments to complete the full business case.

milestones.title: Expected DCO application submission
milestones.description: The Combined Authority is targeting submission of its Development Consent Order application to the Planning Inspectorate in January 2028, following completion of environmental surveys and statutory consultation.

milestones.title: Targeted Financial Investment Decision
milestones.description: The Combined Authority is targeting a Financial Investment Decision for construction by 2030, subject to DCO grant and securing government funding support.

EN0110007.name: Stanlow Hydrogen Ready Modular Combined Heat and Power Project
EN0110007.summary: A proposed hydrogen-ready combined heat and power facility at the Stanlow refinery in Ellesmere Port, Cheshire, to be developed by EET Fuels (formerly Essar Oil UK) as part of the HyNet North West industrial decarbonisation cluster. The project comprises gas turbines and heat recovery steam generators with an expected generating capacity of around 200MW, intended to run initially on refinery off-gas and natural gas before switching to low-carbon hydrogen supplied by HyNet. It remains at pre-application stage, with a Development Consent Order application anticipated in April 2027.

milestones.title: HyNet North West selected as Track-1 CCUS cluster
milestones.description: The UK government confirmed HyNet North West as one of the two Track-1 industrial clusters in the Cluster Sequencing Process, providing the policy framework for low-carbon hydrogen and CO2 transport infrastructure that underpins the Stanlow CHP project.

milestones.title: Essar Oil UK rebrands as EET Fuels
milestones.description: The operator of the Stanlow refinery rebranded from Essar Oil UK to EET Fuels, part of the Essar Energy Transition group, as part of its programme to decarbonise the Stanlow Manufacturing Complex.

milestones.title: EET Hydrogen Power launched with Stanlow CHP plans
milestones.description: EET formally announced EET Hydrogen Power and unveiled plans for a hydrogen-ready combined heat and power plant at Stanlow, targeting 125MW of power and 6,000 tonnes of steam per day across two phases, with replacement of hydrocarbon fuel by hydrogen projected to cut emissions by 740,000 tonnes of CO2 a year.

milestones.title: Hydrogen-ready furnace operational at Stanlow
milestones.description: Essar Energy Transition's hydrogen-ready process furnace was brought into operation at Stanlow, a precursor step demonstrating hydrogen-capable combustion equipment ahead of the CHP project.

milestones.title: Phase 1 CHP environmental documents prepared
milestones.description: Environmental Statement and supporting documents for the smaller Phase 1 CHP (around 49.5MW, to be consented locally by Cheshire West and Chester Council) were prepared, providing technical and environmental baseline material relevant to the larger NSIP modular project.

milestones.title: Project listed by Planning Inspectorate as EN0110007
milestones.description: The Stanlow Hydrogen Ready Modular Combined Heat and Power Project appears on the Planning Inspectorate's national infrastructure consenting register at pre-application stage, with EET Fuels as the applicant and an expected DCO submission date of April 2027.

milestones.title: EIA scoping request anticipated
milestones.description: EET Fuels is expected to submit a formal EIA scoping request to the Planning Inspectorate to define the scope of environmental assessment for the DCO application, in line with the April 2027 submission timetable.

milestones.title: Anticipated DCO application submission
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate indicates the application for development consent is expected to be submitted in April 2027.

milestones.title: Targeted commissioning of hydrogen supply
milestones.description: EET's project literature indicates the separate HyNet hydrogen production plants supplying the CHP are planned to be commissioned by March 2028, enabling the CHP to transition from refinery off-gas and natural gas to low-carbon hydrogen.

EN0110029.name: STEP Fusion
EN0110029.summary: STEP (Spherical Tokamak for Energy Production) is the UK Atomic Energy Authority's flagship prototype fusion energy plant, to be built at the former West Burton coal-fired power station site in Nottinghamshire. Delivery is led by UK Industrial Fusion Solutions Ltd (UKIFS, recently rebranded UK Fusion Energy), a UKAEA subsidiary running a public-private partnership; the government has committed over £2.5 billion across five years through the 2025 Spending Review, with first operations targeted for around 2040.

milestones.title: Government announces £220m STEP concept design programme
milestones.description: Business and Energy Secretary Andrea Leadsom announced £220 million over four years to develop a concept design for the Spherical Tokamak for Energy Production (STEP), with a prototype plant targeted by 2040.

milestones.title: Open call for STEP host sites launched
milestones.description: UKAEA opened an invitation for communities to nominate potential sites to host the STEP prototype fusion plant, attracting fifteen long-listed locations across the UK.

milestones.title: Shortlist of five candidate sites announced
milestones.description: Following initial assessments the long list was reduced to five shortlisted sites: West Burton (Nottinghamshire), Ardeer (North Ayrshire), Moorside (Cumbria), Goole (East Yorkshire) and Severn Edge (Gloucestershire).

milestones.title: West Burton selected as STEP site
milestones.description: The Secretary of State confirmed the former West Burton A coal-fired power station in Nottinghamshire as the chosen location for the STEP prototype fusion plant after a two-year competitive process.

milestones.title: UK Industrial Fusion Solutions Ltd announced
milestones.description: Science Minister George Freeman announced the creation of UK Industrial Fusion Solutions Ltd (UKIFS), a wholly-owned UKAEA subsidiary established to lead a public-private partnership to design, build and operate STEP.

milestones.title: Procurement launched for engineering and construction partners
milestones.description: UKIFS opened a competition to select industry partners to deliver STEP, covering an engineering partner and a construction partner with a combined contract pipeline reported as worth up to £10 billion over the life of the programme.

milestones.title: UKIFS stands up as delivery vehicle
milestones.description: UKIFS formally stood up to take responsibility for delivering STEP, with UKAEA continuing as fusion technology partner.

milestones.title: Engineering and construction partner shortlists published
milestones.description: Shortlists were announced for STEP's engineering partner (Celestial JV led by Eni UK; Phoenix UK Fusion led by Cavendish Nuclear) and construction partner (Inovus led by Balfour Beatty; ILIOS led by Kier; Ferrovial-Mace JV).

milestones.title: £2.5 billion committed at Spending Review
milestones.description: The 2025 Spending Review confirmed over £2.5 billion of funding across five years for fusion, with STEP at West Burton as the principal recipient.

milestones.title: New National Policy Statement for fusion energy
milestones.description: Government published its response on a new National Policy Statement for fusion energy facilities, easing the planning route for fusion projects including STEP through the NSIP regime.

milestones.title: Early non-statutory public consultation opens
milestones.description: UKIFS launched a localised non-statutory consultation on the STEP proposals at West Burton with a programme of events across surrounding villages running through March 2026.

milestones.title: ILIOS consortium appointed as construction partner
milestones.description: UKIFS appointed the ILIOS consortium, led by a Kier/Nuvia joint venture with AECOM, AL_A Architects and Turner & Townsend, as construction partner for the c.£200 million Phase 1 redevelopment of the West Burton site.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order application targeted
milestones.description: UK Fusion Energy has indicated it will submit the Development Consent Order for STEP Fusion by March 2029, with examination, decision and construction to follow ahead of first operations targeted for the early 2040s.

EN010156.name: Maen Hir Solar and Energy Storage Project
EN010156.summary: Lightsource bp's proposed 350-360 MW solar PV and battery storage scheme across approximately 1,284 hectares on Anglesey (Ynys Mon), centred on the former Shell Rhosgoch depot near the Wylfa nuclear site. Classified as a Nationally Significant Infrastructure Project requiring a Development Consent Order from the UK Secretary of State, with submission to the Planning Inspectorate currently indicated for October 2026. The scheme is contested in Wales, with the local Plaid Cymru MP and Senedd member, Anglesey council, and rural campaign groups raising concerns about scale, farmland loss and food security.

milestones.title: EIA Scoping Report submitted to PINS
milestones.description: Environmental Impact Assessment scoping request submitted to the Planning Inspectorate, formally beginning the NSIP pre-application process under reference EN010156.

milestones.title: Project publicly announced
milestones.description: Lightsource bp publicly identified land on Anglesey for a major solar and storage scheme centred on the former Shell Rhosgoch depot, with initial estimates of around 3,000 acres.

milestones.title: Stage One non-statutory consultation
milestones.description: Lightsource bp ran an initial non-statutory community consultation on Anglesey, sharing early design proposals and inviting feedback on the scheme's footprint and access routes.

milestones.title: PINS Scoping Opinion issued
milestones.description: Planning Inspectorate issued its EIA Scoping Opinion to inform preparation of the Preliminary Environmental Information Report.

milestones.title: Stage Two Statutory Consultation opens
milestones.description: Statutory consultation under section 47 of the Planning Act 2008 launched alongside the Preliminary Environmental Information Report and Non-Technical Summary.

milestones.title: Westminster debate on large-scale solar and food security
milestones.description: Plaid Cymru MP for Ynys Mon, Llinos Medi, led a Westminster Hall debate citing Maen Hir and Alaw Mon, arguing the schemes could affect productive farmland equivalent to 31 farms.

milestones.title: Statutory Consultation closes
milestones.description: Six-week statutory consultation period ended; Lightsource bp committed to reviewing feedback and reflecting it in the final design and Consultation Report.

milestones.title: Pre-application Programme Document published
milestones.description: Updated pre-application programme document published indicating revised submission timing, with DCO submission deferred from earlier indications of summer 2025.

milestones.title: DCO application expected at PINS
milestones.description: Planning Inspectorate's project page indicates the Development Consent Order application is anticipated to be submitted by Lightsource bp in October 2026.

milestones.title: Secretary of State decision expected
milestones.description: After examination and the Examining Authority's recommendation, the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero would issue a decision on the DCO.

EN010161.name: Stallingborough Combined Cycle Gas Turbine (CCGT) generating plant and Carbon Capture
EN010161.summary: RWE Generation UK plc is developing a proposed 900 MW combined cycle gas turbine (CCGT) generating plant with carbon capture at Hobson Way, around 3.5 km east of Stallingborough in North East Lincolnshire. Captured CO2 (up to 2 million tonnes per year) would be transported via the separate Viking CCS pipeline for offshore storage beneath the North Sea, positioning the scheme as a Humber cluster expansion project. The application is at pre-application stage, with a statutory consultation running March to May 2026 and a Development Consent Order submission expected in July 2027.

milestones.title: RWE announces three UK carbon capture projects including Stallingborough
milestones.description: RWE announced development proposals for three new carbon capture projects in the UK, including a new gas-fired generating station with CCS at Stallingborough, as a capture partner for the Viking CCS network.

milestones.title: Technip Energies and GE Gas Power appointed for pre-FEED studies
milestones.description: RWE appointed Technip Energies (with GE Gas Power) and Bechtel to deliver pre-FEED engineering studies for the integrated CCGT and carbon capture facility, using Shell's Cansolv capture technology.

milestones.title: RWE launches non-statutory public consultation
milestones.description: RWE opened a six-week non-statutory consultation on the proposed 900 MW Stallingborough CCGT with carbon capture, running from 8 April to 20 May 2024, to gather initial community and stakeholder feedback.

milestones.title: EIA scoping engagement with Planning Inspectorate
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate published initial scoping documentation for EN010161, formally entering the project into the pre-application Environmental Impact Assessment scoping process.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation launches
milestones.description: RWE launched the formal statutory (s42/s47/s48) consultation on the Proposed Development, publishing a Preliminary Environmental Information Report (PEIR), Statement of Community Consultation and supporting plans for an eight-week feedback period.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation closes
milestones.description: The statutory consultation period closed at 11:59pm. RWE will review responses, prepare a Consultation Report and finalise the DCO application documents.

milestones.title: Expected DCO application submission
milestones.description: RWE expects to submit the Development Consent Order application to the Planning Inspectorate in July 2027, triggering the 28-day acceptance review under the Planning Act 2008.

EN010165.name: Kingsway Solar Farm
EN010165.summary: Kingsway Solar Farm Limited is proposing a solar photovoltaic and battery storage facility with a generation capacity of up to 500MW, sited on around 1,246 hectares of farmland southeast of Cambridge between Burwell and Balsham. The project is a Nationally Significant Infrastructure Project at pre-application stage, with the Development Consent Order application expected to be submitted in April 2026.

milestones.title: Project announced to local communities
milestones.description: Kingsway Solar Farm Ltd presented the proposal to Balsham Parish Council, the first public announcement of the scheme to affected villages in south Cambridgeshire.

milestones.title: Non-statutory (stage one) consultation opens
milestones.description: First-stage non-statutory public consultation on the emerging proposals ran for just over five weeks between 31 October and 12 December 2024.

milestones.title: EIA Scoping Report submitted
milestones.description: Kingsway Solar Farm Limited submitted its Environmental Impact Assessment Scoping Report to the Planning Inspectorate, covering biodiversity, landscape, heritage, soils, air quality and noise.

milestones.title: Planning Inspectorate adopts Scoping Opinion
milestones.description: PINS issued its Scoping Opinion, requiring the Environmental Statement to address waste streams, potential effects on Devil's Dyke and Newmarket Heath SSSIs, and Carlton Lane Conservation Area, among other matters.

milestones.title: Statutory (section 47) consultation opens
milestones.description: The applicant launched its six-week statutory consultation on the Preliminary Environmental Information Report and draft proposals, running from 17 September to 29 October 2025.

milestones.title: Section 46 notification acknowledged by PINS
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate acknowledged receipt of the applicant's notification under section 46 of the Planning Act 2008 and Regulation 8 of the EIA Regulations 2017.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation closes
milestones.description: Statutory consultation closed after six weeks, with responses submitted by Cambridgeshire County Council, Suffolk County Council and Kingsway Solar Community Action among other stakeholders.

milestones.title: DCO application expected to be submitted
milestones.description: The applicant has indicated the Development Consent Order application is expected to be submitted to the Planning Inspectorate in April 2026, after which PINS has 28 days to decide whether to accept it for examination.

EN010171.name: Future Energy Llanwern Solar Project
EN010171.summary: Future Energy Llanwern Limited, funded by NextEnergy Capital, is proposing a ground-mounted solar farm of over 350 MW (up to 400 MW) on the Gwent Levels approximately 2.5km south of the former Llanwern Steelworks, between Whitson and Redwick in Newport and Monmouthshire. The Nationally Significant Infrastructure Project is at the pre-application stage, with statutory consultation completed in February 2026 and a Development Consent Order application expected in November 2026.

milestones.title: Project announced
milestones.description: Future Energy Llanwern Limited, backed by NextEnergy Capital, publicly proposed a ground-mounted solar farm of up to 400 MW on land south of Llanwern Steelworks on the Gwent Levels, sufficient to power around 108,000 homes.

milestones.title: EIA Scoping Report submitted to PINS
milestones.description: The applicant submitted an Environmental Impact Assessment Scoping Report for a solar PV scheme of greater than 350 MW capacity, defining the proposed scope of environmental assessment.

milestones.title: PINS Scoping Opinion issued
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate issued its formal Scoping Opinion under the 2017 EIA Regulations, setting out the matters to be addressed in the Environmental Statement.

milestones.title: Section 46 notification to PINS
milestones.description: The applicant served notice on the Planning Inspectorate of its proposed application under Section 46 of the Planning Act 2008, formalising intent to consult statutory bodies.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: Future Energy Llanwern launched its statutory (Section 47) consultation, including public exhibitions and a feedback portal, on proposed design, environmental mitigation, and community benefits (£10 million over 40 years and free EV charging for nearby residents).

milestones.title: Statutory consultation closes
milestones.description: The statutory consultation period ended on 15 February 2026. Gwent Wildlife Trust gathered around 6,000 signatures opposing major Gwent Levels developments, and Newport East MS John Griffiths raised concerns in the Senedd about cumulative solar development on the Levels.

milestones.title: DCO application expected
milestones.description: Future Energy Llanwern Limited expects to submit its Development Consent Order application to the Planning Inspectorate in November 2026, following review of consultation feedback.

EN0110008.name: Theddlethorpe Flexible Generation Project
EN0110008.summary: Statera Energy's Theddlethorpe Flexible Generation Ltd is proposing up to 1.5GW of flexible electricity generation on land adjacent to the former Theddlethorpe Gas Terminal near Mablethorpe, Lincolnshire. The scheme is expected to comprise combined cycle gas turbines or reciprocating gas engines, capable of running on natural gas or hydrogen, with associated battery storage and hydrogen production. The project is at pre-application stage, with a Development Consent Order application expected in May 2027.

milestones.title: Scoping report submitted
milestones.description: Theddlethorpe Flexible Generation Ltd submitted its Environmental Impact Assessment Scoping Report (main text, appendices A-D and appendix E) to the Secretary of State.

milestones.title: Scoping Opinion adopted
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate, on behalf of the Secretary of State, adopted and published the Scoping Opinion for the project, setting out the required scope of the Environmental Statement.

milestones.title: Engagement on Statement of Community Consultation begins
milestones.description: The applicant began meetings with local authorities (East Lindsey District Council and Lincolnshire County Council) to inform the Statement of Community Consultation, scheduled to run through Q4 2025 to Q2 2026.

milestones.title: Statutory (formal) consultation begins
milestones.description: Statera Energy plans to launch the statutory pre-application consultation under section 47 of the Planning Act 2008 in Q2 2026, running through to around Q4 2027.

milestones.title: DCO application submission expected
milestones.description: The applicant expects to submit its application for a Development Consent Order to the Planning Inspectorate in May 2027.

EN0110009.name: Walpole Flexible Generation Project
EN0110009.summary: Walpole Flexible Generation Limited (a Statera Energy company) is preparing a Development Consent Order application for up to 2GW of flexible thermal generation with co-located battery energy storage at Land near Walpole Marsh, Wisbech, Norfolk. The applicant has indicated reciprocating-engine technology initially fuelled by natural gas with a stated roadmap to transition to hydrogen. No statutory consultation or scoping notification has yet been published on the PINS portal; PINS indicates a March 2028 anticipated DCO application date.

milestones.title: Project website launched by Statera Energy
milestones.description: Walpole Flexible Generation Limited (subsidiary of Statera Energy) established a project website indicating early-stage development of a hydrogen-capable flexible generation facility on land at Walpole, Norfolk.

milestones.title: Project entered on Planning Inspectorate register
milestones.description: Project listed on the Planning Inspectorate's National Infrastructure Consenting portal as EN0110009 at the pre-application stage, with a project description of up to 2GW flexible thermal generation with associated battery energy storage.

milestones.title: Project location confirmed at Walpole Marsh
milestones.description: PINS portal records the project site as Land near Walpole Marsh, Wisbech PE14 7JL, in the Borough of King's Lynn and West Norfolk.

milestones.title: Anticipated DCO application submission
milestones.description: PINS portal records an anticipated application submission date of March 2028 for the Development Consent Order. The applicant's external materials had previously cited an earlier window (April-June 2027), indicating the timetable has slipped.

EN0110011.name: Ferrybridge Next Generation Power Station
EN0110011.summary: SSE Hydrogen Developments Limited is proposing a hydrogen-capable power station of up to 1.2 GW at the former Ferrybridge C coal-fired site in Knottingley, West Yorkshire, with associated infrastructure extending into North Yorkshire. The project, designed to run on natural gas, a hydrogen-gas blend, or 100% hydrogen as supply becomes available, is at the pre-application stage of the Nationally Significant Infrastructure Project process. Statutory consultation closed in October 2025; construction is scheduled to begin in 2028 with completion expected in the early 2030s.

milestones.title: EIA Scoping request submitted
milestones.description: SSE Hydrogen Developments Limited submitted an Environmental Impact Assessment Scoping Report, prepared by Arup, to the Planning Inspectorate, formally registering the project under reference EN0110011.

milestones.title: Programme Document v0 published
milestones.description: SSE Thermal published the initial project programme document setting out the proposed timetable for consultation and DCO submission.

milestones.title: Scoping Opinion issued by PINS
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate published its Scoping Opinion identifying the matters to be addressed in the Environmental Statement for the proposed power station.

milestones.title: Non-statutory public consultation opens
milestones.description: SSE opened an early Ferrybridge Hydrogen public consultation, running from 10 February to 10 March 2025, with information events to gather initial views from local communities.

milestones.title: Section 46 notification submitted
milestones.description: SSE submitted its Section 46 notification to the Planning Inspectorate, formally notifying it of the proposed application and accompanied by EIA Regulation 8 information.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: SSE opened statutory consultation under Section 47 of the Planning Act 2008, inviting the public, community groups, and statutory consultees to comment on proposals for the up to 1.2 GW hydrogen-capable power station on the former Ferrybridge C site.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation closes
milestones.description: Statutory consultation closed after an extension from the original 6 October deadline. Feedback will be summarised in a Consultation Report to accompany the Development Consent Order application.

milestones.title: DCO application submission expected
milestones.description: SSE expects to submit its Development Consent Order application to the Planning Inspectorate following consultation review and design refinement.

milestones.title: Construction expected to begin
milestones.description: Construction is scheduled to start in 2028, subject to development consent and a final investment decision, with completion targeted for the early 2030s.

EN020036.name: Grimsby to Walpole
EN020036.summary: National Grid Electricity Transmission's proposed c.140km 400kV overhead line and five new substations between Grimsby West (North East Lincolnshire) and Walpole (Norfolk), forming part of the Great Grid Upgrade and identified as HND-essential in the ESO Network Options Assessment to connect offshore wind, solar and interconnectors. The project remains in pre-application; DCO submission is anticipated in summer 2027 with construction targeted 2029-2033, and it faces opposition from local councils (now Reform UK-controlled in Lincolnshire), residents and the No Pylons campaign, which argue an integrated offshore grid is preferable.

milestones.title: ESO Holistic Network Design identifies need
milestones.description: The Electricity System Operator's Pathway to 2030 Holistic Network Design and Network Options Assessment identified a Grimsby-to-Walpole reinforcement as HND-essential to connect offshore wind and meet the UK's 2030 offshore wind target.

milestones.title: Stage 1 non-statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: NGET launched its first public consultation on early-stage proposals for a 140km 400kV overhead line and substations between Grimsby West and Walpole, running until 13 March 2024.

milestones.title: Stage 1 consultation closes amid opposition
milestones.description: The initial consultation closed having drawn significant opposition from Lincolnshire County Council, North East Lincolnshire Council, parish councils and the No Pylons campaign, which advocates an integrated offshore grid alternative.

milestones.title: EIA Scoping Report submitted
milestones.description: National Grid submitted a Scoping Report to the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, with the project classified as EIA Development.

milestones.title: PINS adopts Scoping Opinion
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate adopted and published its Scoping Opinion on behalf of the Secretary of State, drawing on responses from statutory consultees including Natural England and local authorities.

milestones.title: Reform UK takes Lincolnshire County Council
milestones.description: Reform UK won 44 of 70 seats on Lincolnshire County Council and pledged to use local powers to block the pylon route, citing landscape, farming and tourism impacts.

milestones.title: Stage 2 statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: NGET launched statutory consultation under the Planning Act 2008 on updated proposals, including 40m low-height pylons north of Barnoldby le Beck to Waithe to reduce effects on the Lincolnshire Wolds National Landscape, plus route changes around Hubbert's Bridge and Newton-in-the-Isle.

milestones.title: Stage 2 statutory consultation closes
milestones.description: Statutory consultation closed at 23:59 on 6 August 2025; North East Lincolnshire Council confirmed support for Lincolnshire County Council's intention to legally challenge the need case, arguing alternatives have not been adequately assessed.

milestones.title: DCO application targeted
milestones.description: NGET indicates it intends to submit the Development Consent Order application to the Planning Inspectorate in summer 2027, following further refinement of route and environmental assessment.

milestones.title: Anticipated construction start
milestones.description: If consent is granted, NGET's published timetable envisages construction between 2029 and 2033.

EN0210003.name: Eastern Green Link 3 and Eastern Green Link 4
EN0210003.summary: Eastern Green Link 3 and 4 are two paired 2GW HVDC subsea and underground cable projects developed by National Grid Electricity Transmission with SSEN Transmission (EGL3) and SP Energy Networks (EGL4). EGL3 runs around 680km from Peterhead/Longside in Aberdeenshire to Walpole in Norfolk via landfall at Anderby Creek, Lincolnshire; EGL4 runs around 530km from Westfield in Fife to Walpole via landfall at Anderby Creek. The English onshore works are being progressed jointly through a single NSIP at the Planning Inspectorate, with the application expected in August 2026 and target energisation in August 2034.

milestones.title: Identified in NESO Holistic Network Design
milestones.description: EGL3 and EGL4 identified as part of the ESO (now NESO) Holistic Network Design for connecting offshore wind, later incorporated into Ofgem's Accelerated Strategic Transmission Investment framework.

milestones.title: Stage 1 non-statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: First non-statutory consultation on the English onshore elements of EGL3 and EGL4 opened, presenting preferred strategic corridor options and landfall choices between Theddlethorpe and Anderby Creek.

milestones.title: EGL4 Scottish onshore consultation
milestones.description: SP Energy Networks opened the first round of public consultation on the Scottish onshore scheme, including a proposed converter station at Westfield, Fife and cable corridor to landfall at Kinghorn.

milestones.title: Stage 1 consultation closes
milestones.description: Non-statutory consultation on English onshore corridor options for EGL3 and EGL4 closed after 12 weeks, with feedback used to develop a preferred route ahead of statutory consultation.

milestones.title: Section 46 notification to Planning Inspectorate
milestones.description: PINS acknowledged the Section 46 notification for EGL3 and EGL4, confirming the project's progression through the NSIP pre-application stage under the Planning Act 2008.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: Statutory consultation on the English onshore proposals opened alongside publication of the Preliminary Environmental Information Report (PEIR), covering converter stations near Walpole and cable corridor to Anderby Creek.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation closes
milestones.description: Six-week statutory consultation on EGL3 and EGL4 closed, with responses to inform the application for development consent to be submitted in 2026.

milestones.title: Siemens Energy named preferred bidder for EGL4 converter stations
milestones.description: National Grid Electricity Transmission and SP Energy Networks selected Siemens Energy as preferred bidder to deliver the two HVDC converter stations for the EGL4 link.

milestones.title: Hitachi Energy named preferred bidder for EGL3 converter stations
milestones.description: National Grid Electricity Transmission and SSEN Transmission selected Hitachi Energy as preferred bidder for the HVDC converter stations on the £3.7bn EGL3 link.

milestones.title: Ofgem approves early construction funding and revised timetables
milestones.description: Ofgem agreed material scope changes, early construction funding (8% of forecast cost for EGL3 and 13% for EGL4), and revised target delivery dates of August 2034 for both projects, with the redesign avoiding The Wash and reducing onshore overhead line.

milestones.title: DCO application expected
milestones.description: National Grid Electricity Transmission expects to submit the Development Consent Order application for the English onshore elements of EGL3 and EGL4 to the Planning Inspectorate.

milestones.title: Target energisation
milestones.description: Ofgem-set target delivery date for both EGL3 and EGL4, providing combined 4GW of HVDC capacity between Scotland and England subject to consent and construction.

EN0210004.name: Hinkley Point C Connection Project Material Change
EN0210004.summary: National Grid Electricity Transmission is seeking a Material Change to the National Grid (Hinkley Point C Connection Project) Order 2016, which authorised the 57 km, 400 kV connection from Bridgwater to Seabank substation near Avonmouth, including 37 km of new T-pylons and 8.5 km of underground cabling beneath the Mendip Hills. The Material Change application would amend the authorised works, powers and land rights in the original DCO to provide permanent access infrastructure for the long-term maintenance and operation of the connection. The project is currently in the pre-application stage, with submission expected in August 2026.

milestones.title: Original DCO granted
milestones.description: Secretary of State for Energy and Climate Change made the National Grid (Hinkley Point C Connection Project) Order 2016, consenting the 400 kV connection from Bridgwater to Seabank, including approximately 37 km of T-pylon overhead line, 10 km of lattice pylons, 8.5 km of underground cable through the Mendip Hills AONB, and removal of around 67 km of existing 132 kV lines.

milestones.title: All 116 T-pylons installed
milestones.description: Balfour Beatty completed installation of all 116 T-pylons along the 57 km route, the first operational use of the T-pylon design in the UK, providing context for the subsequent need for long-term access arrangements.

milestones.title: T-pylons fully wired
milestones.description: All T-pylons on the Hinkley Connection were fully wired, with the connection moving toward energisation and the temporary lattice pylons due for removal.

milestones.title: EIA Scoping Report submitted (MC1)
milestones.description: National Grid Electricity Transmission submitted its Environmental Impact Assessment Scoping Report to the Planning Inspectorate, setting out the proposed scope of environmental assessment for the Material Change.

milestones.title: Scoping Opinion issued
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate issued its EIA Scoping Opinion for the Material Change application.

milestones.title: Application submission expected
milestones.description: National Grid Electricity Transmission expects to submit the Material Change application to the Planning Inspectorate in August 2026, seeking amendments to the authorised works, powers and land rights in the 2016 DCO to secure permanent access infrastructure for maintenance and operation.

milestones.title: Examination expected to begin
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate's examination of the Material Change application is expected to run for up to six months, considering amendments to authorised works, powers and land rights under the 2016 DCO.

milestones.title: Secretary of State decision expected
milestones.description: Following the Examining Authority's recommendation report, the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero is expected to issue a decision on the Material Change.

EN0210008.name: East Irish Sea Transmission Project
EN0210008.summary: Orsted East Irish Sea Transmission Limited is seeking development consent for onshore and inshore transmission infrastructure to bring power from the proposed 1.4 GW Mooir Vannin Offshore Wind Farm, located in Isle of Man territorial seas, to the National Grid at Penwortham, Lancashire. The project will make landfall at Fleetwood and route underground cables through Wyre, Fylde and South Ribble. The project is at the pre-application stage, with statutory consultation planned for autumn 2026 and a Development Consent Order application targeted for late 2027.

milestones.title: Section 35 direction granted
milestones.description: UK Government issued a Section 35 direction designating the onshore transmission infrastructure as a Nationally Significant Infrastructure Project, enabling a DCO route to consent for the connection to Mooir Vannin.

milestones.title: Scoping Report submitted
milestones.description: Orsted East Irish Sea Transmission Limited submitted its EIA Scoping Report to the Planning Inspectorate, setting out the proposed assessment approach for the cable corridor, landfall and substation works.

milestones.title: Early community information events
milestones.description: Four non-statutory drop-in events held across Southport, Fleetwood, Hightown and Hambleton between 8 and 11 September, attended by over 550 residents to discuss the cable route search area.

milestones.title: Scoping Opinion adopted
milestones.description: Secretary of State adopted the Scoping Opinion for the project, setting out the matters to be addressed in the Environmental Statement.

milestones.title: Preferred cable route confirmed
milestones.description: Orsted confirmed a northern cable route making landfall at Fleetwood and running underground through Wyre, Fylde and South Ribble to a grid connection at Penwortham. The project will use an offshore booster station, removing the previously proposed onshore booster station.

milestones.title: Site investigation works at Fleetwood landfall
milestones.description: Geotechnical and geophysical works began near the proposed landfall at Fleetwood, including boreholes, cone penetration testing, geophysical surveys and monitoring wells.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation expected
milestones.description: Statutory section 42 and section 47 consultation on detailed proposals and Preliminary Environmental Information Report scheduled for autumn 2026.

milestones.title: DCO application submission
milestones.description: Orsted targets submission of the Development Consent Order application to the Planning Inspectorate in late 2027.

EN0210009.name: Tarchon Interconnector Project
EN0210009.summary: Tarchon is a proposed 1.4 GW high voltage direct current (HVDC) electricity interconnector between Essex, England and Niederlangen, Lower Saxony, Germany, comprising approximately 750 km of subsea and onshore cabling with converter stations at each end. The project is being developed by Tarchon Energy Limited, owned by Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners (CIP), and is currently at the Pre-application stage in the UK Nationally Significant Infrastructure Project (NSIP) process, with a Development Consent Order (DCO) application expected in 2027.

milestones.title: Ofgem grants cap and floor regime in principle
