milestones.description: Ofgem confirmed its Initial Project Assessment decision under the Window 3 cap and floor regime for electricity interconnectors, supporting Tarchon to proceed alongside four other projects targeted for operation by 2032 (with a backstop of end-2035).

milestones.title: Non-statutory public consultation launches
milestones.description: Tarchon Energy opened a non-statutory consultation on initial proposals, including the proposed Essex landfall south of Harwich on the Tendring peninsula and the converter station siting connecting to National Grid's East Anglia Connection Node (EACN) substation. The consultation ran until 7 April 2025.

milestones.title: Section 35 direction issued by Secretary of State
milestones.description: The Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero issued a direction under section 35 of the Planning Act 2008 confirming that the Tarchon Interconnector qualifies as a Nationally Significant Infrastructure Project, requiring a Development Consent Order (DCO).

milestones.title: EIA Scoping Report submission anticipated
milestones.description: Tarchon's Q4 2025 community newsletter stated that the Environmental Impact Assessment scoping process was underway and the Scoping Report would be submitted to the Planning Inspectorate in the following months, with a Scoping Opinion expected thereafter.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation expected
milestones.description: Tarchon has indicated that statutory (section 42/47/48) consultation under the Planning Act 2008 is scheduled for early 2026, with route, landfall and converter station options refined following non-statutory feedback.

milestones.title: DCO application submission expected
milestones.description: The applicant has signalled that the Development Consent Order application is targeted for submission to the Planning Inspectorate in November 2027.

milestones.title: Targeted commissioning under Ofgem Window 3
milestones.description: Subject to consents and final investment decision, Tarchon is targeting operation by 2032 in line with the Ofgem Window 3 cap and floor cohort, with a regulatory backstop of end-2035.

EN060006.name: Hynet North West Hydrogen Pipeline
EN060006.summary: Cadent Gas Limited's proposed ~125km underground hydrogen pipeline forming the transport spine of the HyNet North West Track-1 cluster, distributing low-carbon hydrogen from Stanlow production to industrial users across Cheshire, Merseyside and the wider North West, with blending points at Partington and Warburton. The project remains in pre-application; in November 2025 Cadent paused its planned DCO submission pending finalisation of the Hydrogen Transport Business Model, now expected in spring 2026, with submission rescheduled to December 2027.

milestones.title: HyNet selected as Track-1 CCUS cluster
milestones.description: UK Government confirmed HyNet North West and East Coast Cluster as the two Track-1 carbon capture, usage and storage clusters, establishing the policy basis for the hydrogen transport network.

milestones.title: First non-statutory consultation launched
milestones.description: Cadent opened initial public consultation on the proposed hydrogen pipeline route and concept design across Cheshire, Merseyside and Greater Manchester.

milestones.title: PINS Scoping Opinion issued
milestones.description: Planning Inspectorate issued its Scoping Opinion following Cadent's January 2022 EIA Scoping Report submission, setting the environmental assessment framework.

milestones.title: Stage Two statutory consultation closed
milestones.description: Cadent's autumn 2022 statutory consultation on a more detailed pipeline corridor closed, with 547 attendees recorded across in-person and online events.

milestones.title: Track-1 Phase 2 negotiation list confirms HyNet hydrogen offtake
milestones.description: On Energy Security Day, DESNZ named the eight Track-1 Phase 2 projects entering negotiations, including HyNet hydrogen production (Vertex/EET) that the pipeline is designed to serve.

milestones.title: Government publishes Hydrogen Transport and Storage Networks Pathway
milestones.description: DESNZ set out the policy pathway for hydrogen transport infrastructure, framing the future Hydrogen Transport Business Model that the HyNet pipeline would rely on.

milestones.title: Stage Three statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: Cadent launched its third and intended final pre-application statutory consultation on the 125km route, with eight in-person events and two online events.

milestones.title: Stage Three statutory consultation closes
milestones.description: Six-week statutory consultation on the detailed alignment, Hydrogen Above Ground Installations and Block Valve Installations concluded.

milestones.title: Targeted consultation on localised design changes
milestones.description: Cadent ran a four-week targeted consultation (closing 25 February 2025) on localised changes to the pipeline alignment and associated above-ground works following Stage Three feedback.

milestones.title: DCO submission paused pending Hydrogen Transport Business Model
milestones.description: Cadent paused the DCO process, citing uncertainty over Government timelines and subsidy allocation. Environmental surveys and routing assessments continue. Government confirmed the Hydrogen Transport Business Model will be finalised in spring 2026.

milestones.title: Anticipated DCO application submission
milestones.description: Planning Inspectorate currently lists expected application submission in December 2027, following resolution of the Hydrogen Transport Business Model and completion of front-end engineering design.

EN0710001.name: Peak Cluster CCS Pipeline
EN0710001.summary: Peak Cluster Limited is developing a c.200km onshore CO2 transport pipeline linking cement and lime works in Derbyshire, Staffordshire and Cheshire to a coastal connection on the Wirral, where captured CO2 will be exported offshore to Spirit Energy's Morecambe Net Zero (MNZ) store. The joint venture combines Tarmac, Breedon, Holcim and SigmaRoc/Buxton Lime with Progressive Energy, Sumitomo's Summit Energy Evolution and the National Wealth Fund. The project remains at the NSIP pre-application stage, with PINS issuing an EIA Scoping Opinion in February 2026 and DCO submission targeted for September 2027.

milestones.title: MNZ-Peak Cluster MoU signed
milestones.description: Peak Cluster signed a Memorandum of Understanding with Spirit Energy's Morecambe Net Zero (MNZ) cluster to provide offshore CO2 storage for captured emissions from the cement and lime producers.

milestones.title: MNZ-Peak Cluster partnership vision published
milestones.description: Spirit Energy and Peak Cluster set out a joint report on unlocking CCS investment, identifying the route to capture and store around 3-4 million tonnes of CO2 per year from cement and lime sites.

milestones.title: Phase 1 non-statutory consultation opens
milestones.description: Peak Cluster launched its first round of public consultation on the proposed pipeline corridor, running until 27 February 2025 with eight public events across the Wirral, Cheshire, Staffordshire and Derbyshire.

milestones.title: National Wealth Fund equity investment
milestones.description: The National Wealth Fund committed GBP 28.6m as part of a GBP 59.6m equity raise alongside Sumitomo's Summit Energy Evolution, Progressive Energy and the cement and lime producers (Tarmac, Breedon, Holcim, SigmaRoc) to fund FEED and consenting work to FID.

milestones.title: EIA Scoping Opinion issued by PINS
milestones.description: The Planning Inspectorate published its EIA Scoping Opinion on the proposed CO2 transport pipeline network connecting capture sites in Derbyshire, Staffordshire and Cheshire to a coastal facility on the Wirral peninsula.

milestones.title: Public opposition rally on the Wirral
milestones.description: Hundreds of Wirral residents protested against the proposed landfall and onshore route; a parliamentary petition against the Wirral section was also opened.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation expected
milestones.description: Peak Cluster expects to run formal Section 42/47 statutory consultation on the refined pipeline alignment and Preliminary Environmental Information Report ahead of DCO submission.

milestones.title: DCO application submission expected
milestones.description: Peak Cluster Limited has indicated it will submit its Development Consent Order application to the Planning Inspectorate in September 2027, with Final Investment Decision targeted as early as 2028.
